His stress levels are informing his actions, he won't be rational or kind because he's focusing on the problems and not the solutions. Could you organise a round the table sort of meeting in which you create an agenda from your perspective and invite him to contribute? Then sit down and try to thrash it out but in a positive, non-threatening way and show him your mettle  With the Bossess too, she needs to understand that you aren't trying to usurp anyone's position but you do want the best for the business and have ideas that you'd like to share.

Soo,I tried this deferentially,forcefully and pally but the thing is this . They need to do it and will not let anyone else in. They both have quit high powered jobs and will not let this lick them. They block ideas or input even on small scale. It did not used to be like that .In the build up phase we were a good team.
I have spoken to to the boss various times about how to attract more customers and he wants to do it by word of mouth ,select the clients he wants end of story. I am saving my comments now as it makes matters worse. Altered realities . A TV that I report broken is not broken I am categorically told ( I managed a mtce helpdesk) talked to like a child that says there is a ghost in the cupboard only to hear her 5 minutes later that she had reported the fault...So no ,no more unwanted input.
Your idea makes total sense but it is a place of shifting realities .

### caroline — 2014-03-18T01:31:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-116074>

you are in a power struggle that you can't win. When we had our restaurant there was always an employee who thought they knew better - I am not suggesting that is you BTW. There was a 19 yo girl who, with in the first hour told us we weren't cleaning the coffee machine properly \[she had no idea what we were doing\] and she also told me that at the end of her shift she would take all my recipes home ....and also her last job she had called the labour board.

I thought to myself - Honey - you aren't going to make it thru your whole shift!! My DH let her go at the end of the day and she cried buckets and didn't know what she had done......

Maybe there is another employee who is negatively impacting??

My feeling always was - I am the one who pays the bills and works 14 - 16 hours a day - so - my rules!
That is not to say we didn't appreciate fresh ideas and imput ... of course we did - needed it and wanted it. Everyone needs to work together as a team for a small business to be successful

Is the girlfriend jealous of you? you obviously have great imput ..... is she a slug?

### Lahelada — 2014-03-22T18:25:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-117179>

Well good day folks, a beautiful cold and sunny autumn day here. Long holiday weekend is not materiañising as I have been called in to work tomorrow but that is good as it includes a temporary promotion for a week and a juicy albeit temporary payrise .If this experiment is a success there will hopefully be other occasions. The cold is a birthday present as I get to run around in a skimpy T shirt and sort of CT all day. I know about thermal load but hey one takes what one can get. I am also eying an old bath tub that has been rediscovered outside which had been overgrown by ivy and invisible until now. It is in good nick so ...
My friend is coming end of this month and she will bring me me K2 and vit D3 to tide me over winter. I can get both here but the manufacturers insist on combining everything with other unwanted substances like calcium or worse in the case of DHEA they add procaine. The latter actually has an interesting history . I read up a bit on it and it was the wonder remedy of the sixties and supposedly used by the Kennedys. I can't make friends though with the idea of ingesting an anaesthetic that works on the basis of a sodium channel blocker on a daily basis.Still,for now I see no need for the DHEA or the procaine .
A wikipedia link to the product from the sixties .
[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gerovital\_H3](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gerovital_H3)

### Lahelada — 2014-04-01T16:55:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-119234>

I am feeling well, not fine just well. My stresshandling has improved so has my sleep.
The last week of managing the hotel has proven to me that I am back ,not as I used to be but different yet just as good. The teaching of Dr K accounts for a lot of that improvement. Thank you !
On the basis of my performance as a house manager I was offered a position as a hotel manager by one of our guests, small hotel but bijou. For various reasons I will not accept the offer but it was important for me to get it . I am still mulling it over but my gut says no as it would take me to another country where I have never wanted to live.
This probably does not make much sense to many but n =1 . I had my nose in here for a long time now. I freely admit that a lot of things I simply do not get yet. Maybe I never will . It does not mean I give up though . Now I have to concentrate on getting a life back I lost, an independent life that allows me to make choices financially speaking. So I will probably not be posting much here as I will focus my computer time else where. But who knows what tomorrow brings?

### sooperb — 2014-04-01T21:53:30+0300

### Lahelada — 2014-04-06T02:37:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-119996>

Hiya Caroline,

I am never far from here but will need to sort out my commercial head. Staying in this job fills me with dread because of the pay involved. Enough of that ,we chewed that over already.
So what is new :
a craving for mushrooms: Sorted , indulging it and waiting for CPC 9 to see what it cravings are signs of
A indulgence of grapes: The least said the better . Let´s just say that I am not short of resveratrol today. Not a carb to be repeated.
Ct in 60 degree non fluoridated water (rain water)in an iron bath tub sitting on earth outside for 20 minutes until the mosquitoes got to me!
Been rereading a lot of the early blogs and they make sense! Dr K must stop sneaking them in though becasue I swear they were not there before.

Generally I am feeling way more relaxed ,less wired and a lot more mental energy ,physically not so much
Recent diet additions ; mushrooms and red palm oil. Turns out we have an oil palm growing in the park and it is just the end of fruiting season .If i can find a way to extract the oil I get very virgin red palm oil and palm kernel oil for free . We shall see if I have the energy to follow up on that .

you would be very welcome.

A curiosity I do not really wish to open a new thread for. What is behind not getting a song out of your head. I remember someone citing somewhere that Dr K thinks it is not a good sign.

### Lahelada — 2014-04-07T11:43:04+0300

Good morning . Up early today but I did get 7 hours sleep so that is good. I do not feel refreshed though. I think I know what caused the not sleeping and that is a rare bout of err hmm input being unequal to output for lack of throughput...
I read up on the palm oil and supposedly it helps sleep rather than hinders it. I will refrain from using it at night for a couple of nights longer until it is my day off and retest it then . Using it in the mornings though as I do want to catch some of that special vit E.
I was mulling over a business idea last idea which may have some merit and almost no cash outlay only effort on my part. I will dedicate part of my day off to that and see how I go.
Other than that we are expecting the great flood today .I exaggerate as we have beenlflooded by a storm a year to date almost and the house is still not dry . I don't think I would have the energy for another cleaning session.
Wish me luck,first day of working with the boss again.

### Lahelada — 2014-04-10T21:08:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-120831>

Stressed much ? Well,not me or am I ?
It all started yesterday when I found myself without cash, that is not without money, but with no cash. I am one of those people who eschews credit cards . So off I go to get cash and can't ..
I have an appointment , am running late and am noticing my cortisol rising. But hey the situation resolved itself and I was happy .

Cortisol must be low now as I am even smiling and off I go to my appointment, only to find that I had a partial address and that after a longish walk. I like walking so why that should have mattered who knows. Anyhow the appointment got cancelled as I arrived late and rescheduled all very amicably, life´s misunderstandings haha right.

So I decide that now armed with cash I go to the bakers and get some supplies . The baker´s are out of bread as it is general strike tomorrow and all the people are buying things up like crazy. I join the paranoia ,traipse around all my favourite bakeries until I find one that still has it, joke about the general air of despair in the air and head home , of course with the thought that I should get some dinner. So off I head to the poultry store, supermarkets were bursting full so I thought a little store would be better.

It probably would have been less stressful if I had not got lost in an area which I normally advise people on how to get from A to B, not lost in the sense of not knowing where I am, but walking in the wrong direction for a good while. Ok, I finally arrive home, we have dinner and I decide I will watch TV which inevitably makes me fall asleep.

Well, I caught myself napping , get up to go to bed ,put a foot forward and there is no foot or at least it feels that way. I stumble only just manage to keep myself from falling and stomp my non existing foot so hard on the ground that it still hurts today . Can I get three consonants ,Bob ,the first one I want is a W and the last an F , I leave you to choose one that fits in the middle...

So this morning I got to thinking reading a post here seemingly unrelated about BH4. I had no idea what that is , googled it and landed on a site talking about detox. The detox is by the by, but it lead me to Serotonin and a hypothesis. If we lack serotonin we crave carbs. What if we crave Cortisol as a feelgood factor as stress is physically and morally superior to depressed ? The highly stressed executive who gets into his car drunk to destress is socially more acceptable than an obese depressed recluse. Physically maybe cortisol is filling a void where serotonin should kick in . Do we set ourselves up for unnecessary stress to not feel the void? Or is it DHEA, the natural antagonist to cortisol that is lacking . I was feeling really good recently so i need to backtrack what I could have done wrong to upset the apple cart.

Well, this morning I did 35 minutes of CT and 20 minutes of grounding. I like cold but this morning I started shivering badly from the get go . That means something ,if I remember correctly it points to excess cortisol too. I will read again though . I seem to be missing a few numbers in this painting by numbers game.

Other than that the country is on general strike. I am also not working ,the air is cleaner for the lack of buses and lorries and maybe just maybe it will rain ,replenishing my non fluoridated CT water .

### Lahelada — 2014-04-11T16:20:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-120983>

Good morning . Just got in from CT in rainwater. I managed to do 25 minutes and it felt a lot easier than yesterday so something was definitely off. Outside temperature 62 F wth water being 58 F. The tub is small but I got all the bits in that I want to shrink or tighten Did my sun salutation this morning too.
Shopping day for all the strange foods not locally available. By that I mean good liver pate instead of industrial ,same for sauerkraut not from the can but from the barrel, stuff like that. And oca if I get to the Bolivian market.
Shivering now.

### caroline — 2014-04-12T02:38:31+0300

### Lahelada — 2014-04-12T19:42:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-121146>

The shopping trip yesterday was a trip what with llamas in the street,eating a chicken pasty that defied the laws of liquid saturation point and wrestling with my non existent Aymara knowledge.

Some things however defy logic in any language. How can one reasonably be expected to stray moderately from the path of Paleo by eating a corn chicken pasty when the woman who makes themis a wizard at including so much sugar that it should really have been solid? Moreover who puts sugar in a pasty.
But still, I saw a llama...

I also managed to find Oca from the new harvest.It is an Andean highland tuber with a long maturation period of 220 days. To me that translates to electron denseness but I could be wrong .
Price was interesting. The stand was run by mother and daughter. Asking the price they responded in unison 20 pesos....30 pesos. I said I go with what the daughter says.
But still, I saw a llama...

You can find a lot of interesting things there, rock like things that are actually dried potato like tubers,strange fruit that comes dried and looks like it makes some sort of fruit water. Asking the vendors is almost always useless as they are very taciturn people and I had no notebook with me to note down the Aymara names.
But still, I saw a llama...

Attached a couple of pics of my treasures from the Bolivian market . They are in clockwise order pine mushrooms, orange chiles and Oca with some red limo chile in the middle.
The second pic is of a calamar fin which I have no idea what I will do with.
But still, I saw a llama...

There is no photo of the llama as they wanted money to have your photo taken with it .
But still, I saw the llama...

### Gracelind — 2014-04-13T03:26:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-121196>

What an interesting plate! And aren't llama's wonderful--who ever would have thought of that design for a creature? And yet they are so beautiful.

Llamas are certainly not native where I live but some people do have them. You can take a trek in the mountains with llama to carry your gear instead of burros or pack mules. There's one hillside I often pass, in the foothills, where an artist has placed two metal figures, using 50 gallon drums for bodies. Standing up on hind legs, they have grabbed right elbows for a do-si-do, one a pink pig and the other a black and white cow. On occasion the local llama has been out, eyeing that sculpture quizzically, skeptically. Sadly it's in a place along the mtn highway where I can't stop to take a picture. I'd love to record that expression on llama's face. So, my words must suffice . . . describing the local, rather puzzled llama.

Artist does have a sense of humor, though possibly dark. There is a giant red and white arrow, slung from some great bow high aloft, piercing the roof of his studio. I hope he isn't skewered inside.

Anyway, love your description of the market, down to the taciturn vendors.

### Lahelada — 2014-04-13T03:49:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-121199>

I love the idea of a puzzled looking llama. To me they always have an air of air of haughtyness about them just like camels. There is a story told in Islamic countries that teh camel looks so smug because it knows tthe hundredth name of god while mere twolegged mortals only know ninety nine.
I love that you have them in their natural setting . In Buenos Aires traffic they look pretty incongruous. Thank you for sharing that story.

Caroline , I am not sure about the skate fin. Seems a different animal .Calamar translates to squid I think . I always get confused between the squid and octopus thing.
I had too much fish today . I also bought "white salmon" and "stick fish" Brazilian flathead. For further info you will need to consult the dog who wolfed it down just like the doc recommends in the latest blog. Clever girl ! Not me ,the dog ...
So when you come we make it part of your itinerary.

### Lahelada — 2014-04-15T02:40:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-121541>

So I am experimenting with in season carbs just a little before we we head into the austral winter which is when I will go ketogenic again . The carbs afor this week are grapes and the so called oca. Grapes are going out tomorrow,in comes carrot hopefully unwashed from teh organic market.
I am basically exploring the new blog a bit. Things I don't chew too much ,things to eat raw. I have also been wondering if my gut biome is in the same latitude as me. I have been a bit of a globetrotter all my life.

I started experimenting yesterday with an organic almond. I suggested to myself that an almond would be a good thing to have, rich in everything etc .Then I popped it whole .No dice I was still wanting almonds after all that marketing . So teh next one I chewed barely to get to the taste and that seemed to register. Almond getting completed ! Reason for teh experiment was to figure out how to present the electron info for it to be receieved and almond suggested itself as it is on my list of foods that I don't mind eating but have no issues with them not being available ever again. I also tried a ricotta raw chocolate dessert yesterday and got an almighty sneeze out of that . I tested raw cocoa today on its own and no such sneeze. Ricotta now that will be interesting. I will buy the same Ricotta some time this week and see if the sneeze is reproduced.

Other than that I ate the usual stuff to include a bit of soil from a potato I was preparing for OH. Raw eggs next and that raw oyster is not looking so terrible now as it once did when I first came in here. If nothing else convinced me ( and a lot of things did) that I was making progress is teh sudden interest in fish ,mushrooms even and now even raw eggs do not face me. As far as the rock oyster is concerned I am still hiding behind teh idea that I have no tool to open it.( I know don't even say it delaying tactics) I might put some raw meat in between to soften the experience .

Pending action points : eat raw fish /rock oyster
repopulate gut biome by chewing less
Vitamin D3 supplementation
finally do something with teh aloe vera plants growing wild around my house
dozens

EMF: got worse since I started working of that i have no doubt. I am hoping that winter CT will allow me to recharge batteries.

So today was a good day . All day out in the open air ,mitigated by 20 wifis looking in and being on the 6th floor but something it was . Good food too. Wild seasonal mushrooms with oca and what seems to be grassfed beef.

You seem to give everything so much thought ...... I just kind of muddle along I think!

### Lahelada — 2014-04-19T16:07:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-122521>

Yesterday , in my search for more cocoa in the pantry I discovered this - carob. I tried it last night after dinner. I made it like a hot chocolate but I since learned that people here drink it with cold water. It grows in the North of Argentina. So tonight I will have it raw in line with feeding my gut new "info"
It might be coincidental but I slept 8 hours last night. Taste is not to my liking though, chocolate with undertones of liqorice. Investigating now .Here are preliminaries.

**CAROB**

"_Furthermore, other studies showed that polyphenols may increase fat oxidation and energy expenditure in humans (16) and in mice (17). Therefore, carob fiber may exert beneficial effects on postprandial lipid metabolism and substrate utilization potentially related to the secretion of gut hormones."_

[http://jn.nutrition.org/content/136/6/1533.fu](http://jn.nutrition.org/content/136/6/1533.fu)ll

[http://www.raysahelian.com/carob.html](http://www.raysahelian.com/carob.html)
[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/12165334](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/12165334) benzodiazepine   NO WONDER I slept well..Don't like that much.

What interests me though is the probiotics in Carob .Will post that separately as I need to go out.
End PART 1

### Lahelada — 2014-04-20T14:42:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-122667>

Happy Easter sunday to all celebrating and a happy Sunday to everyone else.
What a weird day yesterday. I started the day off reasonably well and mid morning all over sudden I started to feel stressed,fire spitting stressed,absolutely no reason for it. We were on our way to the boat. I could not calm myself and in the end resorted to leaning against a tree while waiting for the bus.That helped and the rest of the day passed fairly normally except for a lack of energy.Let´s see how today goes. We will go sailing so it should be as EMF free as can be expected what with universal cellphone coverage.At least no wifis.

I have noticed a separate issue,my gut is making gurgling,churning noises after eating recently. my bristol chart number is 4 ,even with this going on.
Hmm, something changing . For better or for worse?

I am still investigating the carob, more out of curiousity. I am thinking that it may be beneficial as a shortterm gut repopulation project but it is not something I am in love with. Generally it is part of teh project of getting my gut acquainted with new things. Raw animal stuff next week ...yeuch .Sorry but does not appeal. Are we sure that just eating salami will not do the trick on its own?

### Lahelada — 2014-04-20T18:27:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-122696>

The below was inspired by a post about hair as I was seeing in my mind´s eye the hairdresser my Gran dragged me to tut tutting the fine hair I had inherited from my father... Also as a response to a specific question but figured it is something I should hold fast in my journal as well.

Let´s go to that hairdresser. What do you see ? 20 somethings getting antsy in their chairs planning their lives. thirty somethings reading Cosmo ,wondering why their planning is going awry and 40 somethings complaining about the neighbour ,their husband their policeman,the council and the grotesque green colour of the wheelie bin all the same time ogling the behind of the hairdresser. The last group to me is the hormone imbalanced group that talks about niggles but does not go out to pint the green wheelie bin sky blue,paralysed into inaction old and set in their views. Victor Meldrew at his finest for Uk culture readers .
Cut across to older Buddhist monks ,nuns ,shamans monks in seclusion ,indigenous people away from modern civilisation as a stark contrast. Look at their faces. They have an almost childlike air/ curiousity in all things. Who is better placed to learn ? Mistakes do not actually matter but inactivity does.
Wondering instead of doing ,criticising without foundation or follow up action is procrastination.
Getting stuck does matter ,it is a sign of standstilll,mitochondria replaying the same tune. I asked here in my journal why it is so bad to have a song stuck in your head that you cannot shift .I was looking for a neurotransmitter blah blah longwinded explanation and the answer is extraordinarily simple. Not shifting from a song, not shifting from a behaviour, criticism of things beyond reach,being involved in other people´s lives that are out of reach is a sign of a static aging brain. That is why I write little recently as it is more important for me to understand the context. Don't think I am immune to this. OH has been sending up my Cortisol by playing first thing with the dog,winding her up stealing her things and then saying I don't know why she gets wound up like that..
I used to get wound up now I am retraining myself to see what is really happening. The dog adores OH, she plasters herself in front of the bedroom door so she does not miss a minute. What is really happening is that my cortisol levels are still wobbly so now instead of complaining about the noise and chaos I use it as my wellbeing indicator for the day .
Why is TV ,news so addictive ? Same reason, external stimuli to feel action while actually inactive.
And in the words of Forrest Gump a brilliant film character in my book: "And that´s all I gotta say about that .

And in the words of Forrest Gump a brilliant film character in my book: "And that´s all I gotta say about that .

### Lahelada — 2014-05-09T03:20:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-125970>

Been away for a bit . Trouble with notebook persist and on top of that the doc decided to bite a visitor to the landlords house who promptly reported her to tthe authorities . it is all sorted now ,it was really only a scratch but stillI need to pay for vets visits to the house to prove that she has not got rabies even though she is vaccinated.

But today finally it happened.I had the OYSTER. I managed to open it with a short knife I found in the back of teh drawer and when I saw the inside of it for the first time live I thought shame one cannot eat the shell because that looks purdy. The rest hmm... I gave the water to the dog who also thought for a minute before lapping it up. I figure that she could do with some electron balncing too after the biting event . So being eever cautious I liked the knife that had some bits on it and surprisingly it tasted like solidified sea water. However i decided to eat the whole thing I would need to sprinkle it with lemon juice. Wrong ,it spoiled the taste for me so I only managed half of it . the rest went to guess who . No not OH who at the sight of teh oyster inside ran the other way . The dog cme up with a look of interest so I gave her the other half . It was a hit and she now knows the shell so comes runing when I show her.

Wondering what one can do with the shells. I wonder if one could poach an egg in it as i cannot poach for toffeee and who knows one might catch something from the shell in the process?

been reading the Calcium channel blocker thread and the EMF protection it may give. I am not one to swallow meds though so looked for naturally occurring ones . Up pops Quercetin again which has other inputs as well so I have been reading what would be an ergonomic way of catching a boatload.

Feeling good all round but surprise myself with sleeping a lot on average 8 to 9 hours which is a marked increase from summertime when 6 to 7 was the norm. Interesting that the change of season should affect me . To me it feels like my circadian season rythm returning to more normal.

I wrote above that I was going talk about green beans and vinegar. As a kid that is all I wanted to have with a vienna sausage in the soup. I hated meat, not to speak of wish, chicken only the whitest meat .I did like sausage though. I was very fussy about textures and smells. What set me wondering though is that I gorged myself on tht soup . What was I looking for as it s a strange thing to crave . I researched the beans and cannot find anything of note but the maybe it was the vinegar ? An instinct if you will that I need to build my gut biome somehow. I might be reaching there but a strange taste for a 5 year old to be sure.

Gotta end here. I want to read some more while I have access to this notebook.

### Hope — 2014-05-09T04:29:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-125977>

love the sleep changes. congrats!! and love you're feeding the doggie some oysters. My friend's doggies, they LOVE any fish, sardines, cod, salmon, even the remains of some fish roe I got on sale. i was shocked. But down it goes. dogs are the best eh?

funnily I was the same with meat. I'd eat chicken rarely, fish never a problem.....but red meat? No way Jose, always hated it. Though I figured out for me, that was...digestive issues and adrenals too. When those are weak, the body has trouble with them and I instinctively didn't want the stuff that's hard to digest. Vinegar BTW can help some with ph etc in the stomach acid, which I am now certain has been low all my life.

Vocabulary lesson
I have the hump=my cortisol is high
My gut feeling tells me = thoughts are endocrine secretions
Needs a good seeing to= recognising the need for Oxy in others

German:

...is calcified = has Alzheimers

Interesting what language handed down knows and which we dont decipher any more. The Oxy one is not politically correct as it is usualy said to a female of a certain age but bearing in mind our hormonal chaos when we leave childbearing years it is actually kind of good advice.

### sooperb — 2014-05-20T14:56:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-127660>

When you have a busy brain, that's when you need meditation the most, it's also when it's the most difficult to achieve. I remember when I was learning, I would retreat to my bedroom and try to sit quietly. Inevitably one of the children would appear like magic and interrupt me. So I had a cunning plan, I'd meditate in my son's room because he'd never think of looking for me in there. There I was sitting in the dark, eyes closed trying to brake a whirring brain when I heard him coming upstairs, he passed his door, hooray, it's working. When he failed to find me in my room, against all the odds he opened his bedroom door, there was silence, the door closed and as I was thinking, ah he's learning, he ran down the stairs yelling "Daaad, mum's gone mad, she's sitting in the dark in my bedroom". Another failed session lol.

Interesting study but they admit they have no conclusive proof, "Dr Matthew Lam, from the charity, commented: "It's too early to suggest that sleeping in the dark will help prevent obesity". They have at least made the link to circadian rhythms, "One possible explanation is that the light is disrupting the body clock, which stems from our evolutionary past when we were active when it was light in the day and resting when it was dark at night."

Thanks Caroline!

Well,its getting technical here too,now.
I found this:

"d oined: 01 Apr 2011, 21:18 Posts: 3 Salicylate intolerance (SI) is a condition in which affected people develop a change in the way they metabolise certain fatty chemicals which are normally part of the membranes of the bodies cells. When aspirin/salicylates/ non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs such as ibuprofen are taken, they block the normal metabolism of the membrane lipids including arachidonic acid which is normally converted to a group of chemicals called prostaglandins. These are involved both in some normal physiological control systems and also in the generation of inflammation (which is why aspirin and NSAIDs reduce inflammation). In SI when metabolism of arachidonic acid is blocked by salicylates, the arachidonic acid gets converted to a group of chemicals called leukotrienes which are even more inflammatory than prostaglandins. Leukotrienes cause symoms such as asthma, urticarial nettle rash and intestinal colics and diarrhoea. In 2008 I and my colleagues published a report in the British Journal of Dermatology in which we showed that a group of patients with severe SI were cleared of their symptoms by taking high doses of fish oils rich in omega-3 fatty acids. The abstract is below: Abstract We report three patients with disabling salicylate-induced intolerance who experienced abrogation of symptoms following dietary supplementation with omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acids (PUFAs). All three patients experienced severe urticaria, asthma requiring systemic steroid therapy and anaphylactic reactions. After dietary supplementation with 10 g daily of fish oils rich in omega-3 PUFAs for 6-8 weeks all three experienced complete or virtually complete resolution of symptoms allowing discontinuation of systemic corticosteroid therapy. Symptoms relapsed after dose reduction. Fish oil appears a safe and effective treatment for this difficult and often serious condition. The rationale for the success of omega=3 fish oils is that the omega-3 fatty acids substitute for arachidonic acid and allow the formation of much less inflammatory types of leukotrienes. This effect takes at least a month, requires up to 10 capsules (10 grams) of omega-3oils, and continues to increase over 3-6 months. Such high levels of fish oils are generally safe but occasionally they can lead to nose bleeds and poor clotting of blood. I hope this is of some use in educating the correspondents to this page. Peter S Friedmann MD FRCP FMedSci, Emeritus Professor of Dermatology, University o

### Josh — 2014-05-31T16:52:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-129036>

[http://salicylate.me/2014/03/17/a-breakthrough-in-treatment-of-salicylate-intolerance/](http://salicylate.me/2014/03/17/a-breakthrough-in-treatment-of-salicylate-intolerance/)

> ****Why Do People React to Them?****
> In a normal body, one with the correct levels of sulfates and liver enzymes, phenols and salicylates are easily metabolized. The body utilizes what it needs from the chemicals and properly disposes of the rest through the bowels. In those whose levels are abnormal, or in the case of “leaky gut syndrome,” intolerance to this chemical family can occur rather quickly.
>
> Many people with gut issues, such as yeast/bacteria overgrowth or digestive diseases, can develop salicylate intolerance as a result of “leaky gut” syndrome. Leaky gut is a result of various digestive problems and occurs when the small intestine becomes too damaged to properly filter the size and types of food particles or chemicals that enter the bloodstream ([for more on leaky gut read this](http://scdlifestyle.com/2010/04/2010/03/26/the-scd-diet-and-leaky-gut-syndrome/)). When these improper particles are allowed to repeatedly enter the bloodstream, the body tries to get rid of them by triggering an immune system response. Because phenols/salicylates are so common in most foods, a person with a leaky gut will have much higher than normal levels of these chemicals in their blood and can very quickly develop intolerances to these specific particles.

[http://scdlifestyle.com/2010/04/phenols-and-salicylates-what-they-are-and-why-it-matters/](http://scdlifestyle.com/2010/04/phenols-and-salicylates-what-they-are-and-why-it-matters/)

> Salicylate Caution Foods in the autoimmune protocol: berries, apricot, avocado, blackberry, cherries, plum/ prune , green olives, endive, gherkin, radish, tangelo , tangerine, water chestnut, coconut,
> oil, olive oil, all dried fruits, honey, date, grape, guava, orange, pineapple.
>
> Angelone, Anne (2013-02-04). The Paleo Autoimmune Protocol: Foods to Include and Eliminate in The Paleo AIP (Kindle Locations 845-848). . Kindle Edition.

### Josh — 2014-05-31T17:20:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-129040>

Phenols=Salicylates=Quorom Sensing Changes and Dysregulation???

> **Influence of polyphenols on bacterial biofilm formation and quorum-sensing.**
> [Huber B](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed?term=Huber%20B%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=14713169)1, [Eberl L](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed?term=Eberl%20L%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=14713169), [Feucht W](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed?term=Feucht%20W%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=14713169), [Polster J](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed?term=Polster%20J%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=14713169).
> **[Author information](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/14713169#)**
>
> **Abstract**
> Many bacteria utilize sophisticated regulatory systems to ensure that some functions are only expressed when a particular population density has been reached. The term 'quorum-sensing' has been coined to describe this form of density-dependent gene regulation which relies on the production and perception of small signal molecules by bacterial cells. As in many pathogenic bacteria the production of virulence factors is quorum-sensing regulated, it has been suggested that this form of gene regulation allows the bacteria to remain invisible to the defence systems of the host until the population is sufficiently large to successfully establish the infection. Here we present first evidence that polyphenolic compounds can interfere with bacterial quorum-sensing. Since polyphenols are widely distributed in the plant kingdom, they may be important for promoting plant fitness.

> Manganese is another mineral involved in ammonia detoxification. Excess ammonia can deplete manganese stores. Classic signs of chronic manganese deficiency include low cholesterol, elevated alkaline phosphatase levels, and depressed T-cell-mediated immune function (due to thymus issues). Manganese also contributes to the synthesis of dopamine, a key neurotransmitter that helps to regulate mood. Accordingly, when higher levels of ammonia result from the CBS upregulation, manganese can be recruited to detoxify them, impacting dopamine levels. In addition, the pancreas requires manganese for insulin production. Because of the many functional areas impacted when these two minerals are recruited to rid the body of excess ammonia, **_I recommend that those with CBS mutations regularly monitor both molybdenum and manganese levels on an essential minerals test_**—in addition to monitoring both ammonia and taurine levels on a UAA test. If the test results show elevated taurine and ammonia, you and your practitioner can elect to raise the level of ammonia support.

### Josh — 2014-05-31T18:43:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-129049>

Salicylate issue=Glycination issue

Glyination=Sulphation...just got that myself, explains a lot ha ha

>  Glycination: Arginase Enzyme, Glycine, Gly Co-factors (Folic Acid, Manganese, B-2, B-
> 6/P-5-P)

> For most individuals glucuronidation is a supplementary detoxification pathway. It is a
> secondary, **_slower process than sulphation or glycination, but is important if the latter pathways
> are diminished or saturated._** Obese people seem to have an enhanced capacity to detoxify
> molecules that can use the glucuronidation pathway. However, damage to the capacity for
> oxidative phosphorylation, which takes place in the mitochondria, is likely to diminish the
> capacity for glucuronide conjugation.

> Glycination Pathway Salicylates and benzoate are detoxified primarily through glycination. Benzoate is present in many food substances and is widely used as a food preservative. Many other substances are detoxified as well via the glycine conjugation pathway. Patients suffering from xenobiotic overloads and environmental toxicity may not have sufficient amounts of glycine to cope with the amount of toxins they are carrying. People suffering from hepatitis, alcoholic liver disorders, carcinomas, chronic arthritis, hypothyroidism, toxemia of pregnancy, and excessive chemical exposure are commonly found to have a poorly functioning amino acid conjugation system. For example, using the benzoate clearance test (a measure of the rate at which the body detoxifies benzoate by conjugating it with glycine to form hippuric acid, which is excreted by the kidneys), the rate of clearance in those with liver disease is 50% of that in healthy adults Even in apparently normal adults, a wide variation exists in the activity of the glycine conjugation pathway. This is due not only to genetic variation, but also to the availability of glycine in the liver. Glycine, and the other amino acids used for conjugation, become deficient on a low-protein diet and when chronic exposure to toxins results in depletion.
> Toluene, the most popular industrial organic solvent, is converted by the liver into benzoate, which, like aspirin and other salicylates, must then be detoxified by conjugation with the amino acid glycine (glycination). Large doses of glycine and N-glycylglycine are used in treating aspirin overdose. Benzoate is present in many food substances and is widely used as a food preservative.
> Glycine is a commonly available amino acid, but the capacity to synthesize taurine may be limited by low activity of the enzyme cysteine-sulfinic acid decarboxylase. Damage can occur to this enzyme directly by pollutants, or by overload/over-use resulting in depletion.
> **_Both taurine- and glycine-dependent reactions require an alkaline pH: 7.8 to 8.0. Environmental medicine specialists may alkalinize over-acidic patients by administering sodium and potassium bicarbonate in order to facilitate these reactions._**

What have we learned alters PH?

### Gracelind — 2014-06-04T08:52:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-129631>

Hi Lahelada,

From the SNPs level, 23 and Me tests Alcohol Flush reaction.

_Genes vs. Environment

Sensitivity to alcohol— the alcohol flush reaction—depends almost entirely on a person's [genotype](https://www.23andme.com/you/journal/alcoholflush/overview/#Genotype) at two genes, [ALDH2](https://www.23andme.com/you/journal/alcoholflush/overview/#ALDH2) and [ADH1B](https://www.23andme.com/you/journal/alcoholflush/overview/#ADH1B). 23andMe currently reports your genotype at a [SNP](https://www.23andme.com/you/journal/alcoholflush/overview/#SNP) in ALDH2. It is possible that those with the AG or GG genotypes at the SNP are more sensitive to alcohol due to their genotype at ADH1B (which 23andMe does not report).

The [ALDH2](https://www.23andme.com/you/journal/alcoholflush/overview/#ALDH2) [gene](https://www.23andme.com/you/journal/alcoholflush/overview/#Gene) rs671 encodes a [protein](https://www.23andme.com/you/journal/alcoholflush/overview/#Protein) called aldehyde dehydrogenase. This protein is an [enzyme](https://www.23andme.com/you/journal/alcoholflush/overview/#Enzyme) responsible for the second step of [ethanol](https://www.23andme.com/you/journal/alcoholflush/overview/#Ethanol) processing: the conversion of the highly toxic [acetaldehyde](https://www.23andme.com/you/journal/alcoholflush/overview/#Acetaldehyde) to the harmless acetic acid (vinegar). The A version of the [SNP](https://www.23andme.com/you/journal/alcoholflush/overview/#SNP) causes the gene to [encode](https://www.23andme.com/you/journal/alcoholflush/overview/#Encode) an enzyme that is completely inactive and unable to convert acetaldehyde at all. Having two copies of this version results in an extremely unpleasant experience when drinking, as the ethanol is converted into acetaldehyde and is only removed from the body very slowly.

This process makes a person so sensitive to alcohol that it can be used to treat alcoholics. Alcoholics are often given a drug called disulfiram (trade name Antabuse). Disulfiram acts by blocking aldehyde dehydrogenase enzymes and causing a buildup of acetaldehyde, mimicking the naturally occurring slow version of ALDH2. Similarly, people taking Antabuse have unpleasant effects after drinking even small amounts of alcohol.

An ancient Chinese herbal remedy for alcohol abuse contains a compound called daidzin. Although it acts on aldehyde dehydrogenase, it appears to do so in a way that does not cause a buildup of acetaldehyde. Instead it may affect brain chemistry and the rewarding or pleasurable effect of drinking alcohol._

Can you tolerate iodine in natural form in seafood and sea veggies?

### Lahelada — 2014-06-15T23:38:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-130941>

Looks like pati is my fish with 9.41% n3 with a relation of n6 to n3 of 0.58 ,.I cant resolve how much of it is DHA but for a standby fish it will do.
Now the reason for the search. For good fish I need to go to teh Chinese quarter which is nice but at the wrong time it can cost 2 hours of teh day which i do not always have. Frozen fish in the supermarket is usually imported and expensive iwth the exception of our riverfish . I tried sabalo today which does not comeout bad in terms of n3 at 6.74 so it is a good second ,with a n6 to n3 relation of 0.89 .
I am also interested to see that the fish is naturally yellow fleshed . Could be good for something.
[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Luciopimelodus\_pati](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Luciopimelodus_pati)
[http://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Prochilodus\_lineatus](http://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Prochilodus_lineatus)
Looks like sabalo is overfished so wont give a helping hand to that which leaves pati.
long explanation probably not interesting to anyone but for me as a quickreference whne in doubt as I am still without notebook.

other tales
Doing well with sleep, food took aa nosedive for a couple of weeks for pecuniary reasons but back in teh saddle . I did find another obsession though kumquats which you eat with the peel ,rich in quercetin,hesperidin whatever that may be good for and pectin so in total it might be a good food to eat for trying to repopulate the gut. they are in season here,the neighbour has a tree full of them although i do buy mine from the greengrocer .quite addictive the flavour, very perfumed like bergamot.
other than that not much to tell . Doing my being outside bit in the coldest part of teh day with as little clothes as possible again which makes me feel great.

I found a n interesting summary of EMF sources how to check for them with inexpensive methods for anyone who wants to dabble. The conclusions the author draws are very traditional and possibly wrong,his dietary recommendations for sure are but he describes quite accurately sources,mitigators and things that serve as an antenna. For example my earthquake proof house in Mexico I was o proud of as it was ecologicaally cool becasue it was built from basalt rocks with steel reinforced columns probably surrounded me with EMF . When Doc K says details matter it is always good to know more details .

Ah, I also did a little test. I was talking earlier about salicylate and alcohol sensitivity and got it into my head that my sulphuration pathway was off and that molybdenum would help. Well,only source i could lay my hands on that was cheap was split peas and it did help . no headaches from cinnamon and no red cheeks. now split peas are great for a quick and dirty experiment but not really for a ketogenic winter diet . So now I eat a lot moreliver which seems o do teh trick too and has more advantages besides,one being price hehe.

Bored you enough . need to check out reasonable Pc liek things now as reading never mind typing on my i phone drives me potty,probably quite literally.

### Lahelada — 2014-06-21T16:46:35+0300

Back to basics. My gut is still displeased at my sudden intake of pectins. Did the albeit not very accurate bicarb test for HCL presence and nada even after an hour. Adding to that that even as a kid I was a vinegar addict and while normal gummi bears leave me cold the super acid ones ( covered in ascorbic acid powder) I want to eat by the boatload. Maybe my pathological chile addiction features into this too. I must have made inroads as I find some chiles I previously adored too hot now. Still,leaky gut seems to be an issue still so back to that RX with OSF series in mind as well of course.

### Lahelada — 2014-06-21T17:34:05+0300

Comparing the list of so called acid producing foods with foods high in salicylates there is a lot of common ground and all my cannot put down my spoon when eating foods are on both lists.

Another interesting factoid I came across yesterday. I would have sworn that my brain fog is of recent years but a documentary about Stonehenge reminded me of a trip there that was marked by the same excess fatigue symptoms.That was in my thirties. Interestingly it was also a time when I was fascinated by socalled power places and in particular Glastonbury. The search for a more optimal geomanetic field perhaps as it coincides with my highest exposure to EMF professionally speaking.Makes me wonder what attracted me to Amsterdam. As far as I know there are few people who go to a different country for rollerskating.
Enough of the memory lane stuff. Back to basics and out to buy fish.

### Gracelind — 2014-06-22T00:21:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-131682>

Re: your comment on Stonehenge, I have been there in 1995 with horrid jet lag. Then again in 2004 on a day with dramatic clouds and fresh winds--so invigorating on that day. Lots of natural ozone on that day, maybe?

In Bath the rain poured down on us so hard we just stood under our umbrellas and laughed. Later at Glastonbury, the late afternoon sun shone low through the rain clouds--maybe we lucked out with fresh, cleansing weather?

I do know that horrid fatigue and brain fog you describe, more common than not in my, life, too. May we all find solutions.

### Lahelada — 2014-06-26T18:50:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-132344>

Well, I am here. I could not find another place I could go in just a day. I should have clarified that this is my third and last day off. But that is ok. Plenty to do and possibly better as I am still nursing a cold .
Right off the bat, is getting a cold a sign of lowered redox? I am wondering as I am spending my time in max temps of 13 degrees or 55F. Last yr I was a bit more cautious and avoided the winter llergy. Dont misunderstand x.I dont mean that cold exposure means catching cold.
I wonder what made me less resistant. Actually I just gave mysepf the answer. My three weeks of carbs did that to me. Mystery solved.
SLEEP: apart from one night I have been sleeping well but in a different timezone. Sleep lasts from 11 or 12 to 6 am minimum or even 10 am,sometimes with a break at 6 am,if I am not working that is. I see a partial

### Lahelada — 2014-06-26T19:03:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-132346>

recovery of circadian rythm in the sense that my body knows it is winter and demands more sleep but not synched to night and day. Come to think of it it seems to be demanding those extra two hours that I go to bed too late tagged on to nighttime hours which currently end with sunrise at 7.50. Yes I know what I have to do. I just dont want to.
Taking a break now to watch the football. I am an opportunist fan with the virus having a 4year incubation period. more to telp later about tensegrity 2. Have you all discovered it yet?

### Inger — 2014-06-26T22:45:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-132358>

> [Lahelada said:](/goto/post?id=132346)
>
> Yes I know what I have to do. I just dont want to.

Why you do not want to....... Bad decisions..?

I think I am almost drunk - from 1 glass of wine...haha.. and way too late. It is 22:35 PM here. I had the last glass of wine from that expensive bottle, and bye bye wine. I thought it would help my stress.. and it does just the opposite... haha I should have known that? I still can lie to myself, and say... oh man girl you have such a hard time, have some fun! Do some "bad stuff". It will help. Loosen up. But don't you think I regret it every single time. Because the fun... became ugly. Ugly nights... ugly sleep..... no waking up in oxytocin rush...... harder to sense the beauty, harder to do good decisions.

Oh man. If we only could be so wise as to know, what do we need. Or... I actually know. But to do? That is the thing. The only game changer.

I have to say, I could be so much better. It is not about self pity or anything like that. I just see clearly, when I make bad decisions, it is no good - it is not what I need
sorry for rambling in your journal Lahelada. I just wanted to talk to you - and I am proud you are still here - I am proud of every single one that is still on this forum. Seriously.

### Lahelada — 2014-06-26T23:05:41+0300

Hi Inger,
You very eloquently expressed what my throwaway line of "but I dont want to" meant. I got here ,resisted,finally did almost all the necessary and have got better because of it . I am "fine" but not reliably so as when I start to be better I ease back in my pursuit of optimal. Guess that means I am not fine,just better. It is tempting to stay up late just because I can now whereas before I fell asleep woke up at 1am spending the rest of the night trying t fall asleep again.
So today will see me in bed by 11pm and by Sunday 10. Ask me about it.

Oh and never worry about musing in my diary, you are always welcome t d so.It breathes life into it.

### Lahelada — 2014-07-01T16:43:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-132876>

So giving the dog that much dha is not a good idea. She spent all day yesterday giving it back via the same route.  Poor doggie.

I was just reading in the thread maxing autophagy. The side topic being weightloss surgery and cutting the vagus neve in the process. Caroline was surprised that doctors would lend themselves to that. I think it has to do with religion,not the overt churhgoing kind butnthe values our society holds as a result of puritan/protestant ethics that are mainstream. If you eat too much it must be gluttony and if you cannot master your baser instincts then any measure that will is good enough considering the scenario. I dont think it is conscious but serves as a justjfication for such a major intervention in bodily function. Do no harm is offset by the supposed benefits you gain in being societally acceptable.
An anecdote aside but somewhat related. I once saw a doc with a very haughty attitude who could only prescribe me the pill just this once but that I needed to be aware of calories. Well I looked him over with the same disdain and in all innocence asked him whether he would prefer me to eat X or why bread combined with x or why spread rattling off the calories and what carb fat ptot ratio is actually preferable bearing in mind that I would need to get sufficient minerals and vits ? All finished off with a trusting look in the good doctors eyes. Response: Well you gotta eat less. My response: Honestly doc ? I really did not know. Obviously I was only doing all this reading for fun...
We never saw each other again .
Now that doc meant well but could not allow the idea in his mind that maybe his wisdom did not actually lead to results. To add insult to injury he was not exactly svelte.
Anyway just a meandering musing where dogma can lead.

Cold and foggy here for a week running now. I have not been doing trad CT but cold air almost 10 hours a day ranging from temps of 4 degrees centigrade for half an hour to 45 mins to around 6 hours in temps of 12 degrees.

### Lahelada — 2014-07-01T16:55:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-132878>

Well just realised that my math was off in the previous post . 6.5 hours in real cold only. Still it is something as I wear T shirt and summer weight jeans only. To be honest though I cannot face water Ct after this. Being cold all day long to some extent I do crave a hot shower before going out in the cold. If anyone has an opinion as to whether what I am doing is beneficial feel welcome to comment. Reason I do air Ct is th at our water is fluoridated.
Changed my BPC too. I usually get ground coffee but a quick read told me that freeze dried has much less caffeine which I think has been bothering my sleep.
Enough! The sun has come out so I need to be out too. Not working today so I can increase the grade of undress.

### nicld — 2014-07-01T17:00:56+0300

She was standing by the back of the van when I was loading stuff up just waiting to get in and looked so sad. I could not take her with as is was a warmer day and she would have had to sit in the van while I dropped the DD off at respite camp.

Lets just say that she has not touched her food in the past 2 days.

### Lahelada — 2014-07-02T02:58:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-132933>

So I spent all day in the cold either outside or in the nude by the open window. Temps from 4 to 9 degrees. Naked CT was 9 degrees for one hour . That should be like 53 F.

Ate 4 ozs salmon,no pork no oysters.other than that a football day. Both Germany and xargentjna have qualified for the quarterfinals. I joked with the cornerstore owner that if Germany meets Argentina I will not know who to give my loyalty. Wise answer "the winning team" It is all good. We are not really fans but it is interesting viewing. Growing up in Germany in a football family I remember ethics questions being raised when a team committed foul after foul and ther were few teams with that rep. The home team had tobe holier than thou to pass muster. The fouls I saw today were more reminiscent of rugby than football. Me thinks it is allpart of the slippery slope we are on.
BTw the US team was great today. It is obvious that they lack experience but great promise. Other than that I liked the Ghana team . They put on a great dance routine with their goal. Thinking about Caroline and another threadnI would have to say that the shorts fashion is no good. Too long...

Other than that not much new.They are selling shark at our local supermarket so this pms CT will be used to walk up there and get some. No reason for buying it other than never having tried it.

### Lahelada — 2014-07-13T03:05:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-134126>

Lol Caroline you rock.
But no, I do what is requested of me,think about OSF7 and generally mind my own business these days. Sometimes I fall back into my old ways but I pull myself back. So not worth it.
I am concerned about the boss though. He has an op coming up of which he only remembers that it starts with lymph and that it is nothing. As far as I can see no op starting with that has a good reason. He is constantly cold too which he never used to be. Sincerely wishing him that it all amounts to nothing.

But how about mey life? Well treading water at the moment but there are things a tad further down the road. A very EMF reduced weekend retreat,a possibility to do my own thing finally albeit part time to start.
CT is slightly reduced as it js warming up a bit but I still manage an hour in around 5 0 degrees. Absolutely no seafood this week until payday next week.lots of pork ,beef and respective fat though.
Grounding and sunning still happening although less when at work.

### Lahelada — 2014-07-14T03:26:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-134234>

Tired...
Too much sun,wine and laughter.
Sailed,CTed and enjoyed the clear aair thanks to the rain and the world cup.
Encountered a bird onthe way which we had never seen before. Hugged a coniferous tree that turned out to be not. It was an Australian pine,casuarina cantrememberwhat. Quite shocked though to find more celltowers than in the city. Still I spent at least 2 hours out of reach of Wifjs.
Oh yeah Germany did win the world cup. I missed the game except for the overtime. I think as the Argentjnes have lost I will pretend to be Swiss for a while. Just call me Heidi.

Oh yeah and remind me to tel, you about the photon /eating light /crocodiles/bill Haley see you later alligator natural conversation progression... tomorrow.....falling asleep where I sit.

### Hope — 2014-07-27T03:21:36+0300

I wanted Arg to win for all the individuals I know who were absolutely excited. Collectively though it is pyobably better that there is no euphoria that papers over the cracks. My personal favourite was Costa Rics as they did really well,had never got that far and had an amazing goalie. I did feel a bit of nostalgia too though thinking about past worldcups when most of my family was still alive. So who dif I

LOL. I cannot tell you how much I miss germany already after just a few days of being back....more than I can express in words. I know it's far from perfect there, like everywhere, and yet deep in my heart I long to be there. If for nothing else, the AIR there.....it is cleaner, not so humid....anyway. OH - and the stores and streets, when Germany was playing - was the same too....everything we leer gefegt. People were watching everywhere it seems...in little and big pubs, restaurants, outside, on the beach, in parks. So cool. The Brazil game we were at the beach and we could hear people cheering from across the street from our hotel room. it is a great sport and I'm so glad there is soccer...I have never gotten much into baseball and football. I'm hoping the US will catch soccer's fire with their German coach's help. Who knows?!

Oh - and I read that at least the German team had these cooling contraptions to wear in between games or half times etc, to keep from overheating as it was so hot there. Forget what they are called now but apparently they were quite helpful. Made me wish I could reach them and teach them all about CT etc.

I sure went crazy too about those Germans winning. And yes they were lucky, in a few games...in ways there were unlucky before, often....and I'm happy they won. But definitely - ARG could have taken it for sure and I sure felt bad for them...like also for Brazil though their fouling in some of the games - not nice and it's so sad what pressure those players are under too. Yes Costa Rica was great and I thought CH played well too...I am certain if Germany played Holland they would have lost. THough I thought the same about ARG - I never thought they'd make it. But I also think it would be nice for them all to win. They all work so hard etc....and I also am like, why do I care so much for Germany. Or others for another country. At the end of the day, we are people, who cares what country we're from. I mean in the beginning we didn't have all these countries etc.

Anyway. I love your idea with the dress. You describe it so perfectly!!!!!! I recently learned the story of Coco/Gabrielle Chanel...she was an orphan and had a tough life and somehow she created an empire. Some of her choices weren't the best either but it is amazing, what she made for herself. I can't wait for you to put up a pic of the model you will make!!

### Lahelada — 2014-07-27T12:34:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-135703>

Good morning @[caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/).
Too true about that red dress. I had been wondering how to make good o n that design. Money is short,the ffer of good fabric is limited. If it is not Modal that stretches three ways till Sunday it is hard to find. I had an idea though.I had a voluminous pair of red Indian trousers which I have already turned into a floor length skirt. It should have enough fabric for a dress. I will investigate in my next days off. Made me think of the wartime ladies who painted their legs with tea and drew a line up the back of their legs to simulate nylons. definitely crazy with our modern outlook. How can you think of such frivolous things when everything is crashing around you. I think we have lost something on the way.
Gotta run,out of time. @Hope:Will get back to you later.
I have more Feynman videos to watch while making breakfast for the hotel guests. A good giggle, a true showman,a great physicist.

### Lahelada — 2014-08-06T23:45:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-137007>

So this morning I made a bow and arrow,very crude not for any purpose except to give the dog some retrieval work. Why ? Because the recently pruned tree wood was there and reminded me of making them when I was a kid.
Other than that I made a start on the red dress. The once maxi skirt iznow a midi with tendency to mini skirt. It feels real flirty around the legs so the hemline might just move up a bit more. Ihave enough fabric left for the top either with sleeves or as a halterneck. However what I have in mind right now is see how it looks with a Carmen style neckline. It might be too voluminous but it is the quickest mock up . After that cuts will get more intricate.

### Lahelada — 2014-08-08T23:51:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-137319>

Hello Inger, nope the dress is not yet finished,partly because I cannot visualise the top part with an "thats it" certainty and the other part being that there is lots on my mind at the moment. I am branching out into language teaching and translation work so that takes a lot of leg work. I have resolved though that the dress will be ready by 9th Sept which is
Vit D day, the first day when the sun is high enough to produce vit D .@ Caroline, if you are reading it is also your vit d day!

I will say something about the general economic situation. While things were bad before a lot of people are now being pushed towards the edge. Gas (the cooking type) has gone up by 300% today and inflation is running at 30% and rising.
Things will be difficult in the months to come.
Anyhoo,moving swiftly on,biohacking eyes today. For today I have not encountered a change in vision due to being back jn the EMF soup. Lets see how the week goes on. I have taken some easy measurements as to how far away I can comfortably see. Let's see if that changes over the next few months.

### caroline — 2014-08-09T02:35:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-137336>

Good morning Lah..... interesting bio hacks!

the economy sounds dreadful ......xo

People must be in so much distress....

### Lahelada — 2014-08-09T02:48:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-137337>

Good morning,Caroline.
It was Neil B s post that got me started on this. I am blind as a bat or so I thought. Well,to give you a perspective if you got on the bus and I sat in the last row I would not recognize you. After 32 years of glasses I am ready for a change.

Re the economy the mood has definitely changed. But what can you do,the only way is through. Lol,bad rhyme.

So any plsns for Vit D day? Will the red shorts get an outing?

### caroline — 2014-08-09T02:53:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-137338>

I wear them all the time! they are a little short - so just on my balcony!

I have made it my mission to wear shorts and no underwear and sandals all winter!

so I am always walking around cool .....

One good thing ...when I meet friends at the beach for coffee ...they always give me the spot in the sun! win/win
they all have long pants and turtleneck sweaters on ... and jackets!

### Lahelada — 2014-08-10T02:39:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-137435>

Interesting observation for the week. I spent an hour every day this week in a small place in close proximity to a wifi router,a large smart tv an old laptop and a couple of cellphones . Results were the same every day ,really hot dacial flushes and wild hair duento satic. Refinitelynan unhealthy room . I might just get forgetful and unplug some things..
Visual observation:
Some muscle mvements are really hard which lends credence to myneye muscles being slack. I will have to pass on sarcasm as I cannot roll my eyes very well.
I also read somewhere that longterm glasses wearers have a tendency to view every detail at the same time while non lenswearera.s flick back and forth between details to make up the picture. As objects are smaller to a myopic person with glasses it is convenient to take it all in . I tried the theory and it seems to be correct as without glasses my eyeball moves a lot more.
Random observations: With glasses in sunny places the sun dazzles me and I need to avert my eyes,without I can look reasonably comfortably into the sun. Blue light must have the same effect. How many hours did I professionally spend in front of a VDU screen? Lol showing my age.
Time without glasses today: 3 hours

### Lahelada — 2014-08-10T02:52:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-137438>

Just a little calculation: 6 hours of Pc work over 17 years and 35 years of 3 hours daily Tv. Well thats just 65000 hours of blue light. That cant be that bad,can it ? ( I can sarcastically roll my eyes virually )

### Lahelada — 2014-08-10T02:58:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-137439>

So we just did an eye rolling contest and I just cannot complete the circle . OH was watching me and the movement to the left just stops.Freaky . I am obviously not a sarcastic person or I would have noticed sooner.
I also notice that when looking out of the corner of my left eye I have less peripheral vision than on the right.

Vision experiments:
It has been so long that I went without glasses at night that I forgot: All the lights from cars traffic lights and street lights turn into stars. Really purdy .
Found out I can roll my eyes. Not sure if I had an off day the other day or if the exercises have already born fruit.
No detectable improvement in vision.

I found a forum that solely deals with natural vision improvement. I have no jntention of spending that much time on it but I did find a good addtl. exercise.

### Lahelada — 2014-08-14T12:44:34+0300

Good morning all.
A cold morning and I can say from the heart that I am now officially fed up with cold especially cold feet. Do remind me of that when I complain about the heat next week. I hope though it means that it was a winter well lived and that it served its intended purpose.
The dress designed itself
yesterday . It will be V back and front. As you may recall the fabric came from
voluminous harem trousers. My idea had been to keep as many of the seams intact so as not to have to handsew too much. That in turn is giving me mechanical problems with the skirt riding up,the cleavage being too flappy etc. Seams are a bit like dogma ,they need to be undone to allow free movement.
LOL you heard jt here first the Dogma model from the House of Philosophy. Well it could work, true religion sold well.
Kidding,maybe..who knows. Should I know? I don't.
You guessed correctly. It is 6.45 am and there is no coffee in the house...lol.
Signing off to start the day !

### Inger — 2014-08-14T15:55:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-137826>

No coffee in the house? I still love my 1 cup/day of cold cardamom coffee that i sip on for many hours.....  I think it is a mental thing?

Do not forget to post a pic from the dress, ok

I ate a flower of my bush... tasted very good! They are one sort of Malven too, the Hibiskus... so you guessed right from the beginning

It has been about 16-17 degree C inside lately, I love that. Outside around 18 C at its warmest. Like that temp too! My feet get a little cold too as the floors are stone but not really much, in the night my feet always want to be outside the blanket lately, go figure! It is like they want to breath? My feet do not easily get cold anymore... i have walked so much barefoot lately. That said, it has not been cold here yet!
Do you have stone floors too, Lah? Are they grounded too, 1st floor? I still cannot believe how lucky i am to have grounded stone floors... this winter I am going to get so skinny!!! going to be barefoot on the stone floor all the time = grounded - i actually cannot wait for the winter to be honest

If you are in a high EMF area the hot weather might not be too fun..... at least it was not for me...

### Lahelada — 2014-08-15T15:01:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-137917>

Good morning Arrrgentina!
Good morning @[Inger](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1057/). Glad it was a hibiscus. They fascinated me from childhood. We have 2 in the garden. I should try them. In Mexico they dry the red ones and make a tea infusion which is very refreshing drunk with ice. It is called Jamaica.

Our floors here are ceramic tile over concrete in the kitchen and the rest is floating parquet floor over concrete. We also have a ceramic tile patio and a bit of grass. I do spend a lot of hours at work ungrounded though.
It will be interesting to see what the heat does to me. I hav noticed that when I work in my bosses apartment I flush as If xi have a sunburn ,same when out on the patio. The bosses place is full of gizmos,router,smart tv where they sleep...And the boss is suffering from arthritis and the bossesse from backache and insomnia. Lol,mood swings too but that is my perception only...
Anyhoo,I will have to do a lot more grounding especially as two more wifis are now looking in at home. Bedroom still only has 2 though.
I was intrigued by what the doc said,that he thinks that the AC to DC rectification has a lot to answer for. It may explain why this house feels good. I don't think we have anything that has a Dc converter incorporated. Having worked with cutting edge technology for so long they lost all appeal to me. Th most up to date thing we have is a" flat screen" TV from the 90s.

### Lahelada — 2014-08-16T15:37:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-138011>

Good morning. Lovely day here. Spent the first hour of the morning letting out pork fat.
There is a question that keeps buzzing through my mind that someone asked me. Bearing in mind the restrictions here would I consider moving again. I said no . Today I read in Ingers journal that she sees her life as becoming
more nomadic to avoid more EMF encroaching on her. I see it as a battle of staY.
y and fight that is mitigate but am also wondering if that is my comfort zone talking. I am no stranger to big moves,have immensely enjoyed them but now I would really like to stay put

### Inger — 2014-08-16T16:02:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-138012>

Good morning Lah
I cannot help to think about what might come... not only what is now... and that i need a clear brain and mind to have the wisdom to help my beloved ones the best.
I cannot see me coming anywhere close to optimal bathing in man made EMF 24/7. Of course it depends how much. But we still spend time at the computer.... or I am going to, even if as safely as it gets but it is still never good. I need to balance that out. A camper seems so optimal to me.... i can travel and visit family and friends... i can just go wherever i want... or stay, and it not costing a fortune. I actually found a very nice camper for 2500 €... oh man that was a real beauty! I am a bit obsessed with this thought now. The ex called and i told him about my dream... he does not seemed very enthusiastic about it yet... he is that kind of a man that would never sleep in a tent, but always choose a nice hotel... but i just need to get him here and work on him a bit... we will see
does not matter if it takes a while, i am happy here for now. I am just knowing it is going to get worse here too..... i for sure hope not. But this is the trend I see everywhere

If I was a rich girl, i would buy property somewhere remote, like Jack. That is ideal.... i can tell as a woman i am very tied to my home and my home-surroundings.. and it hurts so bad to leave what was your home. But i have to be strong and i need to just rise over my emotions in this regard... it is about survival. But it is also about, how can i help others when they need me. How can i combine those two, because alone isolated in the woods is nothing for me. what good would that do for the world. And i am highly social too.. But i am also a woman.. and suffer more from the crazyness in our world than a man would do... we need to take special care...

### Lahelada — 2014-08-16T16:10:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-138013>

We shall see. Italked to OH and he says in general yes to a warmer place where there is work and a different mjndset. Hmm,difficult...
The dress is still a brainchild waiting tbe born. It is sort of stuck in the birthcanal. I could not sleep last night and got to reading here and thinking. The reason I do not want to finish it is that the fabric is just enough for one attempt. If I get the cut wrong I will have to make do with a skirt. I am saying this here as I recognise how ridiculous a notion it is. Do you want a dress or not,girl? It does tell me though that my dopamine levels ain't all that which given that I am surrounded by lack of dopa is not surprising. So more threelegged stool basics for me.

I did get some sleep though although disjointed ,some 6 hours or so. While not sleeping I did read here,the jetlag blog which does not really concern me as I am not planning to go anywhere ...Wrong,lots of info in there that transcends the original topic. Good practical advice as to how to increase autophagy. Recommended for everyone. Last but not least the grand Maunder mininum we are maybe facing. I had come across it in my meteo forum where people are also wondering about it, professional meteorologists and hobby ones.
I am going to look into this more deeply as it may just affect a current plan of ours either affirming or nixing it,not sure yet which.
The most immediate thing is though finding another source of income fast. We have 40% inflation here and salaries do not keep up for now anyways. Discretionary spending has dried up which is what our business models were based on. There is a lifeline ahead but only starting December. There is a Mexican mariachi song that seems apt. Translated it means.
"You dont have to be the first,you just have to know how to get there. "
Another thing that hit me last night. This smartphone affects me a lot during the night and in the morning with my typing finger going numb. That does not happen in the afternoon. I will move my usage time to later as I am obviously unzipped.
Reason for my intermittent sleep? A foolish indulgence in too much coffee. I know how much I can take but as I had not had any in a week. I overindulged. Probably also to do with the dopamine thing.
Gotta run things to do.
