### Lahelada — 2016-01-31T13:32:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-187753>

Sunday morning musings inspired by a study @ LilRedDgirl posted about sleep.
I liked it by the way but wanted to point out the flaws of its conclusion.

So we've heard about the paleo diet. Now let's talk about paleo sleep. The idea is that all of our modern devices are hurting our ability to sleep. But did people really sleep any better without these devices? NPR's Nell Greenfieldboyce reports on what scientists found when they askedl\]\[/quote\]

Love the study.! It shows however our compartmentalized thinking. We elevate substances, tradition, whatever to be the non plus ultra solution for any given situation when real solutions should be multifaceted.

I think our scientists have grown accustomed to "niche" questions rather than big picture ones. Sleep for 7 hours is not bad, the programme glosses over the phenomenon of sleeping for four hours every night though, something quite common these days. Remember the Iron Lady ? Clumsy conclusions abound.

Things that affect sleep are many fold. Multitasking for one. Pore over texts for hours on end in enclosures (forget about the light for a moment) Visual overload everywhere we go. Want to cross a street? Listen for the sound of cars, look for traffic signs, watch for cars not respecting speed limit traffic lights, step on street, look down for potholes lurking, not too long though, careful not to spill the coffees one in each hand, anxiously checking that while you performed the surface check no rogue car has materialised. So one hurries across the street, two coffees alto in hand . Was that the sound of your cellphone ringing? Ah no, it was that guy's. You agonised over whether the bosses coffee you are carrying should be an alto, too as he had forgotten to tell you. Would one look stupid to ring him about it? Nah, a text is more innocuous. You went for that option . No hassle, good decision, boss was touched by your thoughtfulness. Thoughtfulness ? Was he implying that I had reacted like a woman,caring too much ? Maybe he thinks John is less of a time waster. When I get back to the office I will work my lunch but not tell him. That way my whole days work output looks more efficient than John's.
Screech ! WTF, where did that car come from? Stupid cow! Did you not see the hidden exit traffic sign ???
Phew ,close one,almost spilled the bosses coffee...

I wrote this tongue in cheek to reflect the micro decisions and multitasking we do every day. The changes between "I" "we" "one " are not bad writing style, but serve the purpose of reflecting the thought processes that make us materialise another person that is not there,or our other self we have a conversation with and second to make the text just as multilayered as our reality. Society keeps us in a constant state of doubt. We feel insecure about our existence. We create another self for counsel. Should you wear a mini at 35/40 ? What are you meant to do when a stranger invites you a coffee? Is that a new trick to drug you and rob you or a marketing ploy to sell you a new hoover ?? Dogma is a response. Dogma is when you have seen enough, my databank is full , this is what I know, ...bite me.... Now you are an even better target for marketing,predictable and docile in your frozen in time rebellion.
Here, sheeepy, sheeepy, got something for your self affirmation. You are alright just as you are.... . Here is a magazine that tells you how to trick out your bliss just a little bit more.... Don't look at the magazine on the other shelf though, where I say the opposite....

Going beyond that takes effort and the exclusion of external stimuli., hard to do when your attention is being called to micromanage artificial emergencies. Now add light stress /EmFstress to that and you get a fuller but still partial picture as to why our sleep /life is not satisfying anymore. Thank god we have Virtual reality to flee to....

### Lahelada — 2016-02-11T12:32:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-188760>

I am writing this on a new netbook. It id an Acer Aspire with Blulightshielding technology,the new thing Acer came out with middle of 2015. I ordered it with misgivings as the reviews said it was a slow machine but as it was the only machine with this technology in my price bracket I ordered it anyway.
Well,I have been testing it yesterday and I must be very non discerning as far as speed of operation/download is concerned as for me it works just fine.
Happy with my purchase.
Next post will be my update finally that has gone into cyberspace at least three times before.

### Lahelada — 2016-02-11T12:56:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-188761>

June 2015 - to date : weight gain ,inflammation increase ,stiff joints and left leg getting stiffer. Right ankle not useable for anything more than careful walking. Uneven ground -yikes . sleep fair to middling
Positives : more mental clarity ,more dopamine ,

Fast forward to Feb 2016 : It has been a week now that I have been using MB and nicotine gum . What follows are my musings that may or may not have to do with the MB.

First day : Took one drop noticed nothing. My bottle does not actually state dilution strength so I was careful.
Applied a drop to my foot at bedtime and a lot of it disappeared within 12 hours leaving a skyblue residue that took time to disappear. Decided that this did not tell me all that much .

Third day : when I learned that others took more I took 2 more drops just before bedtime.Within minutes I had a sensation that someone was milking my heart . It was only for 3 or 4 beats but still I did wonder at that point whether this had been a wise decision. Decided MB was for mornings only but continued with topical MB at night. I finally decided that it disturbed my sleep and dropped it .

Day 5 : Miracles of miracles I can run . MB dose is now 9 drops. My foot feels normal, I can roll it again instead of padding along. I feel less out of breath when walking uphill. This is my marker for the day. It tells me how effective my sleep has been the night before ,whether I am rehydrating ever so slightly.etc .
I forgot a bag in a supermarket locker and I ran back up hill to get it . That had not happened in a long time.

day 6: Boom back to earth with a bump. Sciatica is flaring up. I cannot get out of bed, can only ratchet myself up slowly and well things that dropped on the floor might as well be in another country. My Chinese doctor friend gives me somee Chinese herbal patches and I also use lidocaine and naproxen cream .

day 7 (yesterday ) I did not go to work yesterday . Could not really do much of anything. I could walk but any dynamic movement really was not on the cards. Continued MB . Saw my Chinese doctor friend who suffers from sciatica as well. She gives me the missing clue for something I had been mulling over. She told me that sciatica is dehydration . At night time I had more fluid movements again

Theory : MB only works as long as your hydration level is up to par. If you use it more than your existing water permits you hit a wall or even backslide. Easier put there will come a point where MB has nothing more to work with. It will either feel as if it does not do anything or bite you in the ass.

Today : Back sliding . Pain on sitting ,bending , crouching I will not even try.

Treatment continues adding a bucket of water a day . I also use 2mg nic gum one in the am and during the day as and when but have not felt the need to have more than three.

### Lahelada — 2016-02-11T12:57:41+0200

How increased dopamine levels are affecting me I will have to owe you. A teaser big environment changes are afoot...

### Lahelada — 2016-02-11T19:34:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-188785>

The environment part :
Some may recall that I am working in a place with three Wifi systems of its own and 60 wifis looking in. Recently two cellphone booster antennas have been put onto the roof and it lies in the new 4G corridor. Couldn't get it any worse if I tried. It never was a great job to start with and it never lived up to the original job description. So I am going cold turkey. The bosses will not let me go easily and it is not a fight I am looking forward to but it has to be done.
It means though that the safety net disappears by the end of the month and I will only have my freelance teaching. Breathes in deeply...

In addition we have to move end of May. That means a big chunk of money needed. Savings are almost nil. More breathing...
I had been debating to stick it out just a bit longer but I feel everytime I spend a considerable chunk of time there I am empty ,no juice left in the battery and that does not allow me to start anything new on a grander scale as my dopamine levels are just sufficient for what I am currently doing no extra for that bright spark.

I had an interview today for another job that took interesting turns. He recogniss that I am overqualified for the job he offers and would like to hear a business proposal for a language school he would be willing to back . I will get back to him on that for sure...

Wish me luck that my wings grow before I hit the ground.

### Lahelada — 2016-02-14T19:52:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-189027>

DONE ! It was surprisingly easy. I had thought that my resignation speech would be like Don Quijote fighting imaginary windmills but it was surprisingly well received. My concerns and issues were herad,I even felt a bit sorry for my boss as she let her guard down and let slip her issues with what is going on with her business. I agreed to stay on for next week with limited hours to give them time to readjust to the situation and get a temp in. More than that I cannot do. A very interesting experience though.
Well,now it is onwards and upwards .
Anybody need a cuatrilingual ,customer service professional something with a bit of a flair for writing who you can pay in dollars pesos or bitcoins ? Taking Roeland van Wijks book in part exchange for my services... Pm me with any proposals .

### Lahelada — 2016-02-14T20:02:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-189028>

On another note . I highly recommend this Acer Bluelight shielding technology.I went a bit wild as it has been soo long that I had I fully functional netbook, a tablet does not cut it for me. I have Easy Eyes on my tablet and I have experienced f lux and I must say that this inbuilt filter is the best I have seen .Being very short sighted unlike other filters it does not cut down the contrast. For example I watched a movie ( I said that I went wild...) and the colours are a bit reminiscent of a washed out Fuji colour photo ,which does not seem like a recommendation but it is. I could fall asleep after watching that movie whereas with the tablet I would have stayed awake for a long time. All things being equal I would recommend looking at the Acer range of products that have this facility. Not all do. My netbook is bottom of the range (acer ES 13) but it works fine .

### Lahelada — 2016-02-22T16:38:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-189571>

So we need to move by end of May and I started looking this weekend . Man o Man the requirements are staggering. Proof of income is understandable but that a family member has to put up the deeds of a property that must be in the capital is quite frankly an abuse.
I am looking outside the capital now and sounds all really nice ,less wifi less cell towers or at least they are more avoidable as they are the "crosscountry" high types and not the roof top antennas that you only see if you make friends with the doorkeeper and he invites you on the actual roof. That is how I found out about the ones on my previous workplace when the lady started to share her burdens of her workday. Anyway I digress. The newest challenge seems to be overhead/aerial electricity cables that pass 10 ft away from the houses.

### Lahelada — 2016-02-26T16:53:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-189824>

@[Queen of Elves](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/18728/)

You asked me in Theka's journal if I am the owner of my time
I had to think about this . I am and I am not .
I am in charge of my time in the sense that I do not have to answer to just one boss, but only to my individual students and myself. It is raining today so I could decide to stay home. I am free to choose at a moments notice_._ The difference to a job is that there is a one to many dependency . If one egg breaks I can still make a smaller omelette. With a 9-5 I cannot move until I have put a new job or a earnings network in place.
I am free to choose where I work. One of my students has morning classes with me and we moved those to the park . He digs the freak stuff I talk about. I can choose to be outside in my class prep time which I could not do when someebody else was running me. ( ragged )
Within this society I am not free to ditch everything though unless I live wild or as a religious recluse on alms. So I pick my battles as of now. For now my directive to myself is to earn as much as I can for my move to a new place.
Then step back and evolve the business and devolve the work to employees. Unbelievable but true I found a financial backer . You know what caught his attention. I was referring to brain plasticity as the deciding factor between success and lukewarm results when learning anything new. I pointed out the modern diversions as inhibitions and he shared that he sees a lot of people as away with the fairies in his daily life . He meant lack of concentration,focus . So who knows it might be a quite different language school than the ones currently. That is a bit down the road though as I am testing the water in the field with different teaching methods myself at the moment

### Lahelada — 2016-02-29T19:58:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-189991>

I just wanted to share an example of how your own perceptions can hold you back. I have been teaching English for quite a while now but I have never offered German thinking there is no demand. Well as my English classes barely support me I did offer German and the response is overwhelming. I made a back of the envelope business plan for the next three month and I have exceeded it in the first week.
Looking back : what would have been the problem with offering classes even if there are no takers in the past ? None, except my fixed ideas/lack of dopamine.

### Lahelada — 2016-03-10T12:09:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-190587>

Interesting wake up phenomenon today. I was getting ready to get up,eyes still closed ,the inside of my eyelids serving as projection screen and I started seeing colours , first purple and white swirling into shapes and dissolving again. Then more colours added themselves . The cockerel down the road started crowing and the film became technicolour and I saw a cartoon cockerel. So I decided to play with it and manifested other cartoons, just thinking cartoons not "The Flintstones" and a film started where the images were not my conscious decision.

Hope it means the pineal was activating more and not calcium efflux. I dream quite vividly but this was when I was already awake. It never happened to me before to have an internal Koyaanisquatsi like event.

I tried supplementing my 'battery' with a 9v last night-had such fantastic dreams I was exhausted-but full of energy at the same time-this morning. Strange combination.

Glad to hear the language classes are taking off! Dopamine FTW

### Lahelada — 2016-03-12T17:56:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-190715>

@[Starfish Prime](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/17940/) Thanks for sharing that. It makes me wonder if we do have glimpses of a collective consciousness ever so often. There was an unexpected geomagnetic storm on the 11th so maybe something was brewing.

I am still stopping and smelling the roses,marvelling at my sudden business success,my inner peace of wanting needing nothing. Nothing has changed yes materially I am better off but not that drastically to have made such an impact. So it is not that .
I put it down to this and it is also very much in keeping with Time 11.
I spend alot more time in the sun and away from confined ionising radiation. Why do I specify it like this? My Emf situation is nowhere near optimal. I need to move for normal social reasons as in the lease being up but also to find a better environment. Looking back I felt the worst,most confused when I was in my work kitchen for hours prepping food,cleaning. The ergonomically designed kitchen was a Faraday cage without escape. Imagine if you will a room without sunlight. The only window that existed looked out upon a gloomy dining room,so gloomy that the miserly bosses insisted that the 600 W chandelier must be on at all times when guests were present. The kitchen had alumininium everywhere,even on the walls, so the "wave bounce factor " was off the scale bearing in mind that the kitchen was equipped with every electronic gadget that had to be ready for use at any point. Now add to that a wifi system in the room above my head,two extras down the hall ,a dect phone and maybe 20 Wifis looking in from offices underneath and around me. Blue blue blue ,all the world is blue ....

Why am I wallowing in this ? Not wallowing but understanding. I still have some critical challenges ahead of me . Finding an apartment that is in a better EMF situation, that they will let me have without the traditional requisites. Those requisites being that someone needs to put up the bouse that they own so that you may rent the house that someone else owns . You need dopamine when you are looking for a needle in a haystack. One dopamine provider is knowing that I made the right decision FINALLY and that I can do so again much quicker this time.

Business is still booming. Thats good but it does not change my needs. I was up on the terrace sunning myself and I was thinking that the only thing I ask is this . A clear peaceful mind that needs well nothing. And this feeling of needing nothing made me think about the recent question about "inverse IQ relationships" in lo/hi UV areas. What if the IQ assessment is just an assessment of your productivity within your societies framework ? It used to be that your productivity needs to be stimulated ,now it is your neediness as well. Maybe the IQ tests work out so well in lo UV countries as the questions do not really relate to your IQ but your adaptation to societal dogma?
I have a higher IQ in English than in German, my native language. I also spent my productive life in English speaking countries.

Enough rambling . A bit more sun to be got.

### Lahelada — 2016-03-12T18:01:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-190716>

Lol,lol. I had just interrupted reading Time 11 to write the above ditty so as not to lose my train of thought and just now I read this.
On my dying life I had not read it before writing
"
Are we seeing through a positive lens or a negative lens today? Our perception determines what we think we see. When your dopamine is destroyed and not replaced you become an energy vampire. If you see life painted by the villian’s, paint over that picture and become the artist who chooses to hand out brushes that create hero’s. It seems everyone these days is defensive about their lives; the reason is simple……… _**Everyone has settled for something less than they deserve in some fashion because of changes to our environment**_. They perceive it, but can not admit it to themselves……..If an A is available, why settle for a B, C or D? This is how your light environment builds good or bad thoughts, decisions, and ideas."

### Lahelada — 2016-03-21T13:29:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-191247>

Amazing weekend.
In no particular order things that have been said to me
\- I have already got a website that I was going to use for the same thing as you. Have it ,use it , money we talk later if you can make it work. - A potential employer who saw more potential and turned me down for the job I applied for.
\- This is the office you can have for your school if it takes off. Money we talk when you found your feet. - same potential employer
\- If you ever get fed up living in the city come on out and build a house on my land. Plenty of space. - Someone I have not even met
\- If you want to do group classes use my offices. They are not used to their full capacity anyway. - A friend
\- Jack Kruse ? Influence of light and lack of it on disease? Interesting . Where can I find that film "Supercharged"

Humbling.

Reason for sharing: I would have been one to say that these things just don't happen ,that this higher dopamine state does not change the world around you...Clearly I was wrong.
This post is for anyone else who did ,does think the same.

I experienced such things happening in my life a few years ago, when I used a Russian method for getting off glasses. My eyes improved more than 1 diopter in 14 days, and everything around me worked suddenly well - I easily lost weight, found a great job, but I couldn't keep the results stable. With what I know today from the forum and the blog, it's clear, that it was all about raising dopamine and we are in the lucky situation to know how to accomplish that, so good luck furthermore

> \- I have already got a website that I was going to use for the same thing as you. Have it ,use it , money we talk later if you can make it work. - A potential employer who saw more potential and turned me down for the job I applied for.
> \- This is the office you can have for your school if it takes off. Money we talk when you found your feet. - same potential employer
> \- If you ever get fed up living in the city come on out and build a house on my land. Plenty of space. - Someone I have not even met
> \- If you want to do group classes use my offices. They are not used to their full capacity anyway. - A friend
> \- Jack Kruse ? Influence of light and lack of it on disease? Interesting . Where can I find that film "Supercharged" ?- Cardiologist/Electrophysiologist.
>
> Humbling.
>
> Reason for sharing: I would have been one to say that these things just don't happen ,that this higher dopamine state does not change the world around you...Clearly I was wrong.
> This post is for anyone else who did ,does think the same.

You attract what you put out. Great stuff. Not surprising at all. We are all magnetic. Your magnetism is rising along with dopamine. Everything coupled.

### caroline — 2016-03-23T00:59:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-191325>

> [Queen of Elves said:](/goto/post?id=191296)
>
> I experienced such things happening in my life a few years ago, when I used a Russian method for getting off glasses. My eyes improved more than 1 diopter in 14 days, and everything around me worked suddenly well - I easily lost weight, found a great job, but I couldn't keep the results stable. With what I know today from the forum and the blog, it's clear, that it was all about raising dopamine and we are in the lucky situation to know how to accomplish that, so good luck furthermore

can you tell us more about that method? very interesting when you add all of this in..............

### Queen of Elves — 2016-03-23T10:09:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-191339>

It is merchandised by "Dr." Norbekov, who is quite controversial and his title also.... The exercises are nothing fancy, the book is typical self-help-stuff, provoking and quite woo woo...
But the main thing is to deliberately create a feeling of joy while doing some exercises, which is called "octave". And interestingly he uses some exercises that have to be done in AM light, meditation/visualization and not to forget sexual energy  I never felt better in my whole life (psychically) than after doing a workshop with one of his trainers (female, russian, WOW)  Like I said, I unfortunately couldn't keep neither the improvement of my sight nor the feeling, but now I know why - **low dopamine again.**

### Lahelada — 2016-03-28T13:22:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-191639>

Not much to report. A funny story maybe .
I am generally feeling well but on Thursday I had my old problem back. I was claudicating again. My dopamine must have been high though as I took my shoes off in the middle of the city and walked barefoot to the chocolate shop where it was rumoured that there was 90% chocolate. I walked around 10 blocks in my bare feet , shoes dangling from my hand. The grounding stopped the claudicating and the different pavement materials I walked over gave me a nice pedicure never mind the giggles for doing something so "outrageous" Only one lady commented though saying that she had the same issue with her shoes and she would love to do the same. The rest just passed by , no one even looked at my feet. And tyes there was said chocolate. Very nice with wine I must say.

Oh and another thing about the dog this time . When I got her as a pup she had a black nose and with time it turned piglet pink . I checked and no one seemed to know why but also that it was no reason to worry. We were living 7 blocks from a cross country type electricity pylon then. Fast forward to today and the dog's nose is starting to become black again. We have lived in this house for four years and this only happened recently. One of the things she does is that she makes a beeline for my red xmas lights,lays down in front and plonks her head right under them....

### endless — 2016-04-01T08:36:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-191812>

Interesting observations about the MB....I'm planning to order some and it's good to hear some feedback.
I'm in the market for a laptop at some point too, so will definitely check out the Acer Bluelight!

### Lahelada — 2016-04-02T22:14:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-191917>

This is probably the hardest thing I have written so far here as I am sticking my head above the parapet and by far the craziest I have contemplated. Excuse the typos in advance as I am trying to create a stream of consciousness.

Well,today aged 51 it dawned on me that I would like to study medicine. any other day I would have probably poo poeed the idea but these are strange times for me with more serendipity I can shake a stick at and today it really makes sense and it would have made sense for the longest time looking back . I have just spent the better part of the morning walking through town liek a headless chicken piecing the evidence throughout my life together . Lack of dopamine held me back but I did not recognise it as such . I told myself that as a doctor you need to be an ace in math ( a straight A was a requirement in my day in germany to even be considered for studying medicine , a B put you on the never never waiting list so the thought never even entered my mind) However as life progressed I did learn that nothing is written in stone and that aptitudes change. Neuroplasticity does not stop unless you kill it.
Fast forward to today. As a few of you know I have made various attempts at starting my own business. To recap the ready made soup business for busy business people and now teaching languages. Teaching satisfies me to some extent but given half the chance to opine on all matters medical I am soo there.I dived into aromatherapy,herbalism as an adult doctored my self as an adolescent with my grans home remedy book etc . Always on teh fringe but never accepting responsibility for a decision.

So these days I am teaching a doctor and the emphasis is on medical German . I am surprised at how good his German already is, he captures the much longer German words really well but still I took it upon myself to write a compendium of terminology directed at his speciality. That in itself is not a surprise. I have always enjoyed taking a peek into different worlds. I found that I enjoyed this immensely to the point that I am reading and understanding way beyond the original scope to the point that I surprised him today with something that I had considered an " well how could I not understand that" but he found remarkable. I also noticed that he is not dumbing things down for me. Anyhow so i am reading this handbook with the thought in my head that this not beyond me at all , a hell of a lot of work but not beyond me. There is more beyond this too, a frustration of not being able to make more of a difference ,too.

So I am left with one big doubt , a WTF moment . Is it too late, am I kidding myself? I have read the Time series front to back and vice versa and the basic will be if I can hold out ,adjust my life to be able to keep dopamine constant this time around which is why i am looking for encouragement or discouragement as tha case may be. What I will do with it is then up to me.
Would you be so kind as to opine though on this sudden revelation as I am sitting here shaking my head at myself.Any and anybody's candid opinion would be appreciated.
Thanks all . This was actually shorter than I thought which kind of gives me hope too that this is not one of my unfinished projects or never to be started projects.

### Lahelada — 2016-04-03T03:12:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-191926>

Reading the requisites for university entry. 7 year career having to redo all my physics math and chem hence the extra year. Hospital rotation 24 hour shifts in the last two years age 56.....Possible ,no age limit but sustainable? A case of "Potential doc heal thyself first". Feeling stupid and discouraged and rebellious at the same time. Very sad,feel like crying.

### caroline — 2016-04-03T04:39:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-191937>

Have you thought of other medical options??? There is no question - to me anyway - that you can do whatever you want to.

You do not need anyone else's opinions or permission.

You are the one that can and will do all the hard work...... you definitely have the brain power and I do know how you have devoured all of Jack's stuff and really get it.

This has been a great journey of wonderment and learning and developing into the woman I see before me now.

All of us are on the same journey of self discovery! Haley just mentioned to me ...she never, ever understood why she took Chemistry in University. It never made sense until now!

Do what you gotta do Lah! all roads seem to lead to Rome for you!

You will be able to get enuf cheap wine to get you thru!

### Lahelada — 2016-04-03T06:07:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-191941>

Dear Caroline,thank you so much for those words and all the ones that have gone before. I will follow this up. There is other med schools around. This I quoted from the most wellknown one here. Not that any are easier on the body although the balance seems to be better though. UBA is free but costs more time ,any other costs money. The hospital rotation does not change in any of course. Funny,always boils down to the same thing.
Your word choice rings bells with me.I have not given myself permission to dream this big yet. Todays outburst was of frustration but gave birth to the dream. Now I need to nourish it.
You ask me if I would consider another career in the medical field. At the moment,no because it would be too easy to downscale at this stage . There is also a bit of ego involved. I would like to be able to talk when everybody else talks. Of course I could talk now but I feel the weight is missing verss years of training never mind clinical experience. If things are to change then they will change from the inside. I have so far mentioned Jacks work a few times and caught myself wanting to talk more about it. I am currently teaching three doctors but only one on this specialised level. The other one I mentioned photobiomodulation to and the stare was so blank,so disinterested,so dismissive. I think in this case a professorship would not have lent weight to my words. The lack of dopamine was so evident. Contrarily today in my role as interviewer we touched on why what is important ,what makes a doc special ,what causes a doc to be admired ( talking about another professor) and the answer was Innovation. How I held my tongue I do not know. This young doc has my highest admiration,he is an excellent doc and person and maybe a new friend. What stopped me is probably the thought that this is not what I am paid to do right now,not the reason I was asked there So if one in three docs showing mild interest is a good ratio then I am in for frustration but of a different kind. I talked this over with the other half and he was strangely supportive. His words were that it would be a square,dogmatic world I will be going into knowing a bit about what we study here.
My eyes just fell upon your signature. Very apt for me to read this condensed summary of what is important. Thanks for that ,too.

once a month isn't enuf me thinks!!!

I am sure you have - but just incase ..... there is a Doctor"s thread - about "should I become a Doc?" have you read it?

I need to give you an injection of self confidence! what's up with you????? you are a very bight woman .....have some faith in yourself!

I question how effective you would be as a Doctor ....I am not questioning your ability. The Docs here on the forum have so many frustrations and they have a hard time practising the medicine they want to ....to change the system.

I watched Jack in Orlando repeating the same simple principles that he taught us long ago over and over to Docs. It was really scary that I knew more than them! They thought I was a Doc for Pete's sake! and asking me for my business card!

You think you can change the system from the inside? I think it would be almost impossible - my opinion of course. Remember what part big Pharma plays in all that.....

I want to see you make a difference......

What about starting an optimal health blog - in German? and bringing together all the things you have learned from various sources? and getting sponsors?

### Linz — 2016-04-03T12:16:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-191953>

Lah you echoed my thoughts but I don't have an answer either. Young doctor here not given time (or place) to look up and see the sun!
The training blanks out anything related to nature, and regulation and IT systems stamp out freedom to think independently, especially early in their working lives. Takes a very long time to be senior enough to demand what you want. Add in imposition of a contract that can mean working all night for around $3/hour on the worst rota here...

I'm more and more thinking that 'weight' is being thrown around ever more desperately while stones are hurled from the outside. Had several strange encounters recently where those using their weight murmered some truths under their breath when I turned away. I don't want to think I have to whisper.
This morning I was reading through some alternatives when I opened your post and it wasn't just lack of weight/my ego that rejected them , it was my thoughts on preserving independence.
I think Mel is on the right track. Running a healing place disguised as an (educational) holiday might be the way.

### Lahelada — 2016-04-03T14:33:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-191959>

Linz, thanks for speaking up . The picture you paint is not pretty to say the least. So I would be the one to come in when everyone wants to be out . Hmm indeed food for thought. I was lackadaisical with my choice of words about ego. Independence of thought is highly important to me and yes independence of means ,too. The educational place seems like a good idea forward. I am already talking to my students what may lie behind their seeming inability to grasp basic concepts commit things even to short term memory. Add to that that there are places I flee from because the negativity is palpable.
I think I know what I have to do in the short term at least . What I had originally planned two months back . Become a shining example. Plenty of blue light there...The study business is not on the backburner, but I am making this a multitrack decision as per input above. ### Penny — 2016-04-03T23:00:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-191975>

Not to be negative here but I can't even go into a movie theatre without contemplating how crappy it is for my biology... you would be surrounded by reductionist thinking, never thinking beyond the pill and scalpel, and I think your redox would take a hit...

I have thought of how to "change" the system... and I think it's already changing - when polled, most people ask doctor google before their own physicians and many doctors spend a lot of time now "re-educating" their patients... a pharmacist student posted here how annoyed he was at having to dispense drugs that he knew would harm people (think \*reflect light\*) - he wasn't even sure how to bring up the question to his professors...

One of the problems is also, a doctor is so far in debt after medial school, he has to take jobs where he sees a lot of people and can't deviate from looking at a PDR because he has to pay down that debt...

The way to do it IMHO is to start a "cancer lifestyles" or some kind of thing like AA and start curing people right and left - do nothing but present the anti-inflammatory diet and circadean biology light "diet" , EMF mitigation and provide people with research letting them know what to do - otherwise, you would be a layman practicing medicine... something like AA - or start at Overeaters Anonymous and give the some simple things to try - it seems to me it has to be a groundswell grassroots type of thing - like the Life Extension Foundation - where every time they try to outlaw a supplement, 1 million people write to their congressman...

I think you could affect more people doing it that way without wrecking yourself from the wear and tear on your body not to mention the stress level when you can't tell people what they actually need to do to get well...

I think that would also hold for a young person going into medical school - think about it this way - Could you change the army? It is scary to think the power these pharmceutical companies have over parents and schools also dictate what you have to vaccinate your child with to attend... they buy the government - truly, it's almost like you have to fall off the grid anymore...

Anytime there is a family get-together, someone is always talking about their latest maladies - really I tried telling them but they look at me like a freakshow with 2 heads... imagine trying to do that with the medical profession?

My biochemist brother-in-law told me that the sodium-potassium pump was the most vetted process in biology... his brother - also a biochemist - said he wouldn't be surprised when I told him light controls everything -

The last time I went to my doctor and attempted to tell her - anything - she pulls out a small study from an anti-aging mag, where they had some success stemming dementia with the usual supplement supports - that same study is on the cover of the latest Life Extension magazine - what supplements do is muddy up the waters because it looks like they help, but no one steps back to ask basic questions like why is everyone so sick? It's all toxins and metals and microbes... no one is thinking about light -

What's really crazy is they are now using red light to treat tumors but I doubt any of those cancer patients are being told what light to avoid... from what I've heard, they don't even think diet matters... my uncle's oncologist told him to eat cereal in the morning... every blue moon though you find someone interested, tell them about this web site and hope for the best... like the guy at Lowes in lighting when I went there last week to score some incandescent light bulbs...

Well, sorry for the rant, but I can't relate to the world I see anymore so I just shake my head... and wish them all the best with their maladies...

### Lahelada — 2016-04-04T00:25:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-191977>

Penny, don't apologise . My outburst has sparked a really good conversation. Like you I doubt my durability both regarding the actual 24 hr rotation stints required. It is a young person's game alas and no matter how young I feel this is not it. I also doubt that I could drink from the STFU cup while studying. An interesting question though about what we are to do being neither fish nor meat ,not doc not disinterested public. Just mentioning Jack seems little. I went through all the vit D /uv link papers I have today,used the above for crossreferencing and some more learning. I still found holes in my knowledge and probably will for a good time to come. The rabbit hole never looked so big. Interspersed my study day with actual Uv at various stages of the day. I am glad that I got this off my chest . I will find my niche,give me time.

Edit :Walking I was wondering whether I have found my niche in my teaching.but am not looking at it the right way. Teaching is hard,most people are so distracted that their short term memory cannot hold a basic phrase to repeat after me. Scary,

Ps : Thank you for all the help with the book. A very kind soul has gifted it to me and it will hopefully wing its way over here.

### Sue-UK — 2016-04-06T14:34:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-192157>

I've got a qualification considered "whoo" in the UK, more subtle energy, altered states of consciousness etc, but it was what first introduced me to Becker, William Tiller and other interesting authors. For a time I taught meditation and dowsing, whilst taking other courses by distance learning. I would self study or had already studied at much greater depth for the "whoo" qualification, but they ticked a box ...... I have professional insurance for all of them, which gives me the freedom to introduce ideas, provided in the case of challenge, I can back them up with the science, or, if not conventionally accepted, explain the possible reasoning behind a philosophy. I had pretty well given up practice when I joined this site, because I was aware that my own energy levels weren't up to it, I was leaking energy like a sieve, I had lost my intuition, particularly being able to visualise concepts, and I was struggling to commit things to memory. I had also been thinking that I can't take anyone further than I am able to go myself, and I knew I was going backwards.

What is being taught through the blogs and webinars is life changing, and for me it has also become continued professional development training, particularly from the webinars. It is like I already have a (non medical) peg to hang my new - and far more important - JK coat on. Perhaps if you immerse yourself in the webinars as well as the blogs and get the coat ready ..... the peg choices will follow.

@[Penny](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/446/) Thanks for the excerpt. Something to cut my teeth on. I thrive on these things . I may not understand it all but it sticks with me. It is difficult to explain .Now actively I do not have that vocabulary :I learn differently than most people . I make word associations , pictures or diagramsan unfortunate necessity which means that I have to "translate " anything "sciencey" twice and then I get the diagrams. First of all in a language that sticks with me and lets me understand and then wait for the switch as you would for a second language to become automatice.

### Penny — 2016-04-06T23:02:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-192186>

I personally get almost nothing verbally, I think it's because I reread stuff many times before getting it - I do like pictures also -

but anyway, I ran into this "Guide to the Russian video on water" and it is a guide to a video where many of the \*gods\* of water research are mentioned - if you google any of these people there is a plethora of research about water et al - frankly,reading this paper will blow your mind
[https://www.hitpages.com/doc/5290404618436608/1#pageTop](https://www.hitpages.com/doc/5290404618436608/1#pageTop)

I was reading this Mark Sircus and he mentioned a russian guy named Konstantin Korotkov - anyway, he was able to design a camera that amplified the field in a person so you could see where you were screwed up - but what got me about him is he took some distilled water, combined it with various pharmaceuticals and could test whether the drug would kill the water or leave it be... kind of expensive, but I started thinking maybe if someone were to buy this system, and start testing some common pharma drugs, and post what it did to the water on youtube, it could go viral... and then we could get some air time...

[http://www.korotkov.eu/](http://www.korotkov.eu/)

His page also has some interesting writings you can download - absolutely fascinating how everything affects water... and everything else... here is the link to the downloadable papers... meditation and prayer are key I've even started thanking my water prior to drinking it... the Japanese thank the rice before eating it... all these traditions actually have a basis in science
[http://www.korotkov.eu/selected-papers/](http://www.korotkov.eu/selected-papers/)

### Lahelada — 2016-04-08T13:15:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-192336>

@[Penny](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/446/) Thank you for that .Very interesting. I will do printing this weekend for my Sunday en clausura reading,except that my in clausura will be in an open field ,unreachable for lack of cellphone.
Other than that I just broke an accidental fast. The last two days I have been on the go constantly busy with students,using bus rides to talk to myself and also to some selected students for some opinions. I heard about a wondrous doc who has a light machine in his practice,disappears for hours on end for walks in the green ,usually after three pm....But back to the fast . I was just getting around to thinking about more basic stuff like what to get for dinner nd realised that breakfast or lunch had not even happened. A quick count told me 25hrs into the fast and because of the dynamics I would need to break it at 9pm to eat or stick with it until the next morning. I decided to go for broke and use the hours of the night to "deepfast" as well. Well, it is now hour thirty three, I have just eaten,nothing very optimal eggs with sausage as the reason for the start of the fast was the refrigerator having converted itself into an echo chamber. ..
Reason I am happy about this is that there is a fasting theme on the forum at the moment and I thought heading into winter it would be a good idea for me,too . I was reluctant though as failure to be able to adhere to whatever plan would mean needing to look at whether leptin sensitivity is still present. I have put on weight over the summer and as a chronic weight" onputter" that scared me. I am happy to say though that I have started to lose it even before the fast without trying. I have an unconventional method though. I measure my success by how easily I can squeeze past the refrigerator that halfway blocks the door in the narrow (!) hallway of one of my student's houses. She has now sold it... Guess I will have to measure differently.

About the subject that has sent me on re exploring all the touristic sights of Buenos Aires on foot, meaning I walking my socks off: I could have known what I know now in my thirties, but dogma is a bitch. Thinking now what is still possible. A student going through similar life changes gave me a very good thing to think about. She said I am Argentine you are a foreigner so I tell you for you to understand :"This is Argentina" This is a very popular phrase of resignation used when talking about inflation,expats use it to complain about the lack of peanut butter but it also refers to the exact opposite,to a pride Argentines have in their individuality and the individuality of the country,rightly so in my opinion. It is something I usually portray to people when they ask me why I am here. Trust me in the middle of a 30-40% of inflation,"poisonous ink books" and other interesting societal phenomenons you sound pretty daft saying Argentina is still a land of opportunities .

Anyhoo, two things more then I shut up for a litte while again. I now realise that this doc thing has been latent for a while . Some time back I posted this video below. At the time I thought it was just a bit of nostalgia as I had previously worked for the company. Don't sweat but also don't discard the small stuff.

### Lahelada — 2016-04-10T17:28:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-192467>

@[caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/) I think you will love this.

I had originally planned to go out into the country a bit but decided in the end that I need some music. Quite by accident I came across this. It made my day ,made my week. The tenor I am about to post is probably one of Argentina's finest but had retired from singing or better retired to smaller stages as tenors are wont to do. I saw him in his prime and I was bowled over. The performance I am posting would be a credit to a 40 year old but the tenor singing near 70.
I came across this and understood it totally from what we are doing here to what happened there musically. If you decide to watch it watch everything, the eyebrow of the violinist when the aria reaches a seminal point, ,the women in the audience and the standing ovation later, especially the men.
I was laughing with happiness to see the man realising his dream to be back on THE stage in his hometown,a new stage that has supposedly the best accoustics and is world class. I cried when I got to the end. Watch it even if you do not like opera.

Taking a day for me today ,no reading. I read what Dr K said in another thread ,basically understood it and will act on it. But today I will just revel in being able to feel young again. Have a great Sunday all.

### Lahelada — 2016-04-11T13:46:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-192497>

Thank you thank you thank you,Caroline. I tried to skype you but if you get off the computer at 5pm we have almost no common time . One day we will again.

This week is going to be somewhat interesting with a lot of new life constellations, one completely surprising one out of left field so the shot in the arm does me good. It was the point of the music day ,too . Going to the country would have been good if the sun had been out but it was overcast and later rained. Went into the garden instead and refilled on music.

I wanted to make an addendum to the video. I was present at an opera in London where the tenor "failed" badly just on that very end note of Nessun dorma. I expected the worst that there would be booing etc. The guy on stage was ready to burst into tears it was visible to everyone. And then without a prompt the audience stood up simultaneously, no waiting what everyone else would do and gave the guy a 5 minute standing ovation. When we let ourselves feel things amazing things can happen. German speakers think about the words "fuehlen" und "spueren". They are not exact synonyms. One is more powerful than the other .

Now the English do not even clap normally during a performance so as not to disturb the flow of it. And that got me thinking about a favorite of Dr K
"You can come into my door ,go out my door but you cannot stand in the doorway" People who are of the same frequency as you or near enrich your life,others steal." If you do not want those others to leave ( doubtful decision) then you had bettter make yourself strong and depending where you are you on your journey may not have the extra to support that decision ,even if you think you do. I had to dump the bosses finally before I could move on.

Phrase for the week: **"Don't put your glow on low"** Borrowed from Sean Stephenson

### Lahelada — 2016-04-13T14:18:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-192623>

I am skipping ! Got a very nice thank you for it yesterday,too. The immense offer of help that ensued was even more touching.The unfortunate thing is that the thank you has made me aware of further changes that need to be made. That door had better be wide.
I was thinking about the doctor thing still. I can't get it out of my head. I am more that ever convinced that I would not survive the residence. Talked to a doc the other day about the ins and outs and basically all said and done not even allowing for false starts it is ten years. Now I am planning to be alive for that long and a lot longer but it is not it. A bitter sweet goodbye but the idea is a bit like a hiccup. Just when you think it is gone it comes back. But every time it comes back it leaves a grain to build something new with.

In other news I FINALLY bought the black light. Another useless procrastination on my part. So what if I hadn'tt really needed it medically speaking could I not just have settled for some wacky lighting? Some things are just open and shut not half open, or maybe the wind opened it and it should not be open.
It is raining again, British times here but it gave me the final push to buy it.

I am intrigued by the link Dr K left . I will check it out now. Still chewing on the last clue. A bulb came on with regards to my mother's life and that I had not been wrong to suspect that because I resembled her that I was in a bad way. Not finding my own pulse as a trained first aider should also have been given more prominence. That is why I did not answer the question about what I did with MB. I mentioned that it maybe did a number on my heart ,that the kickstart to my system was pretty drastic, (juicy heart palps) I have also not mentioned the ammonia smell on this forum and I should have as it is a pivotal element of the whole picture. It is now almost gone after MB and nicotine.

Nicotine is a godsend . I cycle between one in the morning or on heavy days up to 4 x 2mg gums. Not feeling signs of addiction though ,the must have my nic gum in the morning so coming from a family of alcoholics that is pretty astounding.

Heteroplasmy o heteroplasmy what is thy number!

### Lahelada — 2016-04-13T14:37:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-192624>

New thoughts about the ex bosses. I left thinking that with my arse I would not need to look at them. A crude saying in German that means that you do not have any reason to look at someone ever again. But to be stuck in the same groove about a past situation says more about your own brain than someone else's . I do know that my boss was sad when I left that went beyond logistics of replacing me. He even ventured that in other circumstances I should not have had a need to accept the kind of job that he had offered (talking about low pay)
I met my ex colleague in the street the other day and of course I could not refrain from asking what happened after I left. Apparently they turned over a new leaf. The chicanery seems to have all but stopped. I asked about the bosses health and he told me no better no worse. last I heard he was on Cox 1 Cox2 inhibitors.
I must say it needled my heart . I liked the guy understood his "problems" but should never have suffered them . There have been good times,the English humor banter, semi insults that you can only weigh when you have lived there for a while which the bossesse had not and she definitely did not like that I could elicit a grudging laughter in the morning from the boss. My "clinical symptoms" are almost identical to the boss so maybe when I am better I will have to pop in and say hello...
My history of light story I cobbled up in the kitchen between serving breakfast ,about missing light frequencies that blue was so penetrating that she could not stand the to have the CFL lights in her office hit home with the bossesse,though. About two weeks later a kinesiologist type infrared lamp appeared in her office.. In the end we all suffered from backache in the same region on the same side. Of course it was given the usual excuses,too much bending,too much sitting etc, weight you name it ... Time 14...

### Lahelada — 2016-05-20T20:24:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-195137>

Back for a little musing and update.
First a musing about my forum name .It was meant as a joke the cold one due to my high cold resistance, but it also means the frozen one , the scared one even if you will. Unconscious choice? Well , the ice has melted and the puddles of water are reforming. Be like water says Bruce Lee. Do I have 5 phases ? I will try and find out.

Having a great time living although that does not mean all is roses but I always find something to sing about or dance to.
Concrete wins:
\- mobility is returning . I can walk on tip toe without pain, bend at every angle and the tightness in my left leg is gone. Back is almost normal. I even managed some gymnastics this morning upstairs on the terrace. Feeling a need to move every which way I can. I seldom stand still these days. Equilibrium is good even in wild dance routines which I now indulge in frequently.

Some time back I reported that I had trouble tapping out a rhythm with my left foot. Happy to say that this is much better now.
I took a little break from MB. After a while I had migraines which I do not normally suffer from. Prolactin inhibition? Not giving it up though ,it has done me too much good. Just recalibrating .

I am also doing a little experiment with singing ,how easy it is to hold a tune and project my voice. It differs from day to day currently. The song I picked as a benchmark is Beautiful Dreamer by Stephen Foster. Always wanted to be able to justice to that song. My voice had gotten very small in the past months .

I am also magically drawn to drums. I want one ! I listen to drums , Kodo drummers for example.

Still many stones unturned, one burning need , and a lot of humility surfacing.

Another win for today - I can run again ,I mean with proper lift and without being too breathless.I had days when I could run but felt that I was going to topple over as if my command to run would not reach the feet.

Yup nic gum two . Using it ad hoc. Yesterday I used 3x 2mg gums ,the first around 9 and the second at 2 pm ish.
Yesterday's menu was not good. I was on the go and after breakfast which was 5 ozs of too salty pork loin I went to a buffet to get some egg dishes for lunch. The supposed vegetarian egg dishes all had squash so after chucking that out they were still too sweet and too little food. I extended my eating window to 4pm to be able to try the new 100 % chocolate just out but not even an ounce. Total fasting hours yesterday : 15 hours

Today so far 6 eggs with 2 ozs salami and coffee. Frontloading today as I will be out all day and do not want to hunt outside looking at the clock. No MB today so far. I ran out..

### Lahelada — 2016-05-29T16:11:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-195577>

Euphorically pensive this Sunday morning. I really do not want to share this as someone might think me silly but that would just be carrying on with the same old crap. So here goes
For some reason this song popped into my head this morning .
"Lets go fly a kite" from the Mary Poppins movie
At the beginning of the song I only felt that the lyrics are the embodiment of the FB post I so wildly "love" every time they appear which say "Live with euphoria"

"With tuppence for paper and string
You can have your own set of wings
With your feet on the ground you're a bird in flight
With your fist holding tight to the string of your kite"

Read more: [Julie Andrews & Dick Van Dyke - Lets Go Fly A Kite Lyrics | MetroLyrics"](http://www.metrolyrics.com/lets-go-fly-a-kite-lyrics-julie-andrews-dick-van-dyke.html#ixzz4A2b5f0p8)

But then something else that is recently on my mind and the product of all the discussion on page 53 of this journal crept into this too.

While conscious that this is my projection only , and that is the job of a good piece of art to be the lie that reveals the truth, I saw something else in the face of Mary Poppins that I had not expected : CPC 11 ..after all she is the nanny/teacher to the children and the faces of two very good friends , my face and that of a lot of strangers who without knowing became my teachers. Watch 1:23 . Watch the expression . That is the face of anyone who has managed to make a difference to someone's life and they skip down the road into their new life without looking back. Don't take it for granted. Look back, smile and say thanks even if you expect a gruff response. One's thanks makes a difference and comes back to your recipient as a gift when times are not so rosy and quite frankly hard. One might even try and say thanks even when one is not sure if one has been given something of value. Some things only show their value later on in the process. I have been given all these gifts.

Thanks @[Albert83BCN](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/5984/) for accidentally pointing me in a direction I had never gone and entering a world I never would have seen. Now that sounds cryptic haha. It was actually a joke he posted with no intent but in true quantum fashion it sent me scurrying down another rabbit hole and I have not surfaced yet again.
Thanks @[caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/) for the enlightening,caring conversations.
Thanks @[Scompy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/17718/) for inspiring my new fasting regime.
Thanks to all my students for teaching me.
Thanks to everyone who helped me through the tribulations on page 53 and made me see a bigger picture
Thanks s to someone who sent me "light in shaping life" which is now languishing in customs
Thanks to two good friends who pull me up on my mistakes or make me realise them while talking about something seemingly unconnected.
And as always my biggest thanks to @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)

docere = to teach, to cause to know

### Lahelada — 2016-06-02T03:55:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-195845>

So this was already a perfect day but it got better :
I spent the afternoon in the park reading Time 6 and time 14 back to back and finally pieced it all together.Where I am good what I need to do better. I took some selfies but technical reasons prohibit me for posting at the moment so the place holder "Geniol screw head "(Geniol is a local aspirin) will have to do for now.
Came back to news that the contravention of the the 2nd law of thermodynamics" is being talked about here ,too. Lo and behold there is someone spreading the word even down here. Maybe that is a shot in the arm for our Doc up there...

In other news I did my own to promote CT here. I am a poster child as I walk around in Indian summer clothes when everybody else wears fleece and padded jackets. Two ladies had quite an eye opener this afternoon when I asked them how well they felt, how often they got sick and to compare themselves to the guys that walked around in T shirts just like me but worked in the meat freezer section. One said as if she had never noticed before : True that. They do not get sick as often as we do...

Those 2 incidents made my day...

### Lahelada — 2016-06-02T14:41:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-195865>

@[notsoperfick](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/10850/) You are not so late to the party. The question is still on my mind and FB ever so often acts to keep it there by reminding me of where I could study.
Funny thing happened yesterday night at a student/friend's house I was talking about having an offer of an apartment but in area which I know to be bad for EMF. She tried to talk me into it saying : "Think that you would be closer to your patients ,err I mean students oh well same difference". I won't deny that the involuntary slip gave me pleasure , left me open mouthed but also that maybe I am looking for the place where I am best placed and I am already there. Docere = to teach and if my rusty Latin is trust worthy" to bring out "as well. My teaching methods are unconventional in the sense that I assess my student and their current learning ability and adjust their curriculum accordingly . It is quite amazing how really bright minds can be switched off and stay switched off when put under excess pressure from the side where they most seek to excel in,where they feel most vulnerable in ,be it relationship or work. My evening students appear with bloodshot eyes and fixed size pupils as if they had been on a bender for two days on normal days,add unexpected stress and you have the danger of losing your student as they cannot process one more thing .

About nicotine I actually would not like to say too much as people use it for various reason brain clarity, energy, as part of a fasting biohack , a heteroplasmy biohack but in fact we are all working on the same in different guises it seems to me only our presentation is different. Google "Jack Kruse" nicotine and you will get all sorts of good answers.

Another funny from yesterday. I spent a lot of time in the local park and on leaving decided to take a shortcut leaping of a 2 feet high wall. I say leaping it was an ungraceful hop. Oh yes as expected the result was an "Ouch" but surprisingly I held up and stayed on my feet . Previously my leg might have given way and I would have lost balance. First lesson was that my "heart" knew more about what I was capable of doing than what my head was letting me believe. Head was projecting an ungainly splat on the pavement. Second lesson was that I am overcompensating for my right ankle still when there is no need . Collagen recovering?
And yes there were probably less jarring methods of coming to that conclusion . But it was fun to play in the park !

### Lahelada — 2016-06-02T19:09:53+0300

I transcribed it as : "Losing the omnipotent projection of impotence in future events frees up a lot of potency in the now. "
Richard Branson put it better though: Screw it, just do it!
I hope humble pie has no carbs..

### Lahelada — 2016-06-15T16:29:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-196627>

And then there was light .... LIGHT in shaping life is here ! Thank youuuu ! It was almost thwarted at the last hurdle and ended up in customs for collection. Not a good sign around these parts usually as the import duty is enormous and the paperwork even more so.
So yesterday I was sitting on an island in the delta, leathersoled boots on and read my first pages. Barefoot would have been better of course but the boots had been on for hours and probably would not have gone on again.

I stopped MB and nic gum for now. Next week I will go for routine blood tests and I am leaving 10 days between the last nic gum and the tests. I miss it. There is no sun to speak of at the moment and I feel rather sluggish with spurts of energy interspersed. Cold helps. Sunbed later this week too. Let's see how that goes.

One good thing to report today is that my right ankle is getting better every day. Until now it was very weak from non use and inflammation that had made it impossible to bend or put my full weight on . There is now a minimal twinge and I can even run a block even in above mentioned boots.

I must get a new UV lamp,mine broke ,maybe that will help too.

The dog is experiencing a renaissance which I feel kind of ridiculous to share as the lights involved are cheap Chinese Xmas lights . Basically ever since Xmas she has made a beeline for the red lights and insists on sleeping right under them . She was one of those pups that came with a black nose that in her first year started to go pink. According to supposition that has to do with melanin. Now age 9 she has a black nose again. I don't have any pics of her before the arrival of teh lights which annoys me .Could it have to do with the lights ?? If they were some sort of biomed light I would not hesitate to think it, but three dollar xmas lights??

### Lahelada — 2016-06-21T14:01:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-196999>

To think I almost did not share that puppy hack..

Air CT yesterday with 4C/19F . People are gasping, jeje. Pain so reduced I can almost do what I want again. Come to think of it, what do I want.

The apartment hunt is not going anywhere. What I have been offered is a 6th floor very nice apartment,big,airy with a bath tub for Ct but it is the 6th floor in a highrise surrounded by highrises in the same area where I used to work. I keep saying , I am stronger ,healthier but even I cannot convince myself of that this will be good for me. It is true , one cannot "unknow".. What I suspected at the beginning of the year though is now happening, inflation is increasing and people are getting paranoid and are even less likely to rent without guarantee. Blah.

One interesting thing, I did a Latin test this weekend. I found my old school report and remembered that I hold the certificate for Comprehensive Latin . Well,lo and behold after 30 years of not even thinking about it I can still pass the exam and am at midlevel . This is kind of important to me as the teaching method is quite different, but seems to stick better than all the modern stuff like multiple choice etc.

### Lahelada — 2016-06-29T03:20:09+0300

Blah,succumbed to the fourth round of "flu" here. The annoying thing is that all the people I told that CT is good for you now tell me "you see.." No mention that they got all four go arounds. The ladies at the chicken shop still have faith. They have taken one out of three jumpers off and are rolling up their sleeves....baby steps
For my treatment I am doing curcumin in everything, mag sulphate/hydrogen peroxide sponged all over my body and in ears. no humidifier or bath tub to intensify treatment.

### Lahelada — 2016-06-29T14:02:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-197509>

Thanks guys. I feel better this morning. The mag, hydroxy seems to have done something , although not a miracle cure. I am better though than could be expected on the second day.
@[Inger](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1057/) no fear about taking anything chemical meaning a commercial anti flu thing. It is actually not vene the flu just a nasty cold but ever since the big flu pnic every sniffle is a flu it seems. Everywhere you go there are posters reminding you of the free flu shot. No thanks.

In other news I feel great though. Being cold has extinguished the rest of my ankle/foot pain. I can now run,even bound down stairs . The tell will be if I can make it last over summer or if inflammation will continue. DHA intake this winter will be key.

Trying to get DHA . Still not easy. I have days when I crave fish and others when I cannot stand it. Oysters are ok any day but are in short supply at the moment. Smoked salmon for lunch today though.

### Lahelada — 2016-07-04T12:50:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-197834>

Funny,relevant Story I have not shared yet.
Last Thursday I got a text from a friend saying 2 Ha, you are always cold because the acute stress does you good ." No further explanation. I had had the impression that she looked at me a bit worried the other day when it was really cold and I still was in my T shirt. I wrote back with a Haha, nope not quite but good. I wondered though as at the time of texting she should have been at the doctor's office. I saw her Friday and indeed she had texted me from the doctors office after mentioning my weird case in her consult...Apparently the longhand reply from her doc was I was correct in what I was doing and that I was trying to elicit a paleolithic extreme stress response to combat the physical stresses the modern world puts me under. Now if I interpret that correctly then this is not too far from what we think here. Now there is at least two docs I know of here who are roughly on board with what we do here .

The story goes on though . Her doc told her about her lack of sleep and as same said doc has an undefined programmable light source on his desk we started talking about the effect of light. I told her what I know about sun and frequencies and she is going to buy amber lights,maybe a UV light too. The whole conversation collapsed into heaps of laughter though when her partner came through the door and she told him that soon she would be sleeping well but needed to be under yellow and black light but that he should not worry too much as that will make her younger too. He was quite on board after I explained a little to him , too but the look on his face when we so graphically described his new homelife a minute after he had walked in through the door from work was priceless...

The laughter was good though as on a serious note her doctor had quite graphically explained what she would heading for if she did not adjust her sleep cycle. Part two of the conversation to follow next week.

### Lahelada — 2016-08-09T21:31:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-199460>

So my days since the "I wanna be a doctor" episode have never been quite the same again . I spent the morning preparing a multilevel talk on quantum principles and disease that can either skim the surface depending on acceptance of my audience or go deeper and which I am nervous as heck about and then have to interrupt the undertaking to give a class to a new student.

Well, so I met this student, a lady who lives just across the park from me and we talk about the park and that we should do the class in the park, when it is warmer. I am compelled to comment on how people avoid the sun, how contrary it is to our nature and guess what I have a captive audience who in turn introduces me to the work of Enric Corbera with the apologetic words that to understand his writings I would need to know about quantum physics...

Guess who never stopped talking after that ? Yup, out came haplotypes, Doug Wallace ,mitochondria,grounding and cold thermogenesis in quite a neat little package and to be quite frank I had no idea that I could present as I did. The lady had to remind me that our hour was up and that if I wanted we could talk about this subject next time again . I thought oops got carried away but as it is a conversation class one has to talk about something,right?

Well, I got quickly corrected. The lady came back with my fee augmented by 25% saying that this class was really worth more than my advertised rate and that if I agreed we would continue on this basis...
Was I just paid as a quantum health speaker ? The mind reels...

### Lahelada — 2016-09-11T17:47:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-201302>

Lesson for the rest of my life:

Fear should never be a reason to say NO.
Fear should never be a reason not to ask for help.
Fear should never be a reason not to accept the help you are given.
And sometimes you are not given help so that you may overcome your fear
and that way regain your strength.

My father said it to me : Know when to hold and when to let go, of everything and everyone . Know what people need of you to do. Know what to do for yourself.

Too much going on to explain this so I will just let it stand . Someone out there may just understand.

### Lahelada — 2016-12-07T03:45:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-204895>

So it has been three month almost . Time for an update on the cryptic last entry.

Basically I was fed up with myself. Even though I had changed my job there was other things I had not taken into account that I needed to do.

Things I realised: I am not happy to just have a job. I want to make a difference. What difference could that be. Well,there is two avenues. One is my own school which I have talked about before, but one that takes into account low dopamine learning difficulties. I am making in roads with developing a programme and method. I have my first students on a trial basis . They are the family of a friend of mine. basic principles I employ are acting tricks for elocution,very old books that actually overload today's brains . ( as learned here..) and lighting of course.

The other idea still seems a few numbers too big for me . There is also no money in it. A labour of love but I have had a couple of tasters of it and it is very rewarding.

A move is on the cards next year. Outside of the city definitely or maybe into the country completely. I am getting teaching offers left right and centre. The one that intrigues me most is going to the Patagonia where English teachers are needed for companies and schools. Low EMf, but harsh winters . UV is dicy , only at altitude and also in summer really but the altitudes to be had are no more than 2200 feet. However being a Northern haplotype it might be enough. Worth a biohack maybe.

I am single again...Basically our paths were not very joined from the beginning. Which I had realised at the beginning but things change ,right ? Right...not! So better to paddle my own canoe. Over the last three month I realised what an energy drain this has been on me.

In another update I will try and do a health review. I will send ahead though that I am now having my first completely painfree days ,in other words to include the pesky ankle that previously always hurt and weight is creeping into the right direction,too.

### fitness@home — 2016-12-07T14:50:10+0200
