# Thread: Getting back to a normal weight from the other end of the scale
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 427
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hemming — 2013-07-04T22:10:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-89507>

As opposed to many other who come here I have the problem that I need to gain weight - not lose it. I've already told some of my story in this thread [http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?7308-How-to-recover-from-anorexia](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?7308-How-to-recover-from-anorexia) (I really appreciate all the replies I got) but figured I wanted to make it a bit more formal and with ongoing updates. I really hope that a lot of the people here will take part in my journey with comments, questions etc.

I'm a 26y old male, 5'9''. My story is that I developed anorexia athletica after I was suddenly done studying, had the perfect job but didn't know what to do with my life outside of that as I had no clear goal(s) which I was use to (as in get and education and get a job). Following that I developed an eating disorder which took me to a weight of 108lbs at the lowest, I'm currently around 113.5-114.5lbs. I've been feeling miserable both physically and mentally for around 1y now but after I discovered Jack's blog and resets my mood has definitely increased. For the last year I've followed a paleo diet and for the last eight months, more or less a ketogenic diet. I've been through all of the physical and mental symptoms of anorexia which I can elaborate on if anyone is interested.

I wanted to start this journal as I will go on medical leave from my job tomorrow to get time to relax and get myself back in shape. It's amazing how much the reset protocols have helped me already but there is still a long way to go, despite my weight being where it is I still find it very difficult to convince myself that I should gain although I don't want to lose weight either.
The thing that has surprised me the most is how effective CT has been in improving my mood. I take a cold shower in the morning before breakfast and another one before leaving work or going to bed. Additionally, I take a swim in the ocean 2-3 times per week for 15-20min - that is my way of getting high.

My plan for the next couple of months is to 'get back to mother nature' by continuing with the protocols and especially ocean swims since summer is on and I live close to the ocean. I'm also seeing a specialist clinic with a doctor, psychologist, dietitian etc., but honestly, I don't understand their methods and especially don't believe in their dietary guidelines.

### caroline — 2013-07-05T13:10:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-89523>

Honestly - I think you should be swimming in the ocean every single day and walking on the rocks and lots and lots of sun.
Dr. Kruse has said somewhere here .....if you live near the ocean and you are not in it every single day ....you are a damn fool!! This is the perfect opportunity for you to spend everyday at the ocean with as little clothing on as possible.

What about fishing? what about camping? you need to enjoy every single second of this spectacular opportunity you have.
Wake up early - get the sun on you - have your BAB...... and head to the ocean.

Do you know anything about Movnat? google it and have a read. Erwan is an amazing young man who is tuned into nature 100%.

Cold showers are only okay if you can't do full immersion - a bathtub full of ice would be so great for you. Do you have a hot tub outside? Dr. K. uses his hot tub as a cold tub ....just add ice and stir .... no clothing allowed! \[you don't want tan lines! and you need the sun on every single inch of your body\]

All of your health problems didn't just happen - they were along time in the making .... and so it is going to take awhile to get better ....just baby steps and enjoy the ride

Are you reading all JK's blogs? You will discover that anorexia and obesity are on the same spectrum - just at opposite ends. Have you listened to the webinars? and don't forget when you read a blog - you really need to read the comments as well - they are often just as important and sometimes more important.

Good luck .... have a beautiful day at the ocean....notice the children - they are majic ....notice the sky and the tress and the flowers etc. and engage in conversations with strangers - they, very often, have a wonderful story to tell.

Dr. Kruse has said .....if your hands are open - you will receive gifts......

You are so young with an amazing future ahead of you - just relax and enjoy your journey to optimal health.

### Hemming — 2013-07-05T16:50:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-89532>

I'll be doing more swimming in the coming months. Don't worry about the clothing - I'm as close to naked as possible without being accused of anything

I've been thinking of those things too. I'll be moving back to my parents next week and as they live in a rural area with forests etc. close (in addition to the beach) I'll be using that too. I'm also considering setting up a tent in their garden and sleeping there. Besides that I have a stack of books that I want to read. I do know about MovNat and that is exactly one of the things I've been thinking about doing.

I don't have a bath tub at my apartment. My parents do have a bath tub inside. I would prefer the swims anyway, especially now that I can feel how I've adapted to them.

I've printed out all the blog posts and put them in different folders so I'm slowly reading them from the beginning. Right now I'm at CT 7 and I can assure you I'm reading the comments and commenting too. I really like that Jack specifically mentions anorexia too, all too many get caught with losing weight. It would be great if Jack did a blog post on anorexia because I completely agree with that anorexia and obesity is on the same spectrum - I've been telling a lot of people that already. The thing is that I need people to tell me it's okay to eat, I really like food, especially fats at the moment (whereas three months ago it was protein) yet I still like to restrict my eating which is also why I haven't gained weight. The BAB I really like but I still find myself heading to work too often thinking I could have eaten a little more. Just like some people can't stop I find it too easy to stop eating. Even though I really coconut and butter for instance I still get scared about eating too much of it even though it's probably better for me to eat more. The fun thing is that the foods I like the most are exactly those on the Epi Paleo diet but I'm still scared of eating too much of them.

Today I did 15min (450m of swimming) in 18 degrees water, it was great!

Thank you so much for the support! I'm hoping that this is only beginning of a journey towards optimal. I'll probably be asking a lot more questions along the way as I need constant reassurance that I'm doing the right thing, this disease completely ruins your brain.

### caroline — 2013-07-06T05:42:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-89576>

Please, please, please ask away..... you are only making this trip once - lets make it outrageous and wonderful and dream big........why the heck not.....

My one big wish for you.......you will get back your health.....and then pay it forward and help others ......actually, I think you already see enough results to start paying it forward. I would guess that this disease is harder for a man?

When you are staying with your parents and sleeping in a tent and looking at the stars and dreaming big dreams ....anything is possible!

You will be able to create a movnat adventure for yourself every day in the forest ..... how lucky are you ..... beach and forest!! Are you able to do a movnat with Erwan? He has the whole mind/ body/ brain thing going on it. It would be worth every dime.

Have you listened to the June webinar? and the Q&A with Erwan? Think of all this as the optimal investment in your health and future.

Please be supremely positive about your future......allow no maybes.......everyone needs support from time to time but you are going to get there if you are 100% committed.....the alternate isn't an option.
Even JK says he learns stuff from us sometime...... he might have just been being nice tho......

Have you thought of having a BPC in the morning with your BAB? \[bulletproof coffee .... and using MCT oil and GS butter in it plus cocoa and vanilla\] Don't stress - you will get there.....you have years of wrong thinking to overcome but you will get there. If you fall down \[and we all do\] just get back up and start again. That is the mark of a committed, courageous person who is 100% all in.

I would love it if you would post everyday and tell us the good, the bad and the ugly. Make a commitment to talk to strangers everyday and listen to their stories. Take yourself out of the equation for awhile ....and smile at a child and see what happens - they lite up the world!

Have a great weekend!

### caroline — 2013-07-06T05:46:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-89577>

One other thing.... If you have a look at Dave Asprey's site ....he has things that might interest you - like colustrum and whey protein etc. etc. They may be really good things for you to add in.

### caroline — 2013-07-06T10:47:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-89585>

One more thing! Have you tried chocolate bark? ... I think that is what we call it

Just melt some butter and coconut oil and a couple bars of dark chocolate \[85% or more\] then add some pistaschios and shredded coconut and macadamias or whatever you fancy - stir and put in a pan and let it set in the fridge....very yummy.

This would be so good for you every day as a treat!

### Hemming — 2013-07-06T12:51:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-89589>

Caroline, again thank you so much for all of your input and support, really appreciate it (keep it up, please)!

It has definitely been difficult to tell it to certain people and I still find it easier to 'eating disorder' compared to 'anorexia'. I've been contacted by the national tv station by a reporter who wants to do a documentary about a guy with bulimia and me. She hasn't received final approval yet but I've said I want to participate to 'pay it forward' without sounding too philanthropic

I'll look more into the MovNat but right now I don't want to plan too much ahead. I haven't heard any of Erwan's webinars actually, MovNat is pretty new to me other than I know the principles of it.

I very rarely have coffee with my breakfast but have it a few hours after. I don't have anything particular against coffee along with breakfast, I just prefer to have it afterwards, especially when I go swimming after breakfast I don't want to drink anything hot.

I was planning on doing an update of what I've been doing and how I feel each day - first one will be here later today

I'm not a big fan of supplements, especially not something like protein powder, then I would rather eat some more. I do know of a place where I've sourced raw cream which is amazing

I haven't tried the chocolate bark, it's one of those things I would exactly consider a treat and it would take me ages to get through because I would allow myself to eat it in small quantities a couple of times each week. I made some ganache with 99% chocolate and cream which I'm still fighting my way through. As I said, I need someone to tell me it's okay to eat. Basically what I would like right now is a diet plan to tell me how much to eat of everything. I have a fear of carbs, protein and fat, just to a varying degree. First I get scared of getting too many carbs, then it's protein because of perceived liver and kidney damage then it's fat because of cholesterol/triglycerides then it's the SCT, MCT, LCT, SFA, MUFA and PUFA - I try to optimise everything. So even though I much prefer the fat to carbs I get scared of eating too much fat at some point too, which is, most likely, at a too low level to fuel my body as I have chronic fatigue and feel cold very often.

There are so many things to tell - I'll get to all of it at some point. I hope you and everybody continue to chime in and challenge my beliefs and perceived boundaries.

### Hemming — 2013-07-06T21:15:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-89612>

Today started off with the usual weigh in before breakfast, which I still can't stop doing, to see that I weighed 53kg. I knew that I would most likely weigh a little more than yesterday but I didn't like seeing it at 53kg as I was at 51.6kg earlier this week. Something like that doesn't ruin my entire day but it did stop me from going all in on the BAB (at least I didn't get to 50g of protein). I felt cold and tired afterwards which I'm thinking could be because I didn't eat enough.
I went to the beach to do a 15min swim (the water is around 17-18 degrees), going from being a little down I went to being so relieving and thinking that I'll make it anyway. Back home it was great getting a cup of coffee on the balcony with the sun shining from a clear sky.
Afternoon was spent with my mentor (she is volunteering) at a farmer's market where I got some very good salmon and goat hearts which I'm really looking forward to tasting.

Overall I guess it was a good day but I'm still so confused about how much I should be eating.

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-07-06T21:41:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-89615>

@Hemming: You and I are more alike than I would have imagined from seeing a few of your previous posts. I too try to "optimize everything", as you can tell by my post on the PPP and ketosis, as well as some other posts here and on the Bulletproof Exec forums. Lot's of times I worry myself about too many things for little to no reason (macronutrients, similar to you, for instance). I don't have a diagnosed eating disorder, but now that I'm much more of a 'critical thinker', I know that I had/maybe still have an E.D. I used to worry about my body so much, I would do odd things to make myself 'look better', wouldn't wear certain clothes that were too baggy nor too tight because I'd look fat.... these are all things I see many girls my age now doing, and I'm a male! I'm in the same place as you now - I need to gain weight. I still worry about all the minutia. I'm reading and annotating the book The Ketogenic Diet by Lyle McDonald and I keep getting all worried about refeeds - will I eat so many carbs that I knock myself out of ketosis for more than a few days? Will I become extremely bloated the next morning? Am I simply going to be drastically raising my sdLDL and trigs? Am I not eating enough carbs during this period to gain muscle? Well, in the past week or two, oddly enough, I'm slowing down on all the worrying that I'm doing. That won't help anyone in a position similar to me or you.

When I first started experimenting with my diet and how it makes me 'feel', as opposed to just eating something because it's perceived to be healthy and maybe, just maybe it will help me live a bit longer or prevent some unforeseen disease I probably wouldn't get anyhow, I started by cutting out gluten and very quickly progressing to a calorically deficient vegetarian diet. I wanted to lost weight that I didn't really need to lose. Sure, I wasn't ripped or anything, I was bulky, if I simply lost a little bit of fat or put on some muscle, all would be good. But no, I had to lose a bunch of weight. I dropped down to roughly 129lbs at about 5'8. The dark circles under my eyes that I developed Sophomore year of high school from enormous amounts of stress from family problems further developed. Some of my 'problems' I sought to defeat were improving, but others developed as well. I'm not trying to make this post about me, but I'm just trying to give some context so you don't think I'm just talking out my @$$, lol.

If you weren't in the position that you were in, I'd recommend a book on cyclical dieting, but (please don't take offense), I feel as though you may end up worrying yourself over all the minute details and cause yourself more problems. I'd say you simply have to eat some calorie dense foods since you may have a problem with eating 'lots' of food so you gain the weight you need. I see you're doing the BAB's, so you're probably doing the Leptin Reset. I'd eat lots of eggs and dairy if you tolerate it. Cheese and cream are extremely calorie dense. You can even use cheese and cream to add calories to your morning breakfast without noticing it - add Heavy Whipping Cream to eggs, which makes them really fluffy and adds a interesting texture and creamy flavor, and hundreds of calories; add a bit to coffee in the morning and have it as a side to your meal; then add cheese to your eggs. You can easily get 1000kcal in your breakfast alone doing that. Considering you do CT and you seem to be active, and you eat a paleo/epi-paleo template, you don't have to worry about getting fat, and in your position a little fat may actually be beneficial, rather than negative. I would recommend nuts and seeds, but that is entirely dependent on context (although dairy is as well); each nut/seed has different pros and cons. I myself am waiting to get blood work done and to see how eating a seafood heavy diet makes me 'feel' and do the Leptin Reset through the whole summer, before I add in any nuts/seeds. I also want to see if they cause any cognitive decline after eating due to mold/heating/etc.

Well I kind of went on a rant without getting to much of a point. I personally recommend that you continue reading Dr. Kruse' blogs, reading the forums, especially the Ask Jack threads, and continue updating us, because everyone here will help. Continue eating solid meals (no snacks) and breakfast. Continue CT. You should be able to 'rewire' your brain so to speak and beat this. If obesity and anorexia are truly in the same spectrum of problems (disordered hormonal signalling causing negative psychological feedback loops), then you can perform 'surgery without a blade' and help yourself. You can use these boards/us the members as your own free-of-charge personal cognitive behavioral therapy type device. Then, again, I recommend adding in lots of calorie dense foods. It might be hard in your position, even though you know you need to gain weight you still might have those thoughts in the back of you're head, "what if I get fat," or "what if I'm actually causing myself a slow predisposition to heart disease?" Realize that all those questions you may come up with are obstacles and you should in fact question those questions, lol. Try doing what I said if you tolerate dairy and add a bunch of cheese and HWC to eggs and you will consume massive amounts of calories. If you come up with some psychological aversion, think that how fluffy, light, and delicate the eggs become when you add HWC/cheese, that is how fluffy, light and delicate your lipoprotein particles are becoming thus helping you become healthier, not worse off (trigs/sdLDL). Maybe add face dunks specifically to your CT regiment to train your vagus nerve and mammalian dive reflex.

Hope to see you post back ASAP!

### Hemming — 2013-07-06T22:21:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-89616>

@BigPapaChakra777, thanks a lot for your kind words and chiming in, I didn't at all get the impression that you were trying to make the post about you. It's very interesting to hear from other people how they have worked with the same problems and it does seem like our stories are pretty similar.

I love cheese and have no adverse effects from eating it but I'm still afraid of eating too much of it because of the insuliogenic effect and the protein. Regarding protein, I've been trying to find a particular recommendation for grams/kg of body weight on the Epi Paleo - if anyone is familiar with any I would be very happy to hear it. Jack did reply in the other thread, saying that I should just eat them but if I have 50g at breakfast then I would think that I'm already at, at least 50% of my daily protein need.
Previously I could get bloated from cream but that seems to have gone away, at least if I eat it with other things. As I'm very interested in coffee beans I never put anything in my coffee  I still find it difficult to eat that many nuts, the only ones I eat regularly are brazil and macadamina nut and the brazil nuts are maximally three at a time.

I'm definitely continuing my morning swims (and now I have time to do it every day), it's really incredible how much of a therapeutic effect it has had on me. It was tough in the beginning (I remember shivering for two hours afterwards) but the feeling afterwards is so good.

### caroline — 2013-07-07T09:21:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-89634>

I am wondering about your weigh ins ......could you try to shift them to after your BAB? I guess you already know it would be best to not weigh yourself so often.

Could you make some chocolate bark.....or ask your Mom to do it ....so you don't have to think about the fat calories etc. And then have a piece with your coffee. Maybe she could make you a gorgeous omelet with HC and a lovely cheese.
Research the protein powder and colostrum on Dave Asprey's site. If I understand this correctly - colustrum is an
excellent healing thing to have.

You shouldn't be concerned about the amount of protein - that is what Dr. K was trying to tell you - I eat tons of fat and don't put any weight on at all. Eat a huge BAB and see where that takes you .....and try not to stress.

I know - easier said than done. That was the reason for the BPC - extra fat.

Just take one day at a time......try to do something out of your ordinary everyday ...and then tell us about it!

Alot of us here have had eating disorders - me included. The BAB works wonders.

Have another great day.....

### Hemming — 2013-07-07T13:30:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-89648>

The weighs in are irrational but one way I can 'control' what is happening to my body. I love numbers and to quantify everything. Previously, I would weigh everything I ate. Now I don't have to weigh everything but then I err on the 'safe side' by eating less because I don't have the control of knowing exactly how much I eat.

I haven't moved back home (yet) but mom would make me anything I asked for  I'll have a look at the colustrum.

Yesterday i would say that I ate more than normally - especially fat. This morning I could see that my stomach looked less bloated and I was 52.5kg. I've had problems for a long time with edema in my legs and stomach and I can actually see that it gets better when I eat more - still I'm too stupid to fully realise it and eat more consistently.

When I was at the farmer's market yesterday I came to think of you and started a longer discussion with some of the people there. The most priceless comment was 'you're the first white man to ask for goat kidneys' from the same guy who I bought the goat hearts from
I live near a very big nature reserve and I was thinking of doing a half day walk around there for trying something different. Tomorrow I have a test day at a specialist clinic for eating disorders, looking at the weather forecast I would rather not go and enjoy the weather instead

@BigPapa, I forgot to mention, I have exactly the same thoughts as you about ketosis thinking 'will this little extra cauliflower knock me out of ketosis?' Today when I biked back from the beach I came by one of the best ice cream places in Denmark where they make everything from scratch from the best ingredients. I know I wouldn't die from having an ice cream but it is something I would never allow myself at this point.

Question for everyone. I've often noticed that I get dots/clouds in front of my eyes when I'm out in the sun or when I'm biking, even sitting on the balcony reading Jack's book. Could this be sodium deficiency or dehydration?

### Hemming — 2013-07-08T18:28:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-89700>

Today I went for a test day at a specialist clinic, I can't stop thinking about One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest... I just feel that there is too much control of the patients there for my situation. I appreciate that some people need the control but asking for permission to do a lot of things and 'deciding' whether or not I should go to Paris in August is ridiculous to me. My problem is not that I don't eat at all but that I need to eat more and be comfortable with gaining weight. Besides that, there is a big focus on the 'healthy grains' and a 'balanced diet' - I simply don't believe in any of those.
That's not saying I don't have a problem but it needs to be handled in a different way.

It was great getting home and going to the beach for a swim!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-07-08T19:40:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-89705>

### Hemming — 2013-07-08T20:25:39+0300

Thanks Jack, but I don't expect you mean more carbs in the form of industrially made crispbread and strawberry ice cream as I was given today. Your recommendation on protein has also helped me with my fear of eating too much of it.

I'm trying to do as much as possible about the environment and my daily life. I CT/swim everyday, limit EMF/time in front of the computer, maximise sun exposure walking/biking in green/forestry areas, sleep 8-9 hours (following the sun and with sleep mask and ear plugs), eat locally grown vegetables which are in season, drink non-fluoridated water and (obviously) read your book and blog
If anyone has more specific tips for things I could do I would be very happy to hear them.

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-07-08T21:17:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-89717>

So what do you recommend, Dr. Kruse? The visual snow I've had since a child, I remember one day it randomly appeared and I told my mom I saw 'tv fuzz' everywhere, especially when it was dark. Floaters I've had for at least a few years, maybe longer. When I had my eyes checked when I was 17 at the ophthalmologist they said my eyes were fine and it was either (1) in my head, or (2) something that could be fixed with pharmaceutical eye drops..... I knew it wasn't in my head and I was in no way, shape, or form taking pharmaceutical anything.... especially not daily eye drops, lol. As of now I've just been keeping it in the back of my head and hoping as my diet gets increasingly nutrient dense and I can start affording more 'whole fish' (heads/eyes/etc.) and organ meats, it would get better. If not, I was going to look into Yoga For Your Eyes by Meir Schneider and perform those exercises daily. If you have any recommendations I'd get started on that now.... my quality of life would be so much better without visual snow and floaters - although it's just an annoyance it is a rather large annoyance that I deal with daily.

### caroline — 2013-07-09T10:28:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-89748>

Hi hemming! I am so glad to hear that you engaged in more conversations at the market! Yesterday I was talking to a lady on the bus that had taught in the Gobi desert for years .....at the college of cabbage! I swear - that is what it was called! She was just this sweet little old lady - I was floored when she told me what she had done for years!
It sounds like you are making great progress. So - when you move back home - ask your mom to make your food for you. It might be less stressful for you?
I have some dear friends whose only child (son - about your age actually) was anorexic. They spent many heartbreaking years trying to get him help. They still worry constantly....

What fun/different thing are you going to do tomorrow? I want to hear about something really outrageous!

You are going to Paris? Wonderful! I hope you look into doing a movnat with Erwan - I am thinking that the huge amount of physical exertion in the outdoors would be so good to clear your head and focus your energy on the now....

### Hemming — 2013-07-09T16:37:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-89766>

Yesterday I was talking to a lady on the bus that had taught in the Gobi desert for years .....at the college of cabbage! I swear - that is what it was called! She was just this sweet little old lady - I was floored when she told me what she had done for years!
> It sounds like you are making great progress. So - when you move back home - ask your mom to make your food for you. It might be less stressful for you?
> I have some dear friends whose only child (son - about your age actually) was anorexic. They spent many heartbreaking years trying to get him help. They still worry constantly....
>
> What fun/different thing are you going to do tomorrow? I want to hear about something really outrageous!
>
> You are going to Paris? Wonderful! I hope you look into doing a movnat with Erwan - I am thinking that the huge amount of physical exertion in the outdoors would be so good to clear your head and focus your energy on the now....

The guy at the market was so happy to see me again and shook my hand. I really liked "I know you asked for four goat hearts but I brought all I had in case you wanted the lot", obviously I had to buy all of them, and they're so cheap! They taste very good too.

Mom would indeed be doing most of the cooking. Dad is retired so we would team up for the lunch. Speaking of worried, when I came back from the morning swim today I called dad (while enjoying the sun on the balcony) as we had some practical things to sort out, my phone ran out of battery which I didn't really think about as we had discussed most of the things so I went to get my hair cut. When I came home I turned on my phone and almost instantly dad called saying he was outside, I thought that was a little strange. Turns out, he had been so worried if something had happened to me that he drove all the way to my apartment just to see if I was okay  So we got a nice talk on the balcony and as I was organising the apartment anyway, he took some of their things I had borrowed.

Just organising the apartment and getting some of the crates moved to the basement has been surprisingly relaxing. I have so many loose ends that I want to close regarding my apartment, it's just so great to finally have the energy and time to do something about it. I'm a little surprised that I've been able to relax so much on just a couple of days of medical leave.

Yep, Paris is all booked now. I can't wait to go out and see Versailles!

I haven't planned anything 'outrageous'  If anyone have any good ideas for things I can do (of all kinds) I'm all ears. I was thinking of getting a huge jigsaw puzzle

### caroline — 2013-07-10T10:17:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-89813>

Do you have a dog? Wonderful about Paris......

Maybe try to engage your parents in different ways - go fishing with your Dad and then cook them on a Barbie!! Maybe go camping with your Dad and have some conversations as friends......sleep under the stars.....swim naked in a river.....

### caroline — 2013-07-10T10:23:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-89814>

What about getting a hot tub and sharing a glass of wine with Mom and Dad...... while you relax in the bubbles!

I am sure they are desperate to know and understand that you are getting your life on track .....there is no turning back you know! Share your dreams of your wonderful future with them....

### Hemming — 2013-07-10T12:43:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-89817>

@Caroline, I live in an apartment, it would be fair to any dog to have it crammed in here while I work 8-9 hours each day.

Dad has already suggested different things. Coincidentally, fishing and camping is surprisingly 'difficult' in Denmark because you permits for both of them if you want to do the real thing.

I don't mind spending money on getting but hot tub is not an option. If it was to be made properly it would cost a fortune and my parents are definitely not interested in getting one  Besides that, they would do anything to help me get better. They have already done so much, not least listening to me being such an asshole to so many people because I was basically tired, hungry and confused. They're actually coming by tomorrow, I can't really figure out if I should get an ice cream together with them. This isn't the usual crappy, processed ice cream but made on the spot with organic ingredients and no strange additives. I think I could handle it mentally, I'm just not sure if it's a good idea given my context and quantum biology.

### Hemming — 2013-07-10T15:49:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-89824>

Today I was reading CT 10 and together with some different parts from Jack's book I realised something. One day I said to one of my friends 'it annoys me a little that everyone thinks I'm weird' to which she replied 'that's what I've always liked about you, you go your own ways and don't care what everyone thinks'. My point with this is that to get better I don't want to follow the conventional wisdom and just do as everyone else because that is not an argument for doing something stupid.
This also came to when I asked a random hairdresser how my eczema looked and he said 'what eczema? Oh wait, maybe there is a little spot of something here but it's nothing'. So after cutting grains of all kinds completely I've cured my acne, eczema, allergy (I think) and the usual bloating - why the hell should I go back to eating when it is probably inflicting me with a leaky gut. Some of the other things I've cut out probably also contributed to it.

Bottom line, I want to follow my own path to optimal not just follow the usual, more or less, brain dead advice.

### Shijin13 — 2013-07-10T16:48:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-89828>

One day I said to one of my friends 'it annoys me a little that everyone thinks I'm weird' to which she replied 'that's what I've always liked about you, you go your own ways and don't care what everyone thinks'. My point with this is that to get better I don't want to follow the conventional wisdom and just do as everyone else because that is not an argument for doing something stupid.
> This also came to when I asked a random hairdresser how my eczema looked and he said 'what eczema? Oh wait, maybe there is a little spot of something here but it's nothing'. So after cutting grains of all kinds completely I've cured my acne, eczema, allergy (I think) and the usual bloating - why the hell should I go back to eating when it is probably inflicting me with a leaky gut. Some of the other things I've cut out probably also contributed to it.
>
> Bottom line**, I want to follow my own path to optimal not just follow the usual, more or less, brain dead advice**.

In my experience, this is when you really begin to see health improvements. N=1 is so important and many doctors & patients have forgotten how important... b/c they've been sold on the one size fits all menu of health care

### Hemming — 2013-07-10T16:56:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-89829>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=89828)
>
> In my experience, this is when you really begin to see health improvements. N=1 is so important and many doctors & patients have forgotten how important... b/c they've been sold on the one size fits all menu of health care

Yes, and even more in my situation because everything I say is being turned against me with doctors saying 'it's your eating disorder telling you not to eat grains' despite these improvements happened prior to the eating disorder. But if I tell the doctors now that I wont eat grains because I believe they cause a leaky gut they will just tell me that's the stupidest thing they've ever heard because we all know that whole grains are so nutritious.
My real problem with the treatment is that you're being told (between the lines) that you're so stupid that you can't handle even the most simple things so that they have to disempower you in every way almost to the point where you have to ask for permission to take a piss. I appreciate that some people need someone telling them to eat as they wont do it otherwise, but that's not my problem and having someone controlling my every move is not for me.

### caroline — 2013-07-11T06:35:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-89862>

I am so proud of you for standing up for yourself and being your own man!

I hope you have an icecream with your parents ..... Think of how stoked they would be! ..... And - you don't have to eat all of it this time.

Your parents - I am sure - have been to hell and back ...As have you....

You seem to be thinking pretty darn clearly to me......stick with your friend - she sounds like a winner!

Keep working on your masterpiece ..... It is a remarkable journey.

### Hemming — 2013-07-11T13:20:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-89871>

Caroline, thank you for your continued support.

I think I'm ready to try an ice cream. I realise that this fall into the epi paleo template and it's not something I plan on doing in the near future again but today is a special day. To me this is a giant leap, I know most people think 'an ice cream, so what? You're probably better off eating it than not but I completely agree with Jack and most people here in that 'good is the enemy of great' why settle for mediocre and just bend the rules all the time saying 'everything in moderation'. That has never been my style with anything. I remember while studying how I could simply not stop thinking about I didn't understand and I have an multiple occasions solved these problems while sleeping.

They have, especially dad has been worried, more than I realised at some point. He attended one of the session with my psychologist and I'm pretty sure he was close to crying when I explained my thoughts.

She is the best! We know each other extremely well and she is (and has been) and amazing support all along.

### Hemming — 2013-07-11T13:24:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-89872>

Today at the morning swim I noticed that I felt colder (and a little bit uncomfortable) than yesterday which surprised as the air temperature was higher. I'm thinking it could have something to do with that I didn't eat enough at breakfast as I definitely ate less and restricted it because I was thinking about the ice cream today. Yes, that's how an anorexic thinks.

Has anyone tried the same or any opinion on if my reasoning is correct?

### Dextery — 2013-07-11T15:47:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-89882>

Hemming,

Your thoughts can change how you express your anorexic thinking DNA. If you believe in your anorexic thoughts, that is what you will get is anorexic thoughts. I think you know that already. Quit being a victim of your thoughts.

[http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?7605-Are-you-a-victim-of-your-genes](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?7605-Are-you-a-victim-of-your-genes)

### Hemming — 2013-07-11T16:11:22+0300

Yeah, that's a very good point. Right now I'm thinking "fuck this, I'm having an ice cream".

### Hemming — 2013-07-12T09:04:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-89928>

Following on from my last post I did have the ice cream and I do believe that I'm going to live for more than a couple of days.
Dextery, thank you for reminding me of that thread!

That said, I now realise more than ever how true the statement from Jack in the abovementioned thread "We are not victims of our genes.........we are however, victims of our thoughts." and after reading CT 11 about Kevin is. Of all the help I can get it's ultimately myself who has to change my thoughts and want to get better. It's futile to ask 17 different people for help on this and that if I don't believe them or their methods anyway.
This is my own journey where I have to step up and get back to wanting to live optimally. Like many other, I ended up here by thinking that running 100k/week was the best medicine for any disease. Now I'm in the worst shape of my life feeling 20y older than I am being lethargic and weak (my blood work is fortunately not a complete mess) yet I still can't truly convince myself to let go of the anorexic thoughts and gain weight. Thinking like that is what I've always hated about so many people, especially in university when everyone would just quit as soon as it got a little bit tough.
I have to find back to my inner fighting spirit, the same as got me here in the first place, to get back to a meaningful live instead of being a victim of my own thoughts. I need to regain control of my live. No one is a slave if you keep your spirit high.

I'm not saying this is my big epiphany moment just that I need to get my own house in order before I can get better in any way.

### Hemming — 2013-07-12T09:15:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-89929>

I think this is rather interesting [http://politiken.dk/mad/tema/ECE201...rens-konger/?ref=nyhedsbrev\_politiken-madklub](http://politiken.dk/mad/tema/ECE2015405/opskrifter-spis-som-tourens-konger/?ref=nyhedsbrev_politiken-madklub) . It's an article from a Danish newspaper about they eat at the cycling team Saxo Bank Tinkoff during the tour and the chef's new cookbook. I saw a tv interview with her yesterday where she emphasised getting proteins, vegetables, no gluten (their reason being that it disrupts nutrient uptake in the gut), limited starches and good oils (she didn't mention which oils).
They obviously still gobble down energy bars like there is no tomorrow during the stages but it's an interesting step that it seems like many teams are pursuing. Yesterday Ben Greenfield mentioned that some riders drink MCT oil during the stages.

### caroline — 2013-07-12T09:26:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-89930>

You are a remarkable young man on a remarkable journey! I am so proud of you for having the icecream with your Mom and Dad .....How did they feel about it?

We all have to step up if we want to live optimally.

Dr kruse once told me, when I was questioning some people's horrible attitude etc........."not everyone makes it ...you don't understand that - but I do because I am a surgeon"

Dr. K. Has put a lot on the line for all of this - and taken a lot of abuse ......so I think we should do the same. It really is a win win situation for us,

What fun thing are you going to do tomorrow? Does your friend go swimming with you?

What gigantic massive plans do you have for your future??? I want you to live large......very, very large .....and I am not taking no for an answer!

### Hemming — 2013-07-12T17:35:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-89957>

I've just raised the tent in the garden to sleep there the next couple of nights. I'm looking forward to see if I can notice any difference in my sleep now that I'm outside in the countryside.

Today the morning swim was with dad near my parents place. The beach, water and weather was amazing! After the swim dad and I had a (surprisingly) good talk. For some reason he could tell that I would rather go home to my own apartment - so much for female intuition

Today hasn't been good wrt eating as I've been thinking too much about yesterday and compensating for it. I've been feeling confused and tired which I'm sure is related to this. I'm still in the process of convincing myself that I need to eat more.

Jack, I have a question for you regarding leptin in anorexic people. Is it simply the opposite of what happens in obese people, that the fat cells don't secrete enough of it and you thereby don't feel 'adequate' hunger?
I think you're one of the only people who even mentions anorexia together with obesity which is really interesting. Do you know of any other place where they specifically talk about leptin and anorexia?

Does anybody know if the MovNat in Spain [http://www.movnat.com/train-with-us/5-day-retreat-with-erwan-le-corre-in-spain/](http://www.movnat.com/train-with-us/5-day-retreat-with-erwan-le-corre-in-spain/) would be something that suits my situation?

### Hemming — 2013-07-12T22:31:36+0300

>

>
> What gigantic massive plans do you have for your future??? I want you to live large......very, very large .....and I am not taking no for an answer!

Thank you so much for the kind words.

My friend lives in London so we meet up at irregular intervals. I'm going to Paris with her.

I'm still thinking about the future on multiple accounts. My problem is exactly that I don't have these massive plans for the future which makes me feel like I have no purpose in life - that is what I'm trying to change at the moment.

### Dextery — 2013-07-12T23:39:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-89999>

Hemming,
If you have a Facebook page, find Jack Kruse there and friend him. Every morning & evening he posts some wisdom that I read every day. This was this morning's posting. I think you would benefit by reading his gems.

"All that we are is the result of what we have thought. You improve yourself by improving your thoughts. When your thoughts influence your behavior you become ready to lead. A great leader attracts great people and knows how to hold them together.
leadership actions inspire others to dream more, learn more, do more and become more. Leadership is lifting a person's vision to higher sights, the raising of a person's performance to a higher standard, the building of a personality beyond its normal limitations. A leader leaves behind him in other people , the conviction and the will to carry on. Leadership cannot really be taught. It can only be learned. Stop wishing life was easier, instead, make yourself better. Time is our equal opportunity employer. Take advantage of today because life is brief at its longest. Begin to think to influence you and others. Each of us, need all of us."

### caroline — 2013-07-13T11:57:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-90027>

> [Hemming said:](/goto/post?id=89993)
>
> Thank you so much for the kind words.
>
> My friend lives in London so we meet up at irregular intervals. I'm going to Paris with her.
>
> I'm still thinking about the future on multiple accounts. My problem is exactly that I don't have these massive plans for the future which makes me feel like I have no purpose in life - that is what I'm trying to change at the moment.

maybe - for now - just enjoy the present..... That is all we really have anyway. Enjoy your parents and the time you have with them....and enjoy meeting new people and chatting with complete stranger. Just suck life in, celebrate each day and stuff will happen.....
I think all I meant to say was ....live large ....do crazy, fun things ....be outrageous and maybe most important ....Laugh out loud - long and hard.
You don't need to be putting any more pressure on yourself - and anyway ...life has a way of taking unexpected twists and turns...just enjoy the ride - and have an ice cream cone every once in a while!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-07-13T14:44:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-90028>

Live at your boundaries because it will force you upward and forward in your transformation and it will direct you where you should be going from where you came.

### Hemming — 2013-07-14T15:27:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-90095>

@Caroline, it has been good spending some time with my parents but on the other hand I can also feel that it takes away some energy I should be spending on changing my mind. On the other hand, I can't really complain about living of their homegrown vegetables and my uncle's grass fed cattle

@Jack, thank you for the words. Right now my greatest boundary is to gain weight. I'm accepting it more and more. Dextery's post definitely made and impression on me. My concern is not about gaining weight as such, I'm scared that it will all end up as fat on my stomach. If I knew it would 10kg of muscle distributed across the body I would have done it a long time ago. I know it's impossible to happen like that but it's that uncertainty which makes me question if I really need to gain weight.
The other thing is that I want to continue eating real food, I don't doubt that I could easily gain back weight on a diet laden with cinnamon rolls and soda but that's not where I want to go. I've only had positive effects of cutting gluten so why should I start eating it now...

It's really strange for me to be asking other people if it's okay I gain weight. I've always been a very independent person with nobody telling me how to live my life and now I'm here seeking comfort in someone telling me I can eat as much as I like or that it would better for my health if I gained 10kg. I don't believe in calories in, calories out but I still think I'm just being gluttonous when I eat.

The same goes for my morning swims. Even though I feel great afterwards it has become a negative addiction because I feel like a lazy fat ass if I don't do it every day (and increasing the time in the water each time). I'm so chronically fatigued yet I don't allow myself to sit down and let all my thoughts go when there are so many I could be doing on various accounts. During the winter I turned off the heat completely to be freezing all the time and burn a little extra calories instead of trying to get better.

I'm not asking for pity just explaining how this can completely wreak havoc on your mind. Thinking like I'm doing now is the line of thought that I hate the most when I see it in other people indulging in self pity instead of stepping and doing something about it and now I'm here proclaiming par excellence - I can't believe how bad it's become. But sticking to these thoughts will never get me back in shape I need to step up and get my old mind back by changing my thoughts!

### Hemming — 2013-07-14T15:28:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-90096>

> [Dextery said:](/goto/post?id=89999)
>
> Hemming,
> If you have a Facebook page, find Jack Kruse there and friend him. Every morning & evening he posts some wisdom that I read every day. This was this morning's posting. I think you would benefit by reading his gems.

I follow Jack already and read those

### Hemming — 2013-07-15T17:19:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-90149>

I have a question for everyone.
I've experienced sudden sleepiness when getting warm in general. The most pronounced is definitely when I get back from my morning swim and go out on the balcony if the sun is out. If I sit down and read I basically fall asleep, 10min later I have no problem and it's not a problem for me to stay in the sun for a longer period.
Today I had a foot bath indoors which had the same effect but not to the same extent. My blood pressure is very low, borderline hypotension.

Does anyone have an explanation for this?

### nonchalant — 2013-07-15T18:14:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-90156>

Hi Hemming. I've experienced the same effect. It is concerning, sometimes, to fall asleep in the blazing Texas sun. But I also don't sleep long enough for it to be a problem. I believe Dr. K told someone else that it was part of the healing process. I'm not sure if it is growth hormone or what. I know that when I spend large amounts of time away from the computer & TV, I also get sleepy at odd times.

### Hemming — 2013-07-16T21:51:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-90241>

Today has not been a particularly good day as I've been feeling tired and my mood has been poor. I know it's because I was out walking and biking in the forest and that I didn't eat enough. The eating is still difficult for me because as soon as I start to feel a little better I cut back on it which instantly results in this scenario. It also looks like I haven't gained any weight in the last two weeks.

### caroline — 2013-07-17T01:37:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-90250>

Tomorrow is another day......maybe try a different food that you haven't had before and enjoy it to the fullest ....
I wish I could give you a hug...

I wish I knew more to help. For me - a former binge eater (most of my life). The BAB was an enormous help - but it is scary because you are so scared of putting on weight.

I was so grateful to finally understand that it wasn't my weakness - that it was something out of my control. The BAB changed my life ....along with everything else here. It does take time however...Rome wasn't built in a day.

A year and a half into this and every once in awhile I realized that something else has changed - little things here and there that all add up to enormous progress.

I really wish that you would listen to the Q&A with Erwan and Jacks webinar ...there was so many things about this man that have stuck with me ....his serenity, his calmness, his intellect, his kindness, his joy of life and his big heart......Another man that is, no doubt, not perfect but the take home for me was (and is) huge.

I am reading a book he recommended ...The Cosmic serpent ....and loving it.

O

### Hemming — 2013-07-17T21:26:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-90287>

Caroline, thank you for the continued support.

It's getting better and better with understanding that I need to gain weight - I just need to be constantly reminded of it. The BAB is getting better too but I really need to think of it as a long term strategy towards getting my life back.

I remember Monday last week when one the nurses said I should gain 0.5kg/week. Today I weighed myself at 52.2kg which is basically unchanged. Now I've been offered weekly consultation with a psychologist and a dietitian - the dietitian is going to be a nightmare...

### Hemming — 2013-07-20T10:50:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-90425>

Yesterday I went to see a therapist who has a different approach to eating disorders. It was a very special experience and I feel different already. I've never realised that maybe I've been having post traumatic stress disorder for 16y after being home alone with my having a minor heart attack due to an irregular sinus rhythm.

Afterwards I wanted to get in the ocean and it was very windy at the beach. I thought that it could be cold getting in and out of the water but I experienced a warmth that I haven't done for a long time. The water almost felt too hot for me and when I got up and didn't want to put on a towel.
I couldn't stop thinking of 'how thoughts can change your DNA'. Without sounding theatrical, I think something changed within me at that point. Nothing is impossible if you have the will to do it.

### caroline — 2013-07-20T12:12:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-90426>

> I couldn't stop thinking of 'how thoughts can change your DNA'. Without sounding theatrical, I think something changed within me at that point. Nothing is impossible if you have the will to do it.

### Tones — 2013-07-20T16:13:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-90435>

With a 200g of carb a day limit I put weight very easily without put on much fat (visual and perception for reference as well as a tanita scale). Now, I compete in muay thai and grappling competitions so being lean is something I want to be and I want to minimize water cutting to make weight. I also train 30 (40 if you include walking) hours a week give or take.

Here's my journaled macro nutrients for those days:
100-200g carbs
100-150g protein
200-300g fats

I'd eat about 12,000-18,000 kj a day, with about a 10,000 kj upkeep and another 6,000-10,000 kj (based on perceived METS) weekdays, 500-3,000 kj weekends. Often I was under shooting by 1,000-3,000 kj as well.

I went from 72kg to 74kg in 2 weeks (none of being excess water/nutrition. I gain 1.4-1.9kg of that for a few extra days if I splurge). This wasn't exactly what I wanted though...

To loose I've been technically fasting (eating 8,000-10,000 kj under) and to maintain I'm about 5,000-8,000 kj under.

The more and more I experiment it's really based on feeling rather than number crunching.

To give insite about my body... As a child I never ate much and was skinny because of it. To fatten me up my mother gave me a lot of morning pancakes with honey and ice cream and pizza for dinner. Now days too much carbs (from grain, fruit, sugar) makes me feel like ill and has caused health issues so I've naturally stuck to a low carb diet. I've also always fasted for 12-24 hours after lot of food just because I wasn't hungry.

I've got photos of me at 69kg (72kg - 3kg water) pre keto diet, me in action at 72kg at the start of a Jack inspired diet and then the bigger, leaner me in action at 71.5 kg keto diet me from last week. Just let me know if you're interested and I'll post them up.

### Hemming — 2013-07-20T17:11:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-90439>

@Tones, thanks a lot for posting - especially your macro intake! It's quite interesting to get a feeling of your maintenance/gain intake and how it relates to your body composition. Right now my biggest problem is understanding/realising/accepting that I can (and should) eat more than I do. I eat way fewer carbs than you and I'm sure that is a big part of the reason why I haven't gained weight so reading your post makes me more relaxed about eating in general.

Please post the pictures! I think that could really give a kick in the right direction.

### Hemming — 2013-07-21T10:53:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-90499>

Something I've been thinking about for some time and that I would be very curious to hear if anyone has an answer to is why I get a kick from being hungry. Most people feel happy when they are full and satiated whereas I almost always feel happy when I know that I've eaten just to the low side. I like that 'pain' of always pushing myself instead of feeling better, happy and satiated. Every time I try to eat more I get this feeling and want to stop doing it - it's quite frustrating because I feel tired and irritable when I could be happy energised.

Is there some sort of mechanism in the brain that can explain this?

### Hemming — 2013-07-22T16:54:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-90563>

> [Hemming said:](/goto/post?id=90557)
>
> I have heard the latest podcast and I know there is a brain gut blog. I was just wondering if Jack or anyone can guide me to exact point where it is mentioned either in the blog or in a podcast.
>
> Edit: Sorry, I just realised there is an even newer podcast - I'll listen to it right now!

Just finished listening to the podcast and that was really interesting, I really like that Jack mentions anorexia together with overweight.

I guess for me it's just about continuing with the Leptin Rx. So far I can say that I haven't gained any weight but my body looks a lot better (at least I think so). It actually looks like I've put on muscle mass and that my body is more toned - I'm probably not the one to objectively speak about that though.
The really interesting things is that the look of my body (again, my personal account) started changing when I began ocean swimming each day together with eating more. One thing I noted in the podcast is that Jack mentioned that skinny people should eat more fat whereas he previously said to me that I should have a higher intake of carbs to put on weight, but for right now I don't really want to stress about that

I definitely have to look more into the dopamine issue as that is most likely also making me make bad decisions regarding not eating enough.

### caroline — 2013-07-23T02:28:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-90601>

I know you are worried about being too skinny - but - what would be your ideal weight?

I now weigh considerably less than I ever have .....when I was 6 or 7 kilos heavier, a very dear friend always worried I was getting too thin - now - 6 kilos lighter she thinks I look so healthy! and I certainly don't look too skinny. I have a nice layer of fat - in a good way and I look quite fit. I am only telling you this because I really don't understand what is happening. We view ourselves too subjectively I think.

Before when I would struggle to get my weight down, I would look very drawn. Now - with all the extra fat in my diet I just look and feel very healthy - my take anyway...

I guess it is somewhat because I have a small frame \[I never knew that before\] so now I am more compact?

### Hemming — 2013-07-23T17:23:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-90642>

@Caroline, I understand what you're saying - this is exactly what I'm trying to convince myself daily. I don't think I'm at my ideal weight now (I have no idea what that would be and I'm not sure it can be said by anyone) but weight gain has been demonised so much that I some way think that everyone will think I'm a gluttonous pig if I gain 10kg and that it will all be fat and not muscle.

I did some more reading of the blog and it was interesting to read about how zinc deficiency affects brain function (I knew some of it beforehand as my last showed my zinc is too low) on top of the fluoride issues. I've been very thirsty and urinating frequently for a long period and in some periods wanting coffee and salt. I also need to eat three times each day and get hungry before breakfast, lunch and dinner its not carb cravings its just hunger. My problem is convincing myself that I have chronic fatigue (I still feel tired when I wake up but not as much as previously), irritability and think about food constantly because I'm not eating enough. But because of my mental state I need somebody I really trust (like Jack, other people on the forum or family and close friends) to tell me that it's perfectly fine to eat until I'm satisfied.
Maybe some of these nutrient deficiencies also explain why I continue to prefer to be hungry instead of full, that feeling of punishing myself by being tired and starved makes me happy.

### caroline — 2013-07-24T03:13:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-90700>

It is more than okay to eat three meals a day. Can you stop thinking about your weight for awhile? You said you were seeing more muscle? That sounds like exactly what you want! Maybe you have a very small or narrow frame and what others are telling you isn't correct for you?

Have you thought about why you are punishing yourself?

Have you asked your friend ( the girl who has been there for you) what she thinks about your frame?

In my opinion (as far away from being an expert as you can get). I wish you could stop thinking about all of this for awhile. Just eat until full, swim, play in the sun, laugh, talk to strangers and hear their life story and then fall into bed exhausted and have a great sleep. You are young - you aren't working right now - so you should be having a blast!

Please don't squander this precious time you have....

I am going to keep asking you ....Have you listened to Erwan Lecorre and Jack's Q&A yet? I am not going to let you off the hook on this one......

### Inger — 2013-07-24T08:30:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-90713>

Great advice form Audrey/Caroline! Do not stress about weight, throw away the scale.. just do the good stuff.
I am a former bulemic too, and I do not weight anymore. I just learn to enjoy and love my body as it is. It feels very good. A little skinnier, a little chubbier.. if we do all the good stuff, our body will be exactly as it should!

I have been eating a bit chaotic last week because of visits and stressy stuff here.. I can loose my organized eating plan when it gets too much for me. I kind of sacrifized it just for the others, to eat with them on their meal times wich are different than mine, just on a few occasions. I did not eat any crap for sure, but stiuff like.. way too much fried meat from the grill... some HWC.. a bit too much coffee.. a little too late eating, eating when no hunger.. and this fear come in my mind.. oh no what if I gain now... gosh I am getting fat! But Now I checked me naked in the mirror after everyone was gone, and everything is just fine, a beautiful, nice curvy women I could see..lol Not perfect for sure, but totally loveable.  When my mental state is not the best that is when I get this bad body image. It totally ties together. So I know what to work on then.

Life is just of too much value to spend it to think about my weight. There is so much to do... so much to live, so much to learn!

### Tones — 2013-07-25T18:11:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-90814>

> [Hemming said:](/goto/post?id=90499)
>
> snip

The majority of my carbs was from the sugar and lactose in the sustagen I was taking twice a day (39g carbs per serving, 78g total) and the lactose milk and natural yogurt I consume (~8 grams per serving 4-6 servings of milk 1-2 servings of yogurt. 5 or 8 \* 8g is 40-70 total). Also note that I take one pill of HealthWise LactoBOOST with each serving of milk or sustagen.

So 118g-148g of crabs came from...
2 glasses of milk throughout the day
2 sustagen with a serving of powdered milk (kept a shaker). One asap after midday training session, one after I get home from training (about an hour and a half from high intensity training).

One thing I've noticed from switching to keto, I get that hungry gut rumbling feeling after I eat something carbie.

Hemming, it's a good thing you can stand to eat so little, you can go through what a lot of people struggle to do. You can try experimenting to see how your body reactions in different stages. Personally I've noticed a massive clearance in my thinking and eating little has benefited my Yoga (asana) practice. Other than I've learnt how my body reacts to different foods. Another thing as well, I now look forward to my next big day of eating and then the next day of fasting. This Saturday I've got a German feast at hand =).

Over the last 6 weeks I have been eating one massive meal a day, just to see if I could do it. The answer is yes but the only things I have tweaked is... eat the majority of proteins at night to stop sluggishness after my big meal, drink half or a full glass of milk to keep some proteins in my gut (and/or aminos in the blood) while I train. Just keep in mind I train 5-6 hours most days. Other advice is to cook mainly with Ghee (the state beyond clarified butter, Indian grocers have it for cheap... Although I get mine from an Arab grocer) because it's extremely easy to digest and which would of kept me from feel sluggish.

Here are some photos

About a month before I switch diets. It's a photo of the weigh in (69kg from 72kg). [https://fbcdn-sphotos-e-a.akamaihd....720x720/45283\_553105801396798\_792465324\_n.jpg](https://fbcdn-sphotos-e-a.akamaihd.net/hphotos-ak-prn1/s720x720/45283_553105801396798_792465324_n.jpg)

The bigger but slimmer more keto me. The night before at the weigh in was at 67.8kg, by the fight I was between 71.5kg and 72kg. [https://fbcdn-sphotos-b-a.akamaihd....0x720/1005300\_561461953900142\_415302480\_n.jpg](https://fbcdn-sphotos-b-a.akamaihd.net/hphotos-ak-frc1/q71/s720x720/1005300_561461953900142_415302480_n.jpg)
[https://fbcdn-sphotos-c-a.akamaihd....x720/1069807\_561462213900116\_1744517541\_n.jpg](https://fbcdn-sphotos-c-a.akamaihd.net/hphotos-ak-frc3/q71/s720x720/1069807_561462213900116_1744517541_n.jpg)

I'll PM you the link to the albums.

> Hemming said:
>
> Every time I try to eat more I get this feeling and want to stop doing it - it's quite frustrating because I feel tired and irritable when I could be happy energized.
>
> Is there some sort of mechanism in the brain that can explain this?

What do you actually eat? I've had quite a bit of trouble with my gut in my early adulthood and the solution so far hasn't been eat more... It got to the point where I was assumed lactose intolerant and these days I eat a lot of dairy, even before I was taking the lactoferren and colostrum supplement. Speaking of which, how old are you?

Even if you cannot see it maybe now you are doing great Hemming. Eating three times a day is perfectly ok and actually required at this stage for your health. I loved when you told about eating icecream with your parents.I would not change anything except hide the scale. Joy is in the small details of each day. Wishing you lots of details.

### caroline — 2013-07-26T04:52:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-90885>

### Hemming — 2013-07-26T09:13:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-90894>

@caroline, I have thought about that and I was at a private therapist yesterday where we made some great progress. The idea is based on existentialism where I have to make the decision to get better myself, which I agree with. I think about the entire situation differently now, its obviously not over just in one day but I feel different. The last couple of days I've been eating more for breakfast as I've been more hungry too. At the moment I just feel like I have an insatiable appetite for most things, it has been easier for me to eat more and as Jack said earlier its a good sign that I feel hungry now. I still have to say to myself that I should just eat big but that again goes back to choosing life instead of being tied down by some stupid rules that stops me from living life to the fullest. I've noticed that I like salty things and coffee though which I'm not that happy about. Regarding the salt I've been feeling a little dizzy and quite tired/fatigued in the warm weather so I can't really figure out if the salt cravings is simply my body saying that it needs salt. besides that, I can eat an entire coconut each day at the moment

I have an ectomorph body so yes, small frame.

Thank you for reminding me that I should be out enjoying life. That was another thing we talked about yesterday, why I continue to reject so many other people, I think I now understand why.

Hehe, I thought I had already answered that  I have not heard the Q&A and i believe there is a cost to it.

@Inger, thank you for posting! Its some really good points and something that I just haven't realised fully before because I've always been such a binary person thinking that I had to do everything correct all the time otherwise it would all be useless. I don't think my weight is healthy or optimal here, it has just become a stupid marker I can easily follow each day. I'm becoming more and more detached to it though.

@Tones, I have to agree with the others. I don't get the things you eat. I eat very much according to the Epi-paleo diet with no 'cheating'. I don't disregard your results as I have to say that your body does look better now. I'm 26y.

@Lahelada, the ice cream was quite a step for me. It will not be the last time this summer. Mom and dad really enjoyed having it with me too  Thank you for the support!

### caroline — 2013-07-26T11:21:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-90898>

Yes - there is a cost for the webinars ....but if you can manage it ....if you are a gold member the webinars and Q&A are included. Please think of it as investment in your health and your future ...what could be more important?

The way I look at it \[at the risk of boring you to tears\] Jack has made a huge investment of his time in all of this - to help all of us on our journey to optimal health. This whole website and forum must be costing a fortune to keep up and running. He isn't really selling anything to help defray the costs that other websites - like MDA and Dave Asprey etc.do.

I figure the least I can do - is contribute something.....I am getting such an extraordinary education on so many levels....and I could never repay or even quantify what all this has done for me....

just my 2 cents .....

### caroline — 2013-07-26T11:24:12+0300

### Lahelada — 2013-07-26T14:18:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-90905>

Thank you. It has indeed been a very good weekend. My uncle had his 70y birthday and I helped with a lot of the preparations and it was great seeing family and friends again.

After the last session with my therapist and a really good talk with mom and dad I feel a lot better. I now feel that I really want to get better.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-07-28T15:52:21+0300

### Hemming — 2013-07-28T17:20:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-91038>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=91032)
>
> such good news! Have you done anything crazy and uninhibited and silly today?

Not today. But I would say that the talk I had with mom and dad was something that really changed a lot for me. It wasn't like we were sitting there crying our hearts but we got through a lot of things from the past and discussed them in the most positive way. its difficult to explain all of it - it just relieved my mind of so many things, I think for mom and dad too.
In terms of more 'hands on things' my brother and I slept in the tent at my parent's place after the birthday - just like in the good old days

In general I feel like I'm ready to move on now. I still have some barriers to overcome in terms of accepting that I should gain weight but I don't want to stress out about that, its going in the right direction mentally. Focusing on the BAB has also my mood and cravings. I really like the food so its kind of a win-win situation
