### Hemming — 2013-07-29T09:28:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-91072>

It has improved tremendously, both because of my therapist and the people here on the forum. I'm detaching more and more from it, I feel better and can concentrate on reading, talking to people being focused in general so what's not to like.

### Hemming — 2013-08-03T09:29:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-91413>

Sorry I haven't been posting for some days but I've been out and about.

I've started eating more for all three meals and quickly noticed a higher energy level and my hunger has decreased. Just yesterday evening I was sitting outside in my shorts not feeling cold while everyone else was putting on t-shirts and shirts. The cold showers doesn't feel cold as such anymore, now I have to have my head under the cold water to get the same effect, somewhat similar to face dunking. I came to wonder what activates and recruits the most BAT, having the cold water run on your neck, back and stomach or on your face/head. It feels colder to have it on my head but will the cold receptors signal to the rest of the body to activate the BAT?

My weight hasn't changed but I continue to think that my body overall looks better, especially my legs which I think is the edema going away. I did some push-ups, planks and other body weight exercise after a cold shower late afternoon and I was really surprised of how well I performed. It wasn't like the good old days but something has definitely improved whether its because of the higher food intake or CT.

### caroline — 2013-08-03T10:40:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-91414>

You are doing great! What crazyness do you have planned for tomorrow???

### Hemming — 2013-08-10T15:16:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-92018>

I haven't posted for several because I just got home from Paris - a trip that made me realise some things. Most importantly that life is much more fun when you have energy which basically boils down to eating more, that I have a truly amazing friend because she happily accepted my idea of Paris even though I've been acting sub optimal (to say the least) in different ways, that a book store can be more interesting than Notre Dame and the Eiffel Tower and that the French restaurants (even the fancy ones) are very generous with their portion sizes.

The bottom line is that I'm more and more getting back to my own true self. I've realised that eating is much better than starving myself. I know it sounds simple but once your brain is fried you need to do a lot of work to get it rewired. I honestly believe that eating, sleeping and living according to a lot of the principles laid out by Jack in his blogs is a big factor for my recovery. I can now see how some people destroy them self by making a lot of bad choices without realising that their illness is because they're so out of sync.

### Lahelada — 2013-08-10T15:29:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-92020>

It sounds like a wonderful trip and I absolutely understand how a book shop can be more interesting than Notre Dame. I had the good fortune to work there for three months and I have nothing, but good memories from that. I like the French,with all their idiosyncrasies.
You sound like you have turned into a great electrician, excellent rewiring job.

### Hemming — 2013-08-10T16:13:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-92021>

I completely agree with Paris, I found it so much more relaxing and nice than London (maybe because I always come to London for work) - I was very positively surprised. The only thing that annoys is me is that French is not good enough to talk to them and English is not exactly their forte.

Haha, that's a good analogy!

### Hemming — 2013-08-11T22:29:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-92150>

### nonchalant — 2013-08-11T23:36:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-92159>

Tripe is a year around thing in Texas, with our large Mexican population. Menudo is a soup made with tripe that is very popular, and is also a morning-after tradition (hangover treatment). Tripe is cow stomach.

### caroline — 2013-08-12T01:26:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-92178>

### Hemming — 2013-08-12T13:17:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-92225>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=92159)
>
> Tripe is a year around thing in Texas, with our large Mexican population. Menudo is a soup made with tripe that is very popular, and is also a morning-after tradition (hangover treatment). Tripe is cow stomach.

I had no idea that tripe was common in Texas. Is it possible to buy it at most butchers? Speaking of Texas, I've always wanted to go there to see a big ranch, the open ranges and shoot a .50 cal rifle. Can you recommend any specific places for that? Where do you live in Texas?

### nonchalant — 2013-08-12T19:21:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-92240>

You would be able to find tripe at about 50-75% of most grocery stores, and 100% of the Mexican stores. Most shops that are only butcher shops wouldn't carry it, they are too small. Most Mexican restaurants (which are very popular here) carry menudo, if they provide breakfast.

Heh, I haven't shot anything so large, but I have shot a shotgun. That was big enough for me! You can shoot on any land you own, if it's out in the country. The police here in Dallas would frown on such activity, though. ;-) Or you can lay money down and shoot on an indoor range. I once shot a pistol in a sand dune area of a beach. <-- Fond childhood memory.

### RickD — 2013-08-14T21:09:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-92540>

Wow A Dane that both likes and is familiar with guns - I'm impressed. One thing I really got tired of hearing when I lived in DK was how evil guns were. After a few years of it I said enough was enough and took my team to a gun range. It was very entertaining to watch them go from being afraid to touch the gun to those first tentative shots and then finally enjoyment at killing some paper.

On TX the State is huge and is very diverse. Mts., Forests, Open prairie, deserts and beaches it has it all. Enjoy your visit

### Hemming — 2013-08-14T21:26:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-92543>

I've been wanting to do an update on my overall situation for a couple of days as there some things which have improved and some where I still feel that there is some way to go.

1\. I still think that my energy level overall is too low. My legs often feel like I've ran a marathon and I have that procrastination to a lot of small daily things

2\. I have a huge appetite, especially in the morning. I don't crave sweets of any kind. I don't count calories but I feel like I'm eating tons of food very strictly according to the Epi-paleo diet (except that I do eat a little cheese a couple of times each week but only made ones made with raw milk). I've added more carbs lately as I can get homegrown e.g. tomatoes and apples

3\. My body does look better if I have to say so myself despite no or very little weight gain. Especially my legs have improved noticeably for other than me, that change came after 1-1Â½ week on the BAB. I still really can't figure out how important it is for me to gain weight to get more energy and feel better - I think that is my biggest hurdle in terms of why I haven't gained weight. Could it be that my problem is the reverse of the overweight - I'm not gaining weight because my field, clock and hormones are also out of sync? Or do I need to eat even more?

4\. I've adapted pretty quickly to swimming in cold water and taking cold showers where I definitely have a higher tolerance than the majority. On the other hand I often feel cold when I walk around or sit at home or outside

5\. My mind has changed a lot since I started therapy but I still have some things I struggle with like exercise addiction.

That was some quick points about the situation. Obviously a lot more can be say said and I would be happy to elaborate on anything if anyone has any comments or questions.

### Hemming — 2013-08-14T21:35:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-92544>

After a few years of it I said enough was enough and took my team to a gun range. It was very entertaining to watch them go from being afraid to touch the gun to those first tentative shots and then finally enjoyment at killing some paper.
>
> On TX the State is huge and is very diverse. Mts., Forests, Open prairie, deserts and beaches it has it all. Enjoy your visit

Haha, yes generally people do seem to have an aversion towards guns. I know a guy pretty well who sells guns so he has a very impressive selection of both new and vintage guns in all shapes and sizes. The idea is that even though there is no real market for most of his weapons people has to have to opportunity to try before they buy it so we rent a day with at the shooting range where he brings the lot and we have fun.
I remember that feeling of extreme respect when you're just about to fire the gun. The first time I shot a Desert Eagle it probably took me 10sec to pull the trigger because I was afraid the recoil

I haven't planned anything yet, I would just like to go there to see the open prairie, deserts and hopefully a very big ranch and the cattle there. Something I would really like to do is live/work on a ranch for a shorter period and help out with everyday work and experience the life in a place like that.

### caroline — 2013-08-15T03:30:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-92582>

I love your plans and dreams too! So you want to be a cowboy eh???? Are you gaining more muscle but your body doesn't look any bigger? maybe you are not far off your ideal weight? What does your intuition tell you?

### Hemming — 2013-08-19T15:51:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-93101>

The last week has been quite busy in the best possible way. I've spent time with friends and family and for the first time in close to one year I've laughed like in the good old days.

Today I'm back at my own place and have done a morning swim in the ocean where the water has now cooled to around 17-18 degrees and I found out that I can tolerate it much better now. My arms look bigger and better, I would still like a little more definition on my stomach on the other hand, I've lost a lot of muscle on it that has to be rebuilt. That's the good part.

I'm not sure if its tiredness from the previous week or that I haven't eaten enough but last night I slept 10 hours with ease and today I've been so extremely hungry that i can not remember I've ever eaten so much before and i still feel like I could eat more. Is this still a case of giving in to my appetite and just eat all of the protein and fat that I can or is this actually a sign that something bad is going on? I realise that it could simply be because of not eating enough during the weekend that caused this. I'm getting a little scared that I'm developing an unhealthy relationship with food given my huge appetite even though I haven't gained much weight - if any. Today I also find it difficult to concentrate and think about food almost all the time.

### Hemming — 2013-08-19T15:53:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-93102>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=92582)
>
> I love your plans and dreams too! So you want to be a cowboy eh???? Are you gaining more muscle but your body doesn't look any bigger? maybe you are not far off your ideal weight? What does your intuition tell you?

I just want to try it out and see something very different from what I'm used to  My weight this morning was 53.3kg at 177cm, I still think that's a little low, I'm just surprised at how much I seem to eat and have been doing for at least one month. But I guess there is a lot of repair going on that takes the energy and I still can't skip lunch if I have breakfast early in the morning.

### caroline — 2013-08-20T01:35:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-93157>

You seem to be doing really well ... keep busy.... and, of course, keep laughing!
Why not just eat until full for now .... and see how it feels? What is your intuition telling you? Are you eating lots of good fat?

Your body and brain must be needing to do a lot of repair work ........so it needs as much fuel as possible. Do you have an accurate take on how much food is too much?

We had a restaurant for many years - and I have seen very thin people eat enormous amounts of food and obese people eat hardly anything.....not one size fits all for sure....

### Hemming — 2013-08-21T16:45:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-93350>

Today I thought I wanted to try eating two times and see how I reacted. I had a BAB which I finished around 0800, did some shopping, some reading, went to the beach for a swim, more reading, picked up a package, more reading and here I am posting. I start to feel a little hungry but not in the sense that I need something right now. I'm thinking of starting to prepare dinner in 1.5h from now. The reason for 'trying' this was simply because I thought of the hassle with having to think about lunch and preparing - its much easier just eating two times each day.
The only thing I worry about is eating enough at these two meals when I do for a longer period. I've felt fine during the day but have had some issues concentrating which kind of indicates to me that I should eaten a little more at breakfast. My energy has been fine and the heavy/sore legs that I often experience hasn't been an issue. When I cycled to the post office I had no feeling of not having eaten for 7-8h.

I'm still in the process of learning how much I need to eat to be happy and energetic although it gets easier to convince myself to eat more when I feel better. The problem of eating 'enough' is that feel guilty when I just go all out eating even though I still have some cravings/desire to eat. That's also why I find it difficult to eat with other people because I think they look at me as a gluttonous pig if I really eat as much as I can.

@caroline, one size definitely doesn't fit all. I'm not saying that how I think is a good way - that's why I'm here trying to rewire my brain.

### Hemming — 2013-08-22T21:58:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-93424>

I've gained weight over the last couple of weeks and while I can see that some parts of my body look better I think that my stomach looks more bloated/fat now. I've also noticed that I almost every day get swellings in my lower legs and feet only after being up around 6h. The good thing is that they don't feel so damn sore as they used to do.

As I said earlier, I've started going all in the BAB and eating two times each day. I'm really surprised at how much I can eat at breakfast and it honestly scares me a little. My concern is that I've messed up my appetite regulation so much that my brain is thinking that food is around so I need to eat as much as possible and its demanding all the extra energy to put on fat. Is this simply a temporary sign of a low leptin level that will return to normal once my weight is back to normal?
I'm not too happy about the appetite part as gaining weight is still an issue for me. I was hoping that eating this way would give me a better control of my appetite and cravings but I still find that I have some which make me lose my concentration and procrastinate.

### Lahelada — 2013-08-22T23:28:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-93440>

hello Hemming,

I would imagine that your body is currently bunkering water like crazy as it is unused to your current way of eating.
For me the beauty of this is that the body takes care of itself and asks for what it needs ,no deliberate brain control if you will. If you read in my diary I was also concerned about my appetite increase with the advent of CT and not losing. Well,my appetite has fallen off again so I am sure that with you at the other end of the spectrum it will do the same once it reaches a comfortable balance. .

### Hemming — 2013-08-23T09:30:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-93478>

@Lahelada, yeah. After reading a bit on leptin yesterday (inspired by Trinity's new blog) I found a number of studies on people with AN having very low leptin levels which returned to normal when they regained weight. My take is that my appetite comes from the low leptin levels whereas an overweight's appetite comes from leptin resistance.
I also think that some of it is fluid retention - I've started eating more salt along with more food in general. It's not like I've gained massively, I'm around 54kg now.

### Hemming — 2013-08-24T09:26:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-93589>

After reading some of CT-6 and the post about fish oil I came to think that my recent weight and fat gain could be explained by my daily swims and cold apartment together with a diet high in coconuts and coconut oil. My thinking is simply that my body thinks its winter and I'm eating coconuts which are are found in the summer. I've also been pretty big on seafood and avoiding omega-6 which might also affect this, but that is pure speculation as I haven't tested my tissue ratio.
I'll switch to more butter and lard instead of coconut oil and olive oil.

If anyone has any experience or thoughts about this I would very much like to know!

### prAna303 — 2013-08-24T09:36:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-93590>

> [Hemming said:](/goto/post?id=93589)
>
> My thinking is simply that my body thinks its winter and I'm eating coconuts which are are found in the summer. If anyone has any experience or thoughts about this I would very much like to know!

Since i live in Sweden, not that far away from you i have not seen coconuts in the nature around me, i think you are doing the right thing. Simply look around you what kind of natural fat you would find, fish, clarified butter and so on. If unsure, read up on your local food history, what the people used to eat in the nordic countries.

### Hemming — 2013-08-26T10:07:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-93803>

I'm starting to think that my almost constant hunger, lack of energy and irritability is because I don't eat enough fat to fuel my body. Going low fat and low carb doesn't really work. I'm thinking that I'm not producing enough ketones while not eating enough carbs to keep my brain happy. Hopefully this will change how I feel in general.

I've bought a pair of Gunnar blue light blocking glasses. Do you also use them when reading and have the lights on?

### caroline — 2013-08-26T10:21:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-93804>

going low fat is not a good idea ....this is meant to be high fat, medium protein, low carb ....

When the sun sets..... wear your blue blockers if you are on your computer \[all the time when on computer\] or TV or f you have lites on. Turn off lites when you can. Most of us try not to turn lites on after sunset - use candles ...or star light!!

sit outside and have a glass or red wine and look at the sky, stars etc. ..... barefooted.....enjoy....especially with someone you love......

### caroline — 2013-08-26T10:25:31+0300

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-93808>

@caroline, objectively I know about the high fat - its more about convincing myself about that I need energy from something. No reason for you to be sorry about that

I was planning on wearing the glasses all the time when I'm on the computer (also at work). I don't have a TV and don't plan on getting one. I have been using the natural light all spring and summer and i can't really remember the last time I turned on the light in my apartment.

I have been sitting a lot outside reading with my bare feet in the grass. The last couple of nights I've been sleeping in a tent and, I might be biased, but I would say that I can feel my energy is better and I wake up refreshed looking at the sun. I guess this is the same effect people experience with the magnet sleeping pads.

### Hemming — 2013-08-27T09:28:55+0300

### Hemming — 2013-08-27T12:40:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-93893>

I'm thinking of taking probiotics for my infrequent bowel movements. Can any recommend a specific product or other ways to help this? I already eat fermented foods and drink bone broth.

### cantweight — 2013-08-27T16:20:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-93900>

My family has had the best results with raw milk....if you have it available try that as your probiotic source.

We took probiotics for years! 3lac, 5lac, Theralac, Saccharomyces Boularrdi, and Dr Ohira professional strength were our favorite brands but Ive never seen results from any of those like we are seeing from raw milk. I have a son that struggles with being under weight and one of the first things he noticed was a bowel movement change (all of my kids and husband too). They are twice a day with soft stool....this was never the norm for them no matter how much diet and supplementing we did.

My underweight son is also showing quite a growth spurt. He is 11, maybe it was going to happen anyway, but a few months of raw milk and I am seeing big changes.

### nonchalant — 2013-08-27T16:33:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-93901>

Good idea, Cantweight. My DH has also had some great results using raw milk. He had infrequent bowel movements for decades. (He thought it was normal to "go" just a couple times per week.) I also make sure he drinks lots of non-fluoridated drinks, so he is less dehydrated.

### Hemming — 2013-08-29T22:12:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-94243>

During the last couple of days I've experienced an energy and muscle strength that I almost can't remember I've had. I've not really been hungry and been able to go 16h without feeling hungry. So what did I do? I simply started eating more fat, slightly reduced my carb intake and focused on seasonal food including home grown vegetables/berries.
I think that this just shows that its about keeping things simple. To quote a famous person 'when you know better, you do better'.

### prAna303 — 2013-08-31T08:58:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-94447>

> [Hemming said:](/goto/post?id=94341)

> Yeah, its really great. My rant about my body a couple of days ago was also mainly due to bloating which 'shocked' me.
>
> I still need to improve my bowel movements and that one I'm not really sure how to approach as I've already tried a lot of the things already suggested in this thread and the blog.

Perhaps it is the sync with the season you are now feeling. When we started to use tools to preserve foods we got lost of the period of "dog days" in august. I see the berries getting bad and taste really nasty soon, everything in nature starts it period of getting rotten. When we are in sync we should also change gut bacteria and i think this period is very important, this is when you should skip all the nice carbs because they are dying and start to eat fresh seafoods and fat AND start to get cold in a big way.

### prAna303 — 2013-08-31T15:44:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-94469>

For me it goes something like this:

\- Seafood, alot.
\- Dried reindeer and moose, sometimes smoked, from time to time.
\- Fat, from above animals most. Perhaps some butter, alot.

If i eat carbs, i pay for it and sometimes i do but you can count it on your fingers. Nuts sometimes but not much. Since i am out in the wild for about six hours / day i need fat in the winter, has always been a golden rule amongst the swedish lapps.

### Hemming — 2013-08-31T16:49:54+0300

Peter I am proud of you. I love how you eat! I need to tighten up my diet, and will, as I get organized here. I have had way too much 85% chocolate, and HWC and nuts. Makes my skin dry and not so great. But it is because I have really has issues with organizing my food supply as I live in chaos right now. I was fishing with Optimalbound today, first time,
he has a very nice boat.... and we got SO much fish. 4 bags of mackerel... and other fishes too. It was CRAZY. He has promised me we will go fishing again

(i was able to get a computer at work... so I can finally write something. I sit and watch over a ALS patient that cannot move anything else than he's eyes, not breath, nothing. I need to do many weird procedures with him it is all new and pretty hard for me. heavy. I am so exhausted. I have been working so much, and also too late. But it is only until I get my own place, then I will work only as much as I need, nothing more)

We was in the ocean CTing too... it was not cold water but the waves was huge.. holy cow I was so scared but Optimalbound was not and so I followed him and in he end it was so much fun...!!! I am so exited to have a CT friend, and a fishing buddy too!
Ok I cannot write too long posts as I might have to help the patient every second....

### Hemming — 2013-08-31T17:01:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-94476>

I actually think its rather easy to eat seasonally because you basically just eat whatever you can find outside. This morning I picked some of the last strawberries of the summer (they taste amazing this of year), later my uncle came by with some of the last cucumbers and dad picked some tomatoes.
Its just about keeping things simple.

### prAna303 — 2013-08-31T21:10:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-94505>

> [Hemming said:](/goto/post?id=94471)
>
> Thanks, its quite interesting to hear from someone who lives relatively close to myself.
>
> Do you work outside in Lapland? When you say 'perhaps some butter, a lot' do you mean that you occasionally butter and when you do you eat a lot of it? Or do you mean that you a lot of butter throughout the winter?
> Do you also only eat between sunrise and sunset?
>
> Somewhat related. Is it possible to go to Lapland for hiking?

I live in the middle of Sweden, that means four real seasons with up to -30C in winter and up to +30C in summer. Very much changing
amount of light in days, winter just some hours and summer not a really dark night. I DO not work in the wild, i spend my time in the wild. With that i mean hiking, biking, running, climbing and freediving. I have all natural elements around me just five minutes in each direction.

With the fat part like butter i eat butter everyday IN the winter, starting about now. I mean the mix of natural animal fat and butter, depending on whats available that day. I do not plan my eating, i find it each day.

The window i use for food is only daytime, sometimes i do eat later but that is only in company with others where we share stories, the food is just a good way to store up energies when it is used in good company... That does not ruin my world because i can compensate it by the way i live in large. I think eating in good company will always put us in a good state.

The northern of our country is famous for the hiking, you can go everywhere and find a nice hike. Check this blog for some ideas.

[http://patagoniandreams.com/2012/09/25/lappland-a-summers-long-ramble-through-europes-last-wilderness/](http://patagoniandreams.com/2012/09/25/lappland-a-summers-long-ramble-through-europes-last-wilderness/)

[http://patagoniandreams.com/2012/04/24/snow-flapping-through-sarek/](http://patagoniandreams.com/2012/04/24/snow-flapping-through-sarek/)

This picture from above blog is really catching the beauty of winter, the cold, the so clean nature without all the green and noice in summer. When i look at this i long for it, the thing with me is that i always felt like i have my greatest power in this season. I can be because so many complain about it and stay inside, i light up like a christmas tree in the winters.

[http://farm9.staticflickr.com/8022/7103973751\_ecdb74387d\_c.jpg](http://farm9.staticflickr.com/8022/7103973751_ecdb74387d_c.jpg)

What do you work with now if you have most of the day time off?

### prAna303 — 2013-08-31T22:48:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-94511>

Ok, this one is a tough one...

It is easier to explain it in a place like this than in real life because many think it is somewhat annoying for many. With my lifestyle you are going to step on many toes, break rules and be considered a slacker.

For me today work must be a special passion, i been in many positions thru life, made money and burnt money. I have tried it all one can say, had money like grass and nowdays, borderline to poor.

I "work" for about 40 hours / month. With "customers" with sports mainly as passions. I help people with gait problems and bad posture. Mixed with this i do some speeches, mostly to the sporty side of the community but also with sick / ill persons with pains in the bodies. When i talk to the ones that consider themself healthy i charge them but the sick / ill ones i do it for free.

This puts me in the position that i decide over my life, not a manager above me. Combine this with cutting all extra costs i have only rent, some electricity and food to pay for... With all the free time i always can make choices that do not harm me, if i would work more i must cut corners and make brute force on my soul.

When i talk about this live many can at first be very interested but after a while when they see it is not a trend for a month only many get restricted and almost insulted of this unbound lifestyle. The thing i felt is that women tend to like it more than men so i do not complain.

Like so many other things in life one must always sit down and think about how we see work today, why be a slave? We live in a time where money is everywhere in quite big amounts BUT how much do YOU really need?

### Hemming — 2013-09-02T12:28:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-94670>

That sounds fascinating! Where exactly in Sweden do you live? Coincidentally, before you wrote that post I started thinking if I have the right job, live in the right place etc. I really admire the minimalistic way of live and would in some way like to get rid of most of the neolithic lifestyle. I make more a lot more than the average person here in Denmark but have only found money to be extremely boring. Yes, they do give some freedom but they certainly don't provide happiness or good health.

I've recently thought about buying a smaller farm to start breeding cattle and simply do as you - live simple and enjoy mother nature. Luckily, I have a lot of freedom in my working hours but I'm still confined to sitting in an office wearing a shirt. I grew up near forests, the beach and fresh air so living in Copenhagen is still somewhat of a contrast even though I've been doing that for four years now.

### prAna303 — 2013-09-02T14:04:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-94672>

You pinpoint some things that i feel many get wrong today...

Minimalism is just another fashion i belive, it for sure touches some parts of natural life but much to often i see that it goes to the perverse side of obsession. Minimalism for me is the ability to not get stuck in feelings, thoughts and probably everything. With that i mean also that you always think again over the things you used to belive was true. Minimalism and open mind goes hand in hand...

The other thing is when you decide to do change, always look what you came from, not just the you person but the history of your tribe. Go back and study your family records. Just by changing your environment without a thought behind can for sure make things better, for a short period and soon you feel lost again. You must connect with your DNA and from the blueprint inside you, make action.

This is MY way of thinking so everyone else must start their own discussion with themself and people nearby to find their way. I think me make it more complicated that it should be because we listen to everyone else...

>

Just by changing your environment without a thought behind can for sure make things better, for a short period and soon you feel lost again. You must connect with your DNA and from the blueprint inside you, make action.
>
> This is MY way of thinking so everyone else must start their own discussion with themself and people nearby to find their way. I think me make it more complicated that it should be because we listen to everyone else...

Peter you're always so insightful. This is what's hard How do you balance the blueprint from where you come from, with those of your internal family, when it doesn't quite match.

looking at all options, and identifying options such as access to fresh water, proximity to the ocean, sunlight, access to COLD, while living at sea level. the best place for me - is probably not ideal for long term survival - why? b/c of the lack of access to 3 of those five things.

as jack has said multiple times, you can make cold... you can't make fresh water, and sunlight. Its really re-focused our potential relocation options. While some are not ideal for where we'd like to live - we are looking at all options, which may include the ability to be self-sufficient for food.

When I look at my haplogroup is H6 - "Most branches of haplogroup H, the parent of H6, originated in the Near East and then expanded throughout Europe after the peak of the Ice Age. But H6 was a relatively ancient offshoot of H that arose about 30,000 years ago, before the Ice Age's peak, and moved east into central Asia. Today it is most widespread there, although fairly recent migrations have brought H6 into western Europe over the last few thousand years.

Haplogroup: H6, a subgroup of H
Age: greater than 30,000 years
Region: Urals, Arabia, Europe
Example Populations: Czechs, French
Highlight: The range of H6 extends of more than 3,600 miles across Eurasia."

each of us must balance what works for us given our current field, and where our mitochondria originated in the field. Its a delicate balance - H6- explains why I always feel amazing when I get to the high desert step of Idaho - its very similar to the Mongolian plains my mitochondria originated on...

### prAna303 — 2013-09-02T19:41:02+0300

> This is what's hard How do you balance the blueprint from where you come from, with those of your internal family, when it doesn't quite match.

I think the main clue is to not get stuck in the mind here. Remember that when looking in the rear mirror you see history, take some of the things that resonate with you right now and act with them. Looking only to go back and live 300 years back in history is just not working, the world does not look like that now. I mean that you should take some of the rituals and make them new. Ok, i think that if you live in a city without any nature nearby one should always think about moving for the reason of the bad environment there. If you connect with the mountains as you say, perhaps a life as a guard in a park cold be something just because it reconnects with your blueprint.

As my header in my thread say: Make your own " The new old fashioned", we sure do have some choices to do and as i feel, time is running not in our advance.

### Hemming — 2013-09-07T21:57:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-95154>

I’ve been wanting to write a longer update after I started working again. I’ve numbered all the items below in the hope that more people comment on specific bullets they find interesting. I would be happy to elaborate on anything. I’ve tried to keep it rather short and to the point as I could write an entire book on this.

The steps I’ve taken:

1\. I’ve been rereading a lot of the blogs and listening to the podcasts again. I’m really starting to connect many dots now and can see how it is all tied together. One example is that I mentioned earlier that I had started eating more fat and felt better, yesterday I heard one of the podcasts where Jack specifically mentioned that people with eating disorders often do better with a diet higher in fat whereas obese people often do better with a diet higher in protein. This is only specific example, there are so many things I’ve realized and understood from rereading the blogs and I continue to go back them to find answers – and there is a surprisingly many once you’ve been through the blogs a couple of times.
2\. Currently I eat three times each day but as the days become shorter I’ll be shifting to two times each day. Jack also mentioned this in a podcast where he said that the reason why they need to eat several times each day is because they’re out of sync with their circadian rhythm.
3\. Lately I’ve also been eating seasonally basically consuming whatever I could find outside on a tree or bush as carb sources and more fish (mackerel heads included, well I eat everything on a fish), offal and seaweed from good sources. This implies that I’ve reduced my carb intake and increased my fat intake. It has been a quite natural feeling as my appetite has been for more butter and tallow whereas I don’t eat as much coconut oil. I’ve been eating more protein than earlier as my fear has subsided somewhat.
4\. I’ve increased my salt intake which I think have had a positive effect. As conductivity increases in water when salt is added I assume its same effect in humans. Sometimes I’ll even crave salt (I think sodium deficiency is one of the reasons why some people eat cheese and salted nuts).
5\. My sleep has always been good and I haven’t needed an alarm clock for a long time. I usually wake within half an hour of sunrise and go to bed within a half to an hour after sunset. I get tired at that time anyway so its quite natural for me.
6\. I’ve started wearing blue light blocking glasses whenever I’m on the computer, I’ve hardwired my radio, I’m working on hardwiring my wifi (which I’ve cut down on using), I ground by walking barefoot in grass, I swim in the ocean two to four times each week, I do bodyweight exercises two to four times each week as my energy dictates (push-ups, planks etc.), I’ve stopped using deodorant and I’ve been getting a lot of sun exposure as the summer has been very long here in Denmark. Generally I’ve been following my own way of living and obviously I’ve taken some heat for that which will only get even worse during the winter when try to explain people that eating a banana Christmas Eve makes no sense and that I try to eat between sunrise and sunset.

The results:

7\. My physical energy is much better than it has been for a long time. On the other hand, I still feel I need some mental energy to really engage with the world and other people. Although I’m now able to concentrate on reading again as I’m not constantly thinking about when and what I’ll be eating next (yes, I’ve been so hungry for several months that that was all I could think of). My muscular strength has improved a lot of the last couple of weeks and I would argue that I’ve added some muscle to my arms and legs. My legs also look a lot better now that the edema/swelling is basically gone and my calves don’t feel stiff all the time as they used to do.
8\. My hunger is much better and I don’t think it will be a problem for me to eat two times each day. I do still have days where I don’t eat as much as I really could because I start feeling guilty. My biggest concern is therefore that I don’t eat enough at those two meals. I plan on eating very strict according to season. Something that is often bugging is exactly how much protein and fat I should eat, which I know is a flawed way of thinking about it, but again, that’s something I’m working on.
9\. I still often feel cold (compared to what most people report here), especially in my extremities. I’ve adapted pretty good to cold water but cold air is a bigger problem. I’m not sure if this is something that can be reversed with eating more and maybe getting more iodine. I’m thinking that it’s a lack of energy to heat my body from not eating enough, somewhat related to what Jack has said about UCP1, T3 and leptin (anecdotally, I’m reading a book about a navy SEAL who also said that when they spent 14-16h each day in water during training they started craving fat big time and they were given butter and peanut butter ad libitum). Sleeping in low temperatures isn’t a problem, I seem to get much warmer during the night – and I only sleep with a lamb wools blanket.
10\. My mind continues to be in a state of confusing often. On the one hand I want to get better but on the other hand I find it so difficult to accept to gain weight. Objectively, I don’t think my body is in an optimal state in terms of muscle mass and body fat but I still can’t get my head around gaining weight. Again referring to the blogs I think this is a symptom of some signaling disorder in the brain.

Future hopes and plans:

More mental energy, more muscle strength and definition (as I’ve had before), more longer bike rides, greater resistance to cold air, be happy with eating and enjoying it, be happy with gaining weight, clear mind, longer swims in the ocean during winter, more happiness in life, learn to play the piano, hiking somewhere cold far away from society, living my own life.

### caroline — 2013-09-10T11:30:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-95428>

Look at the answer I gave to Jedeye regarding BPC.....It is all about context and I forgot that. That is why sometimes Jack doesn't answer question specifically .....it is always, always about context.

This isn't a fat loss forum - regardless of how it looks sometimes!

All of us - whether we are obese or have AN or are binge eaters or whatever.....all have screwed hormone panels and cortisol issues etc. etc. etc.

I guess what I am saying - you have to look for the gold....

### Hemming — 2013-09-10T12:38:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-95431>

Yeah, I agree with all of what you're saying.

The problem is that once you end up in this situation you get confused about everything. I'm not saying that I shouldn't take account of my own life because I'm a firm believer in that. Its more that sometimes we all need some professional guidance on how to proceed.
I just discovered this blog [http://www.jackkruse.com/primal-cpc-2-eating-disorder-etiology/#idc-cover](http://www.jackkruse.com/primal-cpc-2-eating-disorder-etiology/#idc-cover) I have no idea how I didn't see it earlier. I'll go through later today with all the comments.

I'm definitely implementing a lot of changes. Eating the BAB is still a hurdle for me though. But what Jack mentioned in the blog about binging I can fully subscribe to and hopefully working more on the BAB can help that. Another interesting thing he mentioned is that AN people get a kick from being hungry. I don't think many people realise how true that is.

Basically I'm just so hungry, tired, cold and lack mental energy but the step to accepting eating more and gaining weight is insane. But again, as was mentioned in the blog, its about rewiring the brain by changing things and getting out of the comfort zone. No one ever said it was going to be easy. I'm looking forward to the day where Jack will post my success story on Facebook

### Hemming — 2013-09-10T20:51:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-95462>

I just finished reading the aforementioned blog and all of the comments. That has really changed my perspective on things.

I think my cortisol levels play a much bigger role than I've previously thought. I guess my obsessing about doing everything right is stressing me than the benefits of the things I do. That's why I'm considering changing a few things.
1\. stop exercising as much and commute at a lower intensity
2\. keep my apartment a little warmer (maybe I'll even start wearing socks)
3\. I'm considering to stop doing two cold showers each day
4\. eat more so that my brain doesn't think we're in the middle of a famine

### caroline — 2013-09-11T11:41:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-95586>

There is a great podcast - maybe you have already heard it? reconnected human website I think.

### Hemming — 2013-09-11T13:12:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-95591>

Giving myself a break is exactly what I plan on doing - I think that has been my missing link after doing all of these changes.

Yes, I need to be stimulated all the time whether its physical or mentally. I can sit down and relax with a book but I have to spend atleast 5min getting in the zone. I have looked into movnat and I'm still thinking of doing one of those of some hiking, it depends on how I can plan it all.

Regarding the farm - yes and no. I could sell my apartment and buy but I would need a longer commute to work. The idea would be to move there and live from breeding cattle but that is way easier said than done.

Thanks a lot for getting back on the webinars, I'll have a look at it!

I've heard the podcast with reconnected human. I even wrote them and told how great it was.

Please continue asking questions! Its really great to share my journey, I just feel that I should be saying so much more.
I hope more people will chime in with their questions and suggestions!

### Hemming — 2013-09-11T20:38:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-95635>

Given my recent post about some of the changes I'll be making I also want to commit to the leptin rx. Today I had a bout of that hunger/binges where even ice cubes taste good and I don't want to live like that anymore. Tomorrow I'm going for the BAB hoping that will kill my hunger and incessant thoughts about food.

### caroline — 2013-09-12T01:04:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-95651>

I thought that you were doing the BAB and had upped the fat too It must be so hard to get your head around all of this. I wish that I could say something to help ....but, I guess, you have to do the heavy lifting. If I could do it for you - I would...
I can see it is such a vicious circle of being hungry and being afraid to eat - Such a huge brain/gut connection there....

That is why I thought you would need something to wholly and fully occupy your mind - something that you love beyond all reason. Could you work on plans to build yourself a log cabin of some kind and make yourself self sustainable?

Someone posted a picture of a very neat house - did you see it?

I hope you do listen to the Q&A with Erwan ....he has an amazing mind and spirit...He understands very clearly what is important to him ...as does Jack...We all need that focus....

I look forward to hearing about your BAB....

### Hemming — 2013-09-12T10:10:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-95688>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=95651)
>

> QUOTE\]
>
> Nothing lasts forever, which is the problem in this.
>
> Uhm, with all due respect - there are certain limitations to what is possible. I have no chance of building a log cabin anywhere.
>

>
> Two questions that came to mind.
> 1\. It says that one should not do IF on the leptin rx but at the same time that it good if you can eat only two times each day. I eat breakfast around 0700, would it be too long without food if I have dinner somewhere between 1700-1800?
>
> 2\. I bike through a natural reserve each day. Does that count as grounding too? I read in EMF 1 that even walking with the right shoes could classify as grounding. Is it possible to ground on a bike or in Nike Free?

### caroline — 2013-09-12T12:33:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-95699>

This IF thing and 2 meals keeps coming up on the forum and I don't understand why ........no where has Jack said that you should go to 2 meals a day.... some people - it naturally happens - but certainly not for most - or , at the very least, not every day. It is just if you need three meals - definitely have them ....if some days you aren't hungry - don't worry about it.

It is all about your hunger...so eat when hungry! That is the one thing that I love about the leptin reset .........before I would be hungry all the time and it would drive me crazy....but doing the reset and eating as much of a BAB as it took to get full and then having no cravings and not being hungry for 7+ hours was such a revelation. There is a huge difference between cravings and actually being really hungry. I love the feeling of true hunger ...it really is much different .......

I am going to go and reread the leptin RX ......but I think all this IF thing comes from Dave Asprey. Don't forget - that is intended for people that are relatively well I guess and are training.....

I think biking every day in nature sounds wonderful - do you having grounding shoes/sandals? Do you hug a tree occasionally? You know that you shouldn't be exercising while doing the reset - right?

I think I am seeing more and more people coming here and they have been to all the paleo sites and they are combining bits and pieces from here and there and it isn't really working for them. Always remember that Jack is a clinician - so he lives this stuff on a daily basis.......
From what I can gather - from reading between the lines - You need to be very busy, at least mentally, with a project.

Why not set about to design a cabin for yourself that would make you self sustainable ...just for fun...to see if you could.

Plan out a BAB for one week that you would have every single day and then just do it without thinking about it...and then occupy yourself with some project that would mean so much to you...something that would be fulfilling and exciting and make your heart beat faster and put a big smile on your face. It doesn't really have to come to fruition ...life has a way of changing our best laid plans anyway.....

### caroline — 2013-09-12T12:44:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-95700>

It was Huck that posted a picture of a "Cobb House" in the new monster forum ...there is a thread on shipping container homes. Dream big, live large ...you just never know what life has in store for you...

### Hemming — 2013-09-12T13:20:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-95701>

Ok, I guess I was reading it a little too literally.

Yeah, I really have to get into that thinking. The 'problem' for me and a lot of other anorexics is that we on one hand are hungry but on the other want to postpone eating because its such a good feeling. That's also why we are so slow to eat, we want to savor the taste for as long as possible and are somewhat scared of finishing because we know that we'll be hungry afterwards but wont allow ourself to eat more. That's when you start living of coffee and tea to get sensation of being full without consuming any calories.

The bike ride is for commuting. I do occasionally stop and go into bushes and trees. Yesterday I saw a little sitting in the tall grass reading

Yes, I like to have something keeping my full attention which is even harder when you're hungry all the time. That means you end up not being able to concentrate on anything. Maybe designing a cabin could be a fun project - at least for hoping on ever getting one.

That's a good idea regarding the BAB. I've almost done it already since its much easier for me to eat what is on the plate because then I know how much it is and don't need to stress about it.
I guess that project would be finding a girlfriend...

### caroline — 2013-09-13T02:12:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-95745>

You deserve to have a full and happy life and to have a wonderful relationship ...but - we got to get you head in the right place

Didn't you talk before about upping your fat and feeling a lot better and full for quite a long time?

Please tell me more about AN........so - even when you are very hungry ... you won't allow yourself to eat?

so - there is a component of punishing yourself?

### Hemming — 2013-09-25T14:17:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-96060>

Thank you caroline, that's also what I said to myself one day 'why should I not live my life to the fullest and not suffer in silence'.

I've been strict on following the leptin rx with eating enough protein and fat in the morning. Very quickly I realised that I only need to eat breakfast and dinner which has made my life so much simpler.

I like to punish myself in a lot of situations whether its not eating, swimming in cold water or sitting in a cold apartment.

I feel like so many things have happened after I strictly started following the leptin rx. I'll do a longer update in the coming days. Its early days and I wont jump the gun but I have to call bullshit (to use one of Jack's terms) on many things the conventional wisdom dictates.
Dare I instead call starfish in the making...

### Jack Kruse — 2013-09-25T15:51:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-96064>

Are you contagious? Do you have zeal? Do you maximize life? Zeal is also called passion. The word passion means zeal, fire, inner-drive, intensity, vehement desire. Only people with great passion are capable of rising to greatness.
When you meet or associate with a passionate person they tend to rekindle the fire inside you. Passion like courage is transferable. If you don't know someone like that, today is your chance to become that person. The most powerful weapon on earth is the human soul on fire. Love is the FUEL of passion. If you want more passion then seek more love. A solitary person filled with passion is better than 100 people merely interested. Today, tap your passion. If you have no zeal, you have no appeal............

### Hemming — 2013-09-25T16:04:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-96065>

Jack, I read your post on FB right after you had posted it and came to think of my own situation. 'A solitary person filled with passion is better than 100 people merely interested' made me think of the last time I met my psychologist where I laid out how I live. Needless to say, she was flabbergasted and was thinking that I was a raving lunatic but I must say that I've never been more comfortable with living my own way. I've always went my own ways and taken the beating for that but as one of my friends said 'that's exactly why I like you, you never get bogged down by what other people think about. You just do as you like'.

### Hemming — 2013-10-09T11:31:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-97337>

Funny you mention it. I talked about exactly that with one of my friends and she said that that was what she had also liked about me and that I never do things half-heartedly. Even more coincidentally, I was at a business trip the last couple of days and I met one who does the same as I do for another company. I told him my story and what I did, how I life and modern life is killing us slowly. To my surprise, he was totally in to the thinking and sort of contemplated helping me start a farm (he actually thought it could be an interesting business case) and fund other food related projects. Its 'significant' to me that another says something like that because we both work with investing in private equity funds and see a lot of companies being bought and sold.

The reporter is quite keen on making the documentary and sharing my story and I'm pretty sure I'll do it as it sounds like she has the right approach to it.

### Hemming — 2013-10-09T14:12:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-97340>

I went for a swim today with the water being 12C. While I was swimming around, diving a little I realised that I didn't want to get out of the water, it was just so nice being there. I haven't had that feeling before at that temperature. The problem is though that I'm swimming alone and in the harbour/ocean and I have some respect for not pushing it to an extent where it becomes dangerous. Just last week I swam out downstream and didn't realise how long (and harder) it would be going upstream. When I did get back to my starting point and was getting out of the water my arms were not that useful

### Hemming — 2013-10-15T15:51:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-97897>

The adaption to cold continues to amaze me. This is not for bragging about me - I think everyone can experience the same.

Today I was out swimming in 11-12C water and it just felt good. There was even a lady who approached when I got out of the water and said that she was surprised that I stayed in for that long. She got in almost every morning the same place but just in and out. The thing that really surprised me though was that I left my clothes at my bike and ran to the water to jump and it was actually pleasant to run just wearing shorts in the 13-14C air. I just came to think of Wim when he runs a marathon in the snow.

### caroline — 2013-10-17T00:05:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-98050>

### Hemming — 2013-10-19T22:54:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-98355>

I was out running today for the first in eight months. I did 5k (with some warm up/cool down) in 21.35 with the last 1.2k at 4min/km. I was surprised that I was able to run at that pace - especially the finish. It was really great being out running again and maybe this confirms CT6 and EMF4. Its possible to perform better than average without training for it and without being fueled by carbs. Given my heart rate the last 1.2k was definitely not 100% aerobic, it is indeed possible to perform anaerobically too.

Now I'm just feeling that pleasant sleepiness and a slight feeling of DOMS in my legs.

### caroline — 2013-10-19T23:38:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-98359>

I just love how well you are doing......you are a force to be reckoned with .....

### Hemming — 2013-10-25T12:40:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-99001>

After my run Saturday I've noticed things which really scares me. Earlier this week I had dried blood in my stool, it wasn't a huge amount but no question it was there (I have tried this before after running). Additionally I had some bleeding from my nose and now I've noticed that my lower gums have receded. In some sense my hair also feels thinner but I'm not sure if that's just me.
I'm thinking it could be vitamin C deficiency but I'm also considering that something else is going. Luckily I'm having my labs checked within a couple of weeks. Right now I'm just confused about what to think. On the one hand, I feel good on the high fat diet on the other I'm afraid that I have excluded some foods which I need the nutrition from. Besides that, I continue to be constipated having 1 or 2's on the Bristol chart.

I just needed to get it out...

### Hemming — 2013-11-13T17:34:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-100634>

I've not written anything in a long time as I've been doing a lot of other things.

I had some labs done last Friday
Trigs: 0.89 equiv. 78.8
HDL: 1.91 equiv. 73.9
LDL: 3.5 equiv. 135.3
TSH: 2.2 up from 1.32 in May
Zinc: 10 which is the lowest value in the range. Its up from 9 in May
Creatinine: 69
B12: 175
Vitamin D2+D3: 107
HbA1c: 5.8 equiv. 34
Thrombocytes: 160 down from 190 in May
Hemoglobin B: 8.1mmol/L
Hemoglobin A1C: 34mmol/mol
Sodium, potassium and magnesium all normal.

Overall I'm pretty satisfied after a year on a ketogenic diet. What does concern me is the fall in thrombocytes and that zinc hasn't increased more since May. I feel better than in May though and I've started to work out three times each week doing body weight exercises after I've been out swimming in the ocean. I'm surprised at how quickly I've seen the result both in the mirror and how I feel stronger. Yes, you can perform anaerobically with consuming excessive amounts of carbs.
I have gained weight but I look better than I have in a long time and everyone is telling me that too. I still get hungry at times, I think its partly because I should be eating more and because there are still some leptin issues to be fixed. It annoys the hell out of me when I get that feeling of being hungry and regardless of how much or what I eat I continue to be hungry (I still follow a very strict epi paleo diet). On the other hand, it has surprised how much I can eat without having gained more.

### caroline — 2013-11-13T23:27:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-100676>

hemming - have you thought of purchasing Dr. Jackson's bootcamp?

I am so happy that you are seeing improvements! Yah! What do your parents think? do they see physical improvements? mental improvements?

### Hemming — 2013-11-14T10:17:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-100696>

Not really. As I can't do any of the tests he is going to talk about I don't see the point.

They see it too and I've realised how concerned they have been about the situation (dad even instructed my brother and wife on what they were allowed to say while I was around). I have more energy, its a strange feeling of suddenly wanting to do different things and not procrastinate. My brain is also working better - that's hardly surprising when I'm not constantly thinking of my next meal because I'm so utterly hungry.

### nonchalant — 2013-11-14T18:19:35+0200

### sooperb — 2013-11-14T19:23:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-100713>

"I have more energy, its a strange feeling of suddenly wanting to do different things and not procrastinate. " Oh my goodness, I've been moving in that direction too, adding lots of coconut oil and butter in. We're lucky our local supermarket sells unpasteurised butter, albeit from France. I kind of thought something was changing but couldn't work out quite what it was, I've been a top procastinator for the last couple of years, no motivation to do anything much. I'm glad to read you're making progress H, it's encouraging for you I'm sure and it certainly spurs on the rest of us.

### nonchalant — 2013-11-14T19:35:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-100714>

I kind of thought something was changing but couldn't work out quite what it was, I've been a top procastinator for the last couple of years, no motivation to do anything much. I'm glad to read you're making progress H, it's encouraging for you I'm sure and it certainly spurs on the rest of us.

I argue with DH about this. I tell him, based on my experience, that laziness isn't necessarily a character fault. It could also be a brain's attempt to conserve energy. If it is low on energy, every body movement saved is a win.

### Hemming — 2013-11-14T21:05:04+0200

### Hemming — 2013-11-14T22:56:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-100734>

### Hemming — 2013-11-20T20:43:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-101134>

Inspired by the thread for non-geeks I wanted to write a short update.

I didn't feel well over the weekend and the beginning of this week. I knew I hadn't been eating as much as other days and Monday I was out swimming and couldn't stay in the water for that long. Yesterday and today I ate more and went out swimming today and felt so much better. Its not just my cold tolerance which is better, its also mood, cognitive abilities/concentration, energy and generally feeling better overall.
My point is that something as simple as eating enough to fuel your body is part of water, light and magnetism for me.

### sooperb — 2013-11-20T23:54:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-101163>

Isigny Saint Mere is the name of the butter, it is unsalted and unpasteurised. There is another French brand called Beillevaire available but I haven't tried it. It is a curious fact that in England supermarkets can sell both unpasteurised cheese and butter but not milk. Luckily for me we have a local farm that is organic and sells raw milk and the most amazing raw cream.

Nonchalant, that is a most interesting observation about "laziness" not being a deficit but a symptom, that there can be a bigger picture of which it forms a part, a remarkable insight if I may say so. I have certainly observed this in my daughter who was severely depressed and who was often labelled as lazy but who just wasn't physically capable of action, her weariness and inertia were, in my opinion, a part of the depression over which she had no control. Movement was reserved only for absolutely essential tasks.

### nonchalant — 2013-11-21T01:09:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-101170>

Movement was reserved only for absolutely essential tasks.

Thanks, sooperb! I have been realizing that many things are related to our energy quotient. Addiction, disease, habits, etc. It seems that though we have free will to make our own decisions, we have a second entity (unconsciousness, hind brain, whatever) that also is trying to optimize its existence but can only indirectly influence our actions.

### Inger — 2013-11-22T11:46:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-101329>

Hemming good to see you are eating more! I sure believe we need to eat enough. I never restrict food, I am a pretty small woman, 166 cm / around 55 kg and I eat about in between 600-1000 grams of meat/seafood / day + additional fat, and that is not little... I imagine as a man you could do the double amount..

### Hemming — 2013-11-25T16:18:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-101606>

The only problem is that I 'forget' so quickly again. This past weekend and today I'm not feeling on top and some it is because of a mild cold but I'm also considering if I've been eating enough - I definitely don't eat as much as you
I really appreciate that you say because it makes me easier to understand how much I actually need to eat. I feel like a gluttonous pig at times because I can eat so much but it might be that I'm just eating what I need to function properly. I'm constantly very thirsty, pee all the time and my stool is 1 on the Bristol chart. I'm not sure if my stool indicates that I'm losing fluid as my skin is also extremely dry and I get spontaneous cuts on my fingers and lips. My scalp is also extremely dry. I have a strong craving for salt too currently.

I know it sounds so stupid that I need someone to tell me its okay to eat and that I should happily eat. But at the moment its just so difficult for me. I really like food and often deprive myself of it going to bed hungry thinking that weight is somehow equal to being healthy.

### Inger — 2013-11-25T17:58:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-101609>

But Hemming... too skinny is not healthy... and I can tell you as a woman I prefer men that have a smooth layer of fat on the top of their muscles. No fat or overweight, but there must be some fat on the body. Underweight is not telling a women this man is healthy and strong and can care for her + having lots of hormones is a big bonus...

Oh man Hemming, it sounds like you are dehydrated! You need to eat more, more fat, more protein too and drink more water! I drink 0,5 liter of Celtic Salt water (0,5-1 teaspoon) every morning, and some more if I feel like it during the day. Maybe seaweed water will be great for you, I think so. It is salty too, and full of Iodine. I drink it almost every day.

You need to never go hungry anymore... it is your body crying for food. Please be kind to it and love it! You are not going to regret

How are you feeling about eating the meat and fish and fat raw? I would imagine it would be way better for you.. IDK for sure but it would not hurt to try. At least it helps how I feel in my mood, it is uplifting... gives great energy and does not makes one "stuffed" like cooked foods may do, I mean from raw I never get that "full" feeling I do when I eat cooked. No idea why. I figure it might help you, if you do not like the stuffed feeling when you eat a bit more than used too.

### Jonathin — 2013-11-25T20:52:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-101620>

Hemming - I am an older skinny guy. Being dehydrated is one of my biggest issues. Hard poop quality is a good indicator. I have maintained three quarts of water and plenty of magnesium malate along with a keto epipaleo diet. Light salting at a meal is not sufficient for me to maintain hydration. Sometimes I have to push it with extra salt water - 1-2 qts with 1/2 tsp salt/qt - to feel proper in the gut and feel balanced in many ways. Eating while dehydrated ultimately stresses the body more. It takes energy for the kidneys to conserve sodium. If you are lacking energy and abundant water then the circle of dysfunction continues. Like others say, when you get the details right the body seems to take off. This still continues to amaze me. Perhaps some day in the future when my body has abundant energy all the time, playing with salt will not be an issue.

### Hemming — 2013-11-25T21:13:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-101624>

> [Jonathin said:](/goto/post?id=101620)

> Hemming - I am an older skinny guy. Being dehydrated is one of my biggest issues. Hard poop quality is a good indicator. I have maintained three quarts of water and plenty of magnesium malate along with a keto epipaleo diet. Light salting at a meal is not sufficient for me to maintain hydration. Sometimes I have to push it with extra salt water - 1-2 qts with 1/2 tsp salt/qt - to feel proper in the gut and feel balanced in many ways. Eating while dehydrated ultimately stresses the body more. It takes energy for the kidneys to conserve sodium. If you are lacking energy and abundant water then the circle of dysfunction continues. Like others say, when you get the details right the body seems to take off. This still continues to amaze me. Perhaps some day in the future when my body has abundant energy all the time, playing with salt will not be an issue.

Ho Jonathan, thanks a lot for sharing your experiences! I probably drink the same amount as you. I'm surprised to hear that the effect of salt is so big on you. I'll definitely try to be more aware of drinking enough and getting enough salt. Do you spread out your salt intake? I only have salt with meals and as I only two times each day I get it all in two portions.

### Jonathin — 2013-11-25T21:44:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-101627>

It seems that salt is lost over time but is not that quick. Twice a day dosing seems ok. During a typical meal, I don't consume that much liquid unless it is some form of soup. That is why taking salt water is good because the water is right there with the salt. I have seen my weight bump up 4 lbs from having hydration problems to finally getting enough salt and water.
Consider that 1/3 of the body's water is outside the cell and 2/3 inside. Outside it needs to be about 0.9% salt (sodium chloride) which is about 1 tsp/pt (500 ml). How are you going to boost that extracellular water without salt? If you take in just water it can hydrate the cells but only to the limit of the 2:1 ratio with the extracellular water. Blood sodium should be about 140 mmol/L. I know somebody with 129 blood sodium that suffers extreme dehydration, constipation and the whole plethora of energy issues that Jack talks about.
Just like getting over my issues about substantial fat consumption, I find good quality salt to be an important mainstay rather than something to be avoided.

Inger, thank you so much for the kind words! Objectively, I know that this is not healthy, speaking of men and hormones, my libido is very low and has been more or less non-existent for a year or so now. I really hate it when some people say that I look so fit when I feel like a train wreck.

Yes, I do think it is dehydration. I already drink the seaweed water whenever I have seaweed - it does have a nice salty taste.

I wouldn't mind eating it raw, I've always liked meat cooked rare. Inspired by you I had calf heart which I only cooked to thaw it, it wasn't quite raw but not that far from it and it was really good  I really like all the different kinds of fat, its just about convincing myself that I need it!

### caroline — 2013-11-25T23:03:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-101634>

Hemming - I find the same as Inger .....raw is much easier to digest ....I never feel over full or stuffed like I do when I eat cooked meat. I have always hated that overfull feeling ..... I never, ever get that with raw.

I may have told you .... I just leave very well marbled GF steak on a wooden board in the fridge for a few days. To eat it - just slice thinly and add lots of celtic salt.

### caroline — 2013-11-25T23:04:56+0200
